2251		resultRelation = resultRelInfo->ri_RangeTableIndex;
2252		rte = exec_rt_fetch(resultRelation, estate);
2253	}
2254
2255	/* Construct the SQL command string. */ view japanese
2256	deparseInsertSql(&sql, rte, resultRelation, rel, targetAttrs, doNothing,
2257					 resultRelInfo->ri_WithCheckOptions,
2258					 resultRelInfo->ri_returningList,
2259					 &retrieved_attrs, &values_end_len);
2260
2261	/* Construct an execution state. */ view japanese
2262	fmstate = create_foreign_modify(mtstate->ps.state,
2263									rte,
2264									resultRelInfo,
2265									CMD_INSERT,
2266									NULL,
2267									sql.data,
2268									targetAttrs,
2269									values_end_len,
2270									retrieved_attrs != NIL,
2271									retrieved_attrs);
2272
2273	/*
2274	 * If the given resultRelInfo already has PgFdwModifyState set, it means
2275	 * the foreign table is an UPDATE subplan result rel; in which case, store
2276	 * the resulting state into the aux_fmstate of the PgFdwModifyState.
2277	 */ view japanese
2278	if (resultRelInfo->ri_FdwState)
2279	{
2280		Assert(plan && plan->operation == CMD_UPDATE);
2281		Assert(resultRelInfo->ri_usesFdwDirectModify == false);
2282		((PgFdwModifyState *) resultRelInfo->ri_FdwState)->aux_fmstate = fmstate;
2283	}
2284	else
2285		resultRelInfo->ri_FdwState = fmstate;
2286}
2287
2288/*
2289 * postgresEndForeignInsert
2290 *		Finish an insert operation on a foreign table
2291 */ view japanese
2292static void
2293postgresEndForeignInsert(EState *estate,
2294						 ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo)
2295{
2296	PgFdwModifyState *fmstate = (PgFdwModifyState *) resultRelInfo->ri_FdwState;
2297
2298	Assert(fmstate != NULL);
2299
2300	/*
2301	 * If the fmstate has aux_fmstate set, get the aux_fmstate (see
2302	 * postgresBeginForeignInsert())
2303	 */ view japanese
2304	if (fmstate->aux_fmstate)
2305		fmstate = fmstate->aux_fmstate;
2306
2307	/* Destroy the execution state */ view japanese
2308	finish_foreign_modify(fmstate);
2309}
2310
2311/*
2312 * postgresIsForeignRelUpdatable
2313 *		Determine whether a foreign table supports INSERT, UPDATE and/or
2314 *		DELETE.
2315 */ view japanese
2316static int
2317postgresIsForeignRelUpdatable(Relation rel)
2318{
2319	bool		updatable;
2320	ForeignTable *table;
2321	ForeignServer *server;
2322	ListCell   *lc;
2323
2324	/*
2325	 * By default, all postgres_fdw foreign tables are assumed updatable. This
2326	 * can be overridden by a per-server setting, which in turn can be
2327	 * overridden by a per-table setting.
2328	 */ view japanese
2329	updatable = true;
2330
2331	table = GetForeignTable(RelationGetRelid(rel));
2332	server = GetForeignServer(table->serverid);
2333
2334	foreach(lc, server->options)
2335	{
2336		DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
2337
2338		if (strcmp(def->defname, "updatable") == 0)
2339			updatable = defGetBoolean(def);
2340	}
2341	foreach(lc, table->options)
2342	{
2343		DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
2344
2345		if (strcmp(def->defname, "updatable") == 0)
2346			updatable = defGetBoolean(def);
2347	}
2348
2349	/*
2350	 * Currently "updatable" means support for INSERT, UPDATE and DELETE.
2351	 */ view japanese
2352	return updatable ?
2353		(1 << CMD_INSERT) | (1 << CMD_UPDATE) | (1 << CMD_DELETE) : 0;
2354}
2355
2356/*
2357 * postgresRecheckForeignScan
2358 *		Execute a local join execution plan for a foreign join
2359 */ view japanese
2360static bool
2361postgresRecheckForeignScan(ForeignScanState *node, TupleTableSlot *slot)
2362{
2363	Index		scanrelid = ((Scan *) node->ss.ps.plan)->scanrelid;
2364	PlanState  *outerPlan = outerPlanState(node);
2365	TupleTableSlot *result;
2366
2367	/* For base foreign relations, it suffices to set fdw_recheck_quals */ view japanese
2368	if (scanrelid > 0)
2369		return true;
2370
2371	Assert(outerPlan != NULL);
2372
2373	/* Execute a local join execution plan */ view japanese
2374	result = ExecProcNode(outerPlan);
2375	if (TupIsNull(result))
2376		return false;
2377
2378	/* Store result in the given slot */ view japanese
2379	ExecCopySlot(slot, result);
2380
2381	return true;
2382}
2383
2384/*
2385 * find_modifytable_subplan
2386 *		Helper routine for postgresPlanDirectModify to find the
2387 *		ModifyTable subplan node that scans the specified RTI.
2388 *
2389 * Returns NULL if the subplan couldn't be identified.  That's not a fatal
2390 * error condition, we just abandon trying to do the update directly.
2391 */ view japanese
2392static ForeignScan *
2393find_modifytable_subplan(PlannerInfo *root,
2394						 ModifyTable *plan,
2395						 Index rtindex,
2396						 int subplan_index)
2397{
2398	Plan	   *subplan = outerPlan(plan);
2399
2400	/*
2401	 * The cases we support are (1) the desired ForeignScan is the immediate
2402	 * child of ModifyTable, or (2) it is the subplan_index'th child of an
2403	 * Append node that is the immediate child of ModifyTable.  There is no
2404	 * point in looking further down, as that would mean that local joins are
2405	 * involved, so we can't do the update directly.
2406	 *
2407	 * There could be a Result atop the Append too, acting to compute the
2408	 * UPDATE targetlist values.  We ignore that here; the tlist will be
2409	 * checked by our caller.
2410	 *
2411	 * In principle we could examine all the children of the Append, but it's
2412	 * currently unlikely that the core planner would generate such a plan
2413	 * with the children out-of-order.  Moreover, such a search risks costing
2414	 * O(N^2) time when there are a lot of children.
2415	 */ view japanese
2416	if (IsA(subplan, Append))
2417	{
2418		Append	   *appendplan = (Append *) subplan;
2419
2420		if (subplan_index < list_length(appendplan->appendplans))
2421			subplan = (Plan *) list_nth(appendplan->appendplans, subplan_index);
2422	}
2423	else if (IsA(subplan, Result) &&
2424			 outerPlan(subplan) != NULL &&
2425			 IsA(outerPlan(subplan), Append))
2426	{
2427		Append	   *appendplan = (Append *) outerPlan(subplan);
2428
2429		if (subplan_index < list_length(appendplan->appendplans))
2430			subplan = (Plan *) list_nth(appendplan->appendplans, subplan_index);
2431	}
2432
2433	/* Now, have we got a ForeignScan on the desired rel? */ view japanese
2434	if (IsA(subplan, ForeignScan))
2435	{
2436		ForeignScan *fscan = (ForeignScan *) subplan;
2437
2438		if (bms_is_member(rtindex, fscan->fs_base_relids))
2439			return fscan;
2440	}
2441
2442	return NULL;
2443}
2444
2445/*
2446 * postgresPlanDirectModify
2447 *		Consider a direct foreign table modification
2448 *
2449 * Decide whether it is safe to modify a foreign table directly, and if so,
2450 * rewrite subplan accordingly.
2451 */ view japanese
2452static bool
2453postgresPlanDirectModify(PlannerInfo *root,
2454						 ModifyTable *plan,
2455						 Index resultRelation,
2456						 int subplan_index)
2457{
2458	CmdType		operation = plan->operation;
2459	RelOptInfo *foreignrel;
2460	RangeTblEntry *rte;
2461	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo;
2462	Relation	rel;
2463	StringInfoData sql;
2464	ForeignScan *fscan;
2465	List	   *processed_tlist = NIL;
2466	List	   *targetAttrs = NIL;
2467	List	   *remote_exprs;
2468	List	   *params_list = NIL;
2469	List	   *returningList = NIL;
2470	List	   *retrieved_attrs = NIL;
2471
2472	/*
2473	 * Decide whether it is safe to modify a foreign table directly.
2474	 */ view japanese
2475
2476	/*
2477	 * The table modification must be an UPDATE or DELETE.
2478	 */ view japanese
2479	if (operation != CMD_UPDATE && operation != CMD_DELETE)
2480		return false;
2481
2482	/*
2483	 * Try to locate the ForeignScan subplan that's scanning resultRelation.
2484	 */ view japanese
2485	fscan = find_modifytable_subplan(root, plan, resultRelation, subplan_index);
2486	if (!fscan)
2487		return false;
2488
2489	/*
2490	 * It's unsafe to modify a foreign table directly if there are any quals
2491	 * that should be evaluated locally.
2492	 */ view japanese
2493	if (fscan->scan.plan.qual != NIL)
2494		return false;
2495
2496	/* Safe to fetch data about the target foreign rel */ view japanese
2497	if (fscan->scan.scanrelid == 0)
2498	{
2499		foreignrel = find_join_rel(root, fscan->fs_relids);
2500		/* We should have a rel for this foreign join. */ view japanese
2501		Assert(foreignrel);
2502	}
2503	else
2504		foreignrel = root->simple_rel_array[resultRelation];
2505	rte = root->simple_rte_array[resultRelation];
2506	fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) foreignrel->fdw_private;
2507
2508	/*
2509	 * It's unsafe to update a foreign table directly, if any expressions to
2510	 * assign to the target columns are unsafe to evaluate remotely.
2511	 */ view japanese
2512	if (operation == CMD_UPDATE)
2513	{
2514		ListCell   *lc,
2515				   *lc2;
2516
2517		/*
2518		 * The expressions of concern are the first N columns of the processed
2519		 * targetlist, where N is the length of the rel's update_colnos.
2520		 */ view japanese
2521		get_translated_update_targetlist(root, resultRelation,
2522										 &processed_tlist, &targetAttrs);
2523		forboth(lc, processed_tlist, lc2, targetAttrs)
2524		{
2525			TargetEntry *tle = lfirst_node(TargetEntry, lc);
2526			AttrNumber	attno = lfirst_int(lc2);
2527
2528			/* update's new-value expressions shouldn't be resjunk */ view japanese
2529			Assert(!tle->resjunk);
2530
2531			if (attno <= InvalidAttrNumber) /* shouldn't happen */ view japanese
2532				elog(ERROR, "system-column update is not supported");
2533
2534			if (!is_foreign_expr(root, foreignrel, (Expr *) tle->expr))
2535				return false;
2536		}
2537	}
2538
2539	/*
2540	 * Ok, rewrite subplan so as to modify the foreign table directly.
2541	 */ view japanese
2542	initStringInfo(&sql);
2543
2544	/*
2545	 * Core code already has some lock on each rel being planned, so we can
2546	 * use NoLock here.
2547	 */ view japanese
2548	rel = table_open(rte->relid, NoLock);
2549
2550	/*
2551	 * Recall the qual clauses that must be evaluated remotely.  (These are
2552	 * bare clauses not RestrictInfos, but deparse.c's appendConditions()
2553	 * doesn't care.)
2554	 */ view japanese
2555	remote_exprs = fpinfo->final_remote_exprs;
2556
2557	/*
2558	 * Extract the relevant RETURNING list if any.
2559	 */ view japanese
2560	if (plan->returningLists)
2561	{
2562		returningList = (List *) list_nth(plan->returningLists, subplan_index);
2563
2564		/*
2565		 * When performing an UPDATE/DELETE .. RETURNING on a join directly,
2566		 * we fetch from the foreign server any Vars specified in RETURNING
2567		 * that refer not only to the target relation but to non-target
2568		 * relations.  So we'll deparse them into the RETURNING clause of the
2569		 * remote query; use a targetlist consisting of them instead, which
2570		 * will be adjusted to be new fdw_scan_tlist of the foreign-scan plan
2571		 * node below.
2572		 */ view japanese
2573		if (fscan->scan.scanrelid == 0)
2574			returningList = build_remote_returning(resultRelation, rel,
2575												   returningList);
2576	}
2577
2578	/*
2579	 * Construct the SQL command string.
2580	 */ view japanese
2581	switch (operation)
2582	{
2583		case CMD_UPDATE:
2584			deparseDirectUpdateSql(&sql, root, resultRelation, rel,
2585								   foreignrel,
2586								   processed_tlist,
2587								   targetAttrs,
2588								   remote_exprs, &params_list,
2589								   returningList, &retrieved_attrs);
2590			break;
2591		case CMD_DELETE:
2592			deparseDirectDeleteSql(&sql, root, resultRelation, rel,
2593								   foreignrel,
2594								   remote_exprs, &params_list,
2595								   returningList, &retrieved_attrs);
2596			break;
2597		default:
2598			elog(ERROR, "unexpected operation: %d", (int) operation);
2599			break;
2600	}
2601
2602	/*
2603	 * Update the operation and target relation info.
2604	 */ view japanese
2605	fscan->operation = operation;
2606	fscan->resultRelation = resultRelation;
2607
2608	/*
2609	 * Update the fdw_exprs list that will be available to the executor.
2610	 */ view japanese
2611	fscan->fdw_exprs = params_list;
2612
2613	/*
2614	 * Update the fdw_private list that will be available to the executor.
2615	 * Items in the list must match enum FdwDirectModifyPrivateIndex, above.
2616	 */ view japanese
2617	fscan->fdw_private = list_make4(makeString(sql.data),
2618									makeBoolean((retrieved_attrs != NIL)),
2619									retrieved_attrs,
2620									makeBoolean(plan->canSetTag));
2621
2622	/*
2623	 * Update the foreign-join-related fields.
2624	 */ view japanese
2625	if (fscan->scan.scanrelid == 0)
2626	{
2627		/* No need for the outer subplan. */ view japanese
2628		fscan->scan.plan.lefttree = NULL;
2629
2630		/* Build new fdw_scan_tlist if UPDATE/DELETE .. RETURNING. */ view japanese
2631		if (returningList)
2632			rebuild_fdw_scan_tlist(fscan, returningList);
2633	}
2634
2635	/*
2636	 * Finally, unset the async-capable flag if it is set, as we currently
2637	 * don't support asynchronous execution of direct modifications.
2638	 */ view japanese
2639	if (fscan->scan.plan.async_capable)
2640		fscan->scan.plan.async_capable = false;
2641
2642	table_close(rel, NoLock);
2643	return true;
2644}
2645
2646/*
2647 * postgresBeginDirectModify
2648 *		Prepare a direct foreign table modification
2649 */ view japanese
2650static void
2651postgresBeginDirectModify(ForeignScanState *node, int eflags)
2652{
2653	ForeignScan *fsplan = (ForeignScan *) node->ss.ps.plan;
2654	EState	   *estate = node->ss.ps.state;
2655	PgFdwDirectModifyState *dmstate;
2656	Index		rtindex;
2657	Oid			userid;
2658	ForeignTable *table;
2659	UserMapping *user;
2660	int			numParams;
2661
2662	/*
2663	 * Do nothing in EXPLAIN (no ANALYZE) case.  node->fdw_state stays NULL.
2664	 */ view japanese
2665	if (eflags & EXEC_FLAG_EXPLAIN_ONLY)
2666		return;
2667
2668	/*
2669	 * We'll save private state in node->fdw_state.
2670	 */ view japanese
2671	dmstate = (PgFdwDirectModifyState *) palloc0(sizeof(PgFdwDirectModifyState));
2672	node->fdw_state = dmstate;
2673
2674	/*
2675	 * We use a memory context callback to ensure that the dmstate's PGresult
2676	 * (if any) will be released, even if the query fails somewhere that's
2677	 * outside our control.  The callback is always armed for the duration of
2678	 * the query; this relies on PQclear(NULL) being a no-op.
2679	 */ view japanese
2680	dmstate->result_cb.func = (MemoryContextCallbackFunction) PQclear;
2681	dmstate->result_cb.arg = NULL;
2682	MemoryContextRegisterResetCallback(CurrentMemoryContext,
2683									   &dmstate->result_cb);
2684
2685	/*
2686	 * Identify which user to do the remote access as.  This should match what
2687	 * ExecCheckPermissions() does.
2688	 */ view japanese
2689	userid = OidIsValid(fsplan->checkAsUser) ? fsplan->checkAsUser : GetUserId();
2690
2691	/* Get info about foreign table. */
2692	rtindex = node->resultRelInfo->ri_RangeTableIndex;
2693	if (fsplan->scan.scanrelid == 0)
2694		dmstate->rel = ExecOpenScanRelation(estate, rtindex, eflags);
2695	else
2696		dmstate->rel = node->ss.ss_currentRelation;
2697	table = GetForeignTable(RelationGetRelid(dmstate->rel));
2698	user = GetUserMapping(userid, table->serverid);
2699
2700	/*
2701	 * Get connection to the foreign server.  Connection manager will
2702	 * establish new connection if necessary.
2703	 */
2704	dmstate->conn = GetConnection(user, false, &dmstate->conn_state);
2705
2706	/* Update the foreign-join-related fields. */ view japanese
2707	if (fsplan->scan.scanrelid == 0)
2708	{
2709		/* Save info about foreign table. */ view japanese
2710		dmstate->resultRel = dmstate->rel;
2711
2712		/*
2713		 * Set dmstate->rel to NULL to teach get_returning_data() and
2714		 * make_tuple_from_result_row() that columns fetched from the remote
2715		 * server are described by fdw_scan_tlist of the foreign-scan plan
2716		 * node, not the tuple descriptor for the target relation.
2717		 */ view japanese
2718		dmstate->rel = NULL;
2719	}
2720
2721	/* Initialize state variable */ view japanese
2722	dmstate->num_tuples = -1;	/* -1 means not set yet */
2723
2724	/* Get private info created by planner functions. */ view japanese
2725	dmstate->query = strVal(list_nth(fsplan->fdw_private,
2726									 FdwDirectModifyPrivateUpdateSql));
2727	dmstate->has_returning = boolVal(list_nth(fsplan->fdw_private,
2728											  FdwDirectModifyPrivateHasReturning));
2729	dmstate->retrieved_attrs = (List *) list_nth(fsplan->fdw_private,
2730												 FdwDirectModifyPrivateRetrievedAttrs);
2731	dmstate->set_processed = boolVal(list_nth(fsplan->fdw_private,
2732											  FdwDirectModifyPrivateSetProcessed));
2733
2734	/* Create context for per-tuple temp workspace. */
2735	dmstate->temp_cxt = AllocSetContextCreate(estate->es_query_cxt,
2736											  "postgres_fdw temporary data",
2737											  ALLOCSET_SMALL_SIZES);
2738
2739	/* Prepare for input conversion of RETURNING results. */
2740	if (dmstate->has_returning)
2741	{
2742		TupleDesc	tupdesc;
2743
2744		if (fsplan->scan.scanrelid == 0)
2745			tupdesc = get_tupdesc_for_join_scan_tuples(node);
2746		else
2747			tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(dmstate->rel);
2748
2749		dmstate->attinmeta = TupleDescGetAttInMetadata(tupdesc);
2750
2751		/*
2752		 * When performing an UPDATE/DELETE .. RETURNING on a join directly,
2753		 * initialize a filter to extract an updated/deleted tuple from a scan
2754		 * tuple.
2755		 */ view japanese
2756		if (fsplan->scan.scanrelid == 0)
2757			init_returning_filter(dmstate, fsplan->fdw_scan_tlist, rtindex);
2758	}
2759
2760	/*
2761	 * Prepare for processing of parameters used in remote query, if any.
2762	 */ view japanese
2763	numParams = list_length(fsplan->fdw_exprs);
2764	dmstate->numParams = numParams;
2765	if (numParams > 0)
2766		prepare_query_params((PlanState *) node,
2767							 fsplan->fdw_exprs,
2768							 numParams,
2769							 &dmstate->param_flinfo,
2770							 &dmstate->param_exprs,
2771							 &dmstate->param_values);
2772}
2773
2774/*
2775 * postgresIterateDirectModify
2776 *		Execute a direct foreign table modification
2777 */ view japanese
2778static TupleTableSlot *
2779postgresIterateDirectModify(ForeignScanState *node)
2780{
2781	PgFdwDirectModifyState *dmstate = (PgFdwDirectModifyState *) node->fdw_state;
2782	EState	   *estate = node->ss.ps.state;
2783	ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo = node->resultRelInfo;
2784
2785	/*
2786	 * If this is the first call after Begin, execute the statement.
2787	 */ view japanese
2788	if (dmstate->num_tuples == -1)
2789		execute_dml_stmt(node);
2790
2791	/*
2792	 * If the local query doesn't specify RETURNING, just clear tuple slot.
2793	 */ view japanese
2794	if (!resultRelInfo->ri_projectReturning)
2795	{
2796		TupleTableSlot *slot = node->ss.ss_ScanTupleSlot;
2797		Instrumentation *instr = node->ss.ps.instrument;
2798
2799		Assert(!dmstate->has_returning);
2800
2801		/* Increment the command es_processed count if necessary. */
2802		if (dmstate->set_processed)
2803			estate->es_processed += dmstate->num_tuples;
2804
2805		/* Increment the tuple count for EXPLAIN ANALYZE if necessary. */ view japanese
2806		if (instr)
2807			instr->tuplecount += dmstate->num_tuples;
2808
2809		return ExecClearTuple(slot);
2810	}
2811
2812	/*
2813	 * Get the next RETURNING tuple.
2814	 */ view japanese
2815	return get_returning_data(node);
2816}
2817
2818/*
2819 * postgresEndDirectModify
2820 *		Finish a direct foreign table modification
2821 */ view japanese
2822static void
2823postgresEndDirectModify(ForeignScanState *node)
2824{
2825	PgFdwDirectModifyState *dmstate = (PgFdwDirectModifyState *) node->fdw_state;
2826
2827	/* if dmstate is NULL, we are in EXPLAIN; nothing to do */ view japanese
2828	if (dmstate == NULL)
2829		return;
2830
2831	/* Release PGresult */ view japanese
2832	if (dmstate->result)
2833	{
2834		PQclear(dmstate->result);
2835		dmstate->result = NULL;
2836		/* ... and don't forget to disable the callback */ view japanese
2837		dmstate->result_cb.arg = NULL;
2838	}
2839
2840	/* Release remote connection */
2841	ReleaseConnection(dmstate->conn);
2842	dmstate->conn = NULL;
2843
2844	/* MemoryContext will be deleted automatically. */ view japanese
2845}
2846
2847/*
2848 * postgresExplainForeignScan
2849 *		Produce extra output for EXPLAIN of a ForeignScan on a foreign table
2850 */ view japanese
2851static void
2852postgresExplainForeignScan(ForeignScanState *node, ExplainState *es)
2853{
2854	ForeignScan *plan = castNode(ForeignScan, node->ss.ps.plan);
2855	List	   *fdw_private = plan->fdw_private;
2856
2857	/*
2858	 * Identify foreign scans that are really joins or upper relations.  The
2859	 * input looks something like "(1) LEFT JOIN (2)", and we must replace the
2860	 * digit string(s), which are RT indexes, with the correct relation names.
2861	 * We do that here, not when the plan is created, because we can't know
2862	 * what aliases ruleutils.c will assign at plan creation time.
2863	 */ view japanese
2864	if (list_length(fdw_private) > FdwScanPrivateRelations)
2865	{
2866		StringInfo	relations;
2867		char	   *rawrelations;
2868		char	   *ptr;
2869		int			minrti,
2870					rtoffset;
2871
2872		rawrelations = strVal(list_nth(fdw_private, FdwScanPrivateRelations));
2873
2874		/*
2875		 * A difficulty with using a string representation of RT indexes is
2876		 * that setrefs.c won't update the string when flattening the
2877		 * rangetable.  To find out what rtoffset was applied, identify the
2878		 * minimum RT index appearing in the string and compare it to the
2879		 * minimum member of plan->fs_base_relids.  (We expect all the relids
2880		 * in the join will have been offset by the same amount; the Asserts
2881		 * below should catch it if that ever changes.)
2882		 */ view japanese
2883		minrti = INT_MAX;
2884		ptr = rawrelations;
2885		while (*ptr)
2886		{
2887			if (isdigit((unsigned char) *ptr))
2888			{
2889				int			rti = strtol(ptr, &ptr, 10);
2890
2891				if (rti < minrti)
2892					minrti = rti;
2893			}
2894			else
2895				ptr++;
2896		}
2897		rtoffset = bms_next_member(plan->fs_base_relids, -1) - minrti;
2898
2899		/* Now we can translate the string */ view japanese
2900		relations = makeStringInfo();
2901		ptr = rawrelations;
2902		while (*ptr)
2903		{
2904			if (isdigit((unsigned char) *ptr))
2905			{
2906				int			rti = strtol(ptr, &ptr, 10);
2907				RangeTblEntry *rte;
2908				char	   *relname;
2909				char	   *refname;
2910
2911				rti += rtoffset;
2912				Assert(bms_is_member(rti, plan->fs_base_relids));
2913				rte = rt_fetch(rti, es->rtable);
2914				Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION);
2915				/* This logic should agree with explain.c's ExplainTargetRel */ view japanese
2916				relname = get_rel_name(rte->relid);
2917				if (es->verbose)
2918				{
2919					char	   *namespace;
2920
2921					namespace = get_namespace_name_or_temp(get_rel_namespace(rte->relid));
2922					appendStringInfo(relations, "%s.%s",
2923									 quote_identifier(namespace),
2924									 quote_identifier(relname));
2925				}
2926				else
2927					appendStringInfoString(relations,
2928										   quote_identifier(relname));
2929				refname = (char *) list_nth(es->rtable_names, rti - 1);
2930				if (refname == NULL)
2931					refname = rte->eref->aliasname;
2932				if (strcmp(refname, relname) != 0)
2933					appendStringInfo(relations, " %s",
2934									 quote_identifier(refname));
2935			}
2936			else
2937				appendStringInfoChar(relations, *ptr++);
2938		}
2939		ExplainPropertyText("Relations", relations->data, es);
2940	}
2941
2942	/*
2943	 * Add remote query, when VERBOSE option is specified.
2944	 */ view japanese
2945	if (es->verbose)
2946	{
2947		char	   *sql;
2948
2949		sql = strVal(list_nth(fdw_private, FdwScanPrivateSelectSql));
2950		ExplainPropertyText("Remote SQL", sql, es);
2951	}
2952}
2953
2954/*
2955 * postgresExplainForeignModify
2956 *		Produce extra output for EXPLAIN of a ModifyTable on a foreign table
2957 */ view japanese
2958static void
2959postgresExplainForeignModify(ModifyTableState *mtstate,
2960							 ResultRelInfo *rinfo,
2961							 List *fdw_private,
2962							 int subplan_index,
2963							 ExplainState *es)
2964{
2965	if (es->verbose)
2966	{
2967		char	   *sql = strVal(list_nth(fdw_private,
2968										  FdwModifyPrivateUpdateSql));
2969
2970		ExplainPropertyText("Remote SQL", sql, es);
2971
2972		/*
2973		 * For INSERT we should always have batch size >= 1, but UPDATE and
2974		 * DELETE don't support batching so don't show the property.
2975		 */ view japanese
2976		if (rinfo->ri_BatchSize > 0)
2977			ExplainPropertyInteger("Batch Size", NULL, rinfo->ri_BatchSize, es);
2978	}
2979}
2980
2981/*
2982 * postgresExplainDirectModify
2983 *		Produce extra output for EXPLAIN of a ForeignScan that modifies a
2984 *		foreign table directly
2985 */ view japanese
2986static void
2987postgresExplainDirectModify(ForeignScanState *node, ExplainState *es)
2988{
2989	List	   *fdw_private;
2990	char	   *sql;
2991
2992	if (es->verbose)
2993	{
2994		fdw_private = ((ForeignScan *) node->ss.ps.plan)->fdw_private;
2995		sql = strVal(list_nth(fdw_private, FdwDirectModifyPrivateUpdateSql));
2996		ExplainPropertyText("Remote SQL", sql, es);
2997	}
2998}
2999
3000/*
3001 * postgresExecForeignTruncate
3002 *		Truncate one or more foreign tables
3003 */ view japanese
3004static void
3005postgresExecForeignTruncate(List *rels,
3006							DropBehavior behavior,
3007							bool restart_seqs)
3008{
3009	Oid			serverid = InvalidOid;
3010	UserMapping *user = NULL;
3011	PGconn	   *conn = NULL;
3012	StringInfoData sql;
3013	ListCell   *lc;
3014	bool		server_truncatable = true;
3015
3016	/*
3017	 * By default, all postgres_fdw foreign tables are assumed truncatable.
3018	 * This can be overridden by a per-server setting, which in turn can be
3019	 * overridden by a per-table setting.
3020	 */ view japanese
3021	foreach(lc, rels)
3022	{
3023		ForeignServer *server = NULL;
3024		Relation	rel = lfirst(lc);
3025		ForeignTable *table = GetForeignTable(RelationGetRelid(rel));
3026		ListCell   *cell;
3027		bool		truncatable;
3028
3029		/*
3030		 * First time through, determine whether the foreign server allows
3031		 * truncates. Since all specified foreign tables are assumed to belong
3032		 * to the same foreign server, this result can be used for other
3033		 * foreign tables.
3034		 */ view japanese
3035		if (!OidIsValid(serverid))
3036		{
3037			serverid = table->serverid;
3038			server = GetForeignServer(serverid);
3039
3040			foreach(cell, server->options)
3041			{
3042				DefElem    *defel = (DefElem *) lfirst(cell);
3043
3044				if (strcmp(defel->defname, "truncatable") == 0)
3045				{
3046					server_truncatable = defGetBoolean(defel);
3047					break;
3048				}
3049			}
3050		}
3051
3052		/*
3053		 * Confirm that all specified foreign tables belong to the same
3054		 * foreign server.
3055		 */ view japanese
3056		Assert(table->serverid == serverid);
3057
3058		/* Determine whether this foreign table allows truncations */ view japanese
3059		truncatable = server_truncatable;
3060		foreach(cell, table->options)
3061		{
3062			DefElem    *defel = (DefElem *) lfirst(cell);
3063
3064			if (strcmp(defel->defname, "truncatable") == 0)
3065			{
3066				truncatable = defGetBoolean(defel);
3067				break;
3068			}
3069		}
3070
3071		if (!truncatable)
3072			ereport(ERROR,
3073					(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
3074					 errmsg("foreign table \"%s\" does not allow truncates",
3075							RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
3076	}
3077	Assert(OidIsValid(serverid));
3078
3079	/*
3080	 * Get connection to the foreign server.  Connection manager will
3081	 * establish new connection if necessary.
3082	 */
3083	user = GetUserMapping(GetUserId(), serverid);
3084	conn = GetConnection(user, false, NULL);
3085
3086	/* Construct the TRUNCATE command string */ view japanese
3087	initStringInfo(&sql);
3088	deparseTruncateSql(&sql, rels, behavior, restart_seqs);
3089
3090	/* Issue the TRUNCATE command to remote server */ view japanese
3091	do_sql_command(conn, sql.data);
3092
3093	pfree(sql.data);
3094}
3095
3096/*
3097 * estimate_path_cost_size
3098 *		Get cost and size estimates for a foreign scan on given foreign relation
3099 *		either a base relation or a join between foreign relations or an upper
3100 *		relation containing foreign relations.
3101 *
3102 * param_join_conds are the parameterization clauses with outer relations.
3103 * pathkeys specify the expected sort order if any for given path being costed.
3104 * fpextra specifies additional post-scan/join-processing steps such as the
3105 * final sort and the LIMIT restriction.
3106 *
3107 * The function returns the cost and size estimates in p_rows, p_width,
3108 * p_disabled_nodes, p_startup_cost and p_total_cost variables.
3109 */ view japanese
3110static void
3111estimate_path_cost_size(PlannerInfo *root,
3112						RelOptInfo *foreignrel,
3113						List *param_join_conds,
3114						List *pathkeys,
3115						PgFdwPathExtraData *fpextra,
3116						double *p_rows, int *p_width,
3117						int *p_disabled_nodes,
3118						Cost *p_startup_cost, Cost *p_total_cost)
3119{
3120	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) foreignrel->fdw_private;
3121	double		rows;
3122	double		retrieved_rows;
3123	int			width;
3124	int			disabled_nodes = 0;
3125	Cost		startup_cost;
3126	Cost		total_cost;
3127
3128	/* Make sure the core code has set up the relation's reltarget */ view japanese
3129	Assert(foreignrel->reltarget);
3130
3131	/*
3132	 * If the table or the server is configured to use remote estimates,
3133	 * connect to the foreign server and execute EXPLAIN to estimate the
3134	 * number of rows selected by the restriction+join clauses.  Otherwise,
3135	 * estimate rows using whatever statistics we have locally, in a way
3136	 * similar to ordinary tables.
3137	 */ view japanese
3138	if (fpinfo->use_remote_estimate)
3139	{
3140		List	   *remote_param_join_conds;
3141		List	   *local_param_join_conds;
3142		StringInfoData sql;
3143		PGconn	   *conn;
3144		Selectivity local_sel;
3145		QualCost	local_cost;
3146		List	   *fdw_scan_tlist = NIL;
3147		List	   *remote_conds;
3148
3149		/* Required only to be passed to deparseSelectStmtForRel */ view japanese
3150		List	   *retrieved_attrs;
3151
3152		/*
3153		 * param_join_conds might contain both clauses that are safe to send
3154		 * across, and clauses that aren't.
3155		 */ view japanese
3156		classifyConditions(root, foreignrel, param_join_conds,
3157						   &remote_param_join_conds, &local_param_join_conds);
3158
3159		/* Build the list of columns to be fetched from the foreign server. */ view japanese
3160		if (IS_JOIN_REL(foreignrel) || IS_UPPER_REL(foreignrel))
3161			fdw_scan_tlist = build_tlist_to_deparse(foreignrel);
3162		else
3163			fdw_scan_tlist = NIL;
3164
3165		/*
3166		 * The complete list of remote conditions includes everything from
3167		 * baserestrictinfo plus any extra join_conds relevant to this
3168		 * particular path.
3169		 */ view japanese
3170		remote_conds = list_concat(remote_param_join_conds,
3171								   fpinfo->remote_conds);
3172
3173		/*
3174		 * Construct EXPLAIN query including the desired SELECT, FROM, and
3175		 * WHERE clauses. Params and other-relation Vars are replaced by dummy
3176		 * values, so don't request params_list.
3177		 */ view japanese
3178		initStringInfo(&sql);
3179		appendStringInfoString(&sql, "EXPLAIN ");
3180		deparseSelectStmtForRel(&sql, root, foreignrel, fdw_scan_tlist,
3181								remote_conds, pathkeys,
3182								fpextra ? fpextra->has_final_sort : false,
3183								fpextra ? fpextra->has_limit : false,
3184								false, &retrieved_attrs, NULL);
3185
3186		/* Get the remote estimate */ view japanese
3187		conn = GetConnection(fpinfo->user, false, NULL);
3188		get_remote_estimate(sql.data, conn, &rows, &width,
3189							&startup_cost, &total_cost);
3190		ReleaseConnection(conn);
3191
3192		retrieved_rows = rows;
3193
3194		/* Factor in the selectivity of the locally-checked quals */
3195		local_sel = clauselist_selectivity(root,
3196										   local_param_join_conds,
3197										   foreignrel->relid,
3198										   JOIN_INNER,
3199										   NULL);
3200		local_sel *= fpinfo->local_conds_sel;
3201
3202		rows = clamp_row_est(rows * local_sel);
3203
3204		/* Add in the eval cost of the locally-checked quals */
3205		startup_cost += fpinfo->local_conds_cost.startup;
3206		total_cost += fpinfo->local_conds_cost.per_tuple * retrieved_rows;
3207		cost_qual_eval(&local_cost, local_param_join_conds, root);
3208		startup_cost += local_cost.startup;
3209		total_cost += local_cost.per_tuple * retrieved_rows;
3210
3211		/*
3212		 * Add in tlist eval cost for each output row.  In case of an
3213		 * aggregate, some of the tlist expressions such as grouping
3214		 * expressions will be evaluated remotely, so adjust the costs.
3215		 */ view japanese
3216		startup_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.startup;
3217		total_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.startup;
3218		total_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.per_tuple * rows;
3219		if (IS_UPPER_REL(foreignrel))
3220		{
3221			QualCost	tlist_cost;
3222
3223			cost_qual_eval(&tlist_cost, fdw_scan_tlist, root);
3224			startup_cost -= tlist_cost.startup;
3225			total_cost -= tlist_cost.startup;
3226			total_cost -= tlist_cost.per_tuple * rows;
3227		}
3228	}
3229	else
3230	{
3231		Cost		run_cost = 0;
3232
3233		/*
3234		 * We don't support join conditions in this mode (hence, no
3235		 * parameterized paths can be made).
3236		 */ view japanese
3237		Assert(param_join_conds == NIL);
3238
3239		/*
3240		 * We will come here again and again with different set of pathkeys or
3241		 * additional post-scan/join-processing steps that caller wants to
3242		 * cost.  We don't need to calculate the cost/size estimates for the
3243		 * underlying scan, join, or grouping each time.  Instead, use those
3244		 * estimates if we have cached them already.
3245		 */ view japanese
3246		if (fpinfo->rel_startup_cost >= 0 && fpinfo->rel_total_cost >= 0)
3247		{
3248			Assert(fpinfo->retrieved_rows >= 0);
3249
3250			rows = fpinfo->rows;
3251			retrieved_rows = fpinfo->retrieved_rows;
3252			width = fpinfo->width;
3253			startup_cost = fpinfo->rel_startup_cost;
3254			run_cost = fpinfo->rel_total_cost - fpinfo->rel_startup_cost;
3255
3256			/*
3257			 * If we estimate the costs of a foreign scan or a foreign join
3258			 * with additional post-scan/join-processing steps, the scan or
3259			 * join costs obtained from the cache wouldn't yet contain the
3260			 * eval costs for the final scan/join target, which would've been
3261			 * updated by apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths(); add the eval costs
3262			 * now.
3263			 */ view japanese
3264			if (fpextra && !IS_UPPER_REL(foreignrel))
3265			{
3266				/* Shouldn't get here unless we have LIMIT */ view japanese
3267				Assert(fpextra->has_limit);
3268				Assert(foreignrel->reloptkind == RELOPT_BASEREL ||
3269					   foreignrel->reloptkind == RELOPT_JOINREL);
3270				startup_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.startup;
3271				run_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.per_tuple * rows;
3272			}
3273		}
3274		else if (IS_JOIN_REL(foreignrel))
3275		{
3276			PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo_i;
3277			PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo_o;
3278			QualCost	join_cost;
3279			QualCost	remote_conds_cost;
3280			double		nrows;
3281
3282			/* Use rows/width estimates made by the core code. */ view japanese
3283			rows = foreignrel->rows;
3284			width = foreignrel->reltarget->width;
3285
3286			/* For join we expect inner and outer relations set */ view japanese
3287			Assert(fpinfo->innerrel && fpinfo->outerrel);
3288
3289			fpinfo_i = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) fpinfo->innerrel->fdw_private;
3290			fpinfo_o = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) fpinfo->outerrel->fdw_private;
3291
3292			/* Estimate of number of rows in cross product */ view japanese
3293			nrows = fpinfo_i->rows * fpinfo_o->rows;
3294
3295			/*
3296			 * Back into an estimate of the number of retrieved rows.  Just in
3297			 * case this is nuts, clamp to at most nrows.
3298			 */ view japanese
3299			retrieved_rows = clamp_row_est(rows / fpinfo->local_conds_sel);
3300			retrieved_rows = Min(retrieved_rows, nrows);
3301
3302			/*
3303			 * The cost of foreign join is estimated as cost of generating
3304			 * rows for the joining relations + cost for applying quals on the
3305			 * rows.
3306			 */ view japanese
3307
3308			/*
3309			 * Calculate the cost of clauses pushed down to the foreign server
3310			 */ view japanese
3311			cost_qual_eval(&remote_conds_cost, fpinfo->remote_conds, root);
3312			/* Calculate the cost of applying join clauses */ view japanese
3313			cost_qual_eval(&join_cost, fpinfo->joinclauses, root);
3314
3315			/*
3316			 * Startup cost includes startup cost of joining relations and the
3317			 * startup cost for join and other clauses. We do not include the
3318			 * startup cost specific to join strategy (e.g. setting up hash
3319			 * tables) since we do not know what strategy the foreign server
3320			 * is going to use.
3321			 */ view japanese
3322			startup_cost = fpinfo_i->rel_startup_cost + fpinfo_o->rel_startup_cost;
3323			startup_cost += join_cost.startup;
3324			startup_cost += remote_conds_cost.startup;
3325			startup_cost += fpinfo->local_conds_cost.startup;
3326
3327			/*
3328			 * Run time cost includes:
3329			 *
3330			 * 1. Run time cost (total_cost - startup_cost) of relations being
3331			 * joined
3332			 *
3333			 * 2. Run time cost of applying join clauses on the cross product
3334			 * of the joining relations.
3335			 *
3336			 * 3. Run time cost of applying pushed down other clauses on the
3337			 * result of join
3338			 *
3339			 * 4. Run time cost of applying nonpushable other clauses locally
3340			 * on the result fetched from the foreign server.
3341			 */ view japanese
3342			run_cost = fpinfo_i->rel_total_cost - fpinfo_i->rel_startup_cost;
3343			run_cost += fpinfo_o->rel_total_cost - fpinfo_o->rel_startup_cost;
3344			run_cost += nrows * join_cost.per_tuple;
3345			nrows = clamp_row_est(nrows * fpinfo->joinclause_sel);
3346			run_cost += nrows * remote_conds_cost.per_tuple;
3347			run_cost += fpinfo->local_conds_cost.per_tuple * retrieved_rows;
3348
3349			/* Add in tlist eval cost for each output row */
3350			startup_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.startup;
3351			run_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.per_tuple * rows;
3352		}
3353		else if (IS_UPPER_REL(foreignrel))
3354		{
3355			RelOptInfo *outerrel = fpinfo->outerrel;
3356			PgFdwRelationInfo *ofpinfo;
3357			AggClauseCosts aggcosts = {0};
3358			double		input_rows;
3359			int			numGroupCols;
3360			double		numGroups = 1;
3361
3362			/* The upper relation should have its outer relation set */ view japanese
3363			Assert(outerrel);
3364			/* and that outer relation should have its reltarget set */ view japanese
3365			Assert(outerrel->reltarget);
3366
3367			/*
3368			 * This cost model is mixture of costing done for sorted and
3369			 * hashed aggregates in cost_agg().  We are not sure which
3370			 * strategy will be considered at remote side, thus for
3371			 * simplicity, we put all startup related costs in startup_cost
3372			 * and all finalization and run cost are added in total_cost.
3373			 */ view japanese
3374
3375			ofpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) outerrel->fdw_private;
3376
3377			/* Get rows from input rel */ view japanese
3378			input_rows = ofpinfo->rows;
3379
3380			/* Collect statistics about aggregates for estimating costs. */ view japanese
3381			if (root->parse->hasAggs)
3382			{
3383				get_agg_clause_costs(root, AGGSPLIT_SIMPLE, &aggcosts);
3384			}
3385
3386			/* Get number of grouping columns and possible number of groups */ view japanese
3387			numGroupCols = list_length(root->processed_groupClause);
3388			numGroups = estimate_num_groups(root,
3389											get_sortgrouplist_exprs(root->processed_groupClause,
3390																	fpinfo->grouped_tlist),
3391											input_rows, NULL, NULL);
3392
3393			/*
3394			 * Get the retrieved_rows and rows estimates.  If there are HAVING
3395			 * quals, account for their selectivity.
3396			 */ view japanese
3397			if (root->hasHavingQual)
3398			{
3399				/* Factor in the selectivity of the remotely-checked quals */ view japanese
3400				retrieved_rows =
3401					clamp_row_est(numGroups *
3402								  clauselist_selectivity(root,
3403														 fpinfo->remote_conds,
3404														 0,
3405														 JOIN_INNER,
3406														 NULL));
3407				/* Factor in the selectivity of the locally-checked quals */ view japanese
3408				rows = clamp_row_est(retrieved_rows * fpinfo->local_conds_sel);
3409			}
3410			else
3411			{
3412				rows = retrieved_rows = numGroups;
3413			}
3414
3415			/* Use width estimate made by the core code. */ view japanese
3416			width = foreignrel->reltarget->width;
3417
3418			/*-----
3419			 * Startup cost includes:
3420			 *	  1. Startup cost for underneath input relation, adjusted for
3421			 *	     tlist replacement by apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths()
3422			 *	  2. Cost of performing aggregation, per cost_agg()
3423			 *-----
3424			 */ view japanese
3425			startup_cost = ofpinfo->rel_startup_cost;
3426			startup_cost += outerrel->reltarget->cost.startup;
3427			startup_cost += aggcosts.transCost.startup;
3428			startup_cost += aggcosts.transCost.per_tuple * input_rows;
3429			startup_cost += aggcosts.finalCost.startup;
3430			startup_cost += (cpu_operator_cost * numGroupCols) * input_rows;
3431
3432			/*-----
3433			 * Run time cost includes:
3434			 *	  1. Run time cost of underneath input relation, adjusted for
3435			 *	     tlist replacement by apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths()
3436			 *	  2. Run time cost of performing aggregation, per cost_agg()
3437			 *-----
3438			 */ view japanese
3439			run_cost = ofpinfo->rel_total_cost - ofpinfo->rel_startup_cost;
3440			run_cost += outerrel->reltarget->cost.per_tuple * input_rows;
3441			run_cost += aggcosts.finalCost.per_tuple * numGroups;
3442			run_cost += cpu_tuple_cost * numGroups;
3443
3444			/* Account for the eval cost of HAVING quals, if any */ view japanese
3445			if (root->hasHavingQual)
3446			{
3447				QualCost	remote_cost;
3448
3449				/* Add in the eval cost of the remotely-checked quals */ view japanese
3450				cost_qual_eval(&remote_cost, fpinfo->remote_conds, root);
3451				startup_cost += remote_cost.startup;
3452				run_cost += remote_cost.per_tuple * numGroups;
3453				/* Add in the eval cost of the locally-checked quals */ view japanese
3454				startup_cost += fpinfo->local_conds_cost.startup;
3455				run_cost += fpinfo->local_conds_cost.per_tuple * retrieved_rows;
3456			}
3457
3458			/* Add in tlist eval cost for each output row */
3459			startup_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.startup;
3460			run_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.per_tuple * rows;
3461		}
3462		else
3463		{
3464			Cost		cpu_per_tuple;
3465
3466			/* Use rows/width estimates made by set_baserel_size_estimates. */ view japanese
3467			rows = foreignrel->rows;
3468			width = foreignrel->reltarget->width;
3469
3470			/*
3471			 * Back into an estimate of the number of retrieved rows.  Just in
3472			 * case this is nuts, clamp to at most foreignrel->tuples.
3473			 */ view japanese
3474			retrieved_rows = clamp_row_est(rows / fpinfo->local_conds_sel);
3475			retrieved_rows = Min(retrieved_rows, foreignrel->tuples);
3476
3477			/*
3478			 * Cost as though this were a seqscan, which is pessimistic.  We
3479			 * effectively imagine the local_conds are being evaluated
3480			 * remotely, too.
3481			 */ view japanese
3482			startup_cost = 0;
3483			run_cost = 0;
3484			run_cost += seq_page_cost * foreignrel->pages;
3485
3486			startup_cost += foreignrel->baserestrictcost.startup;
3487			cpu_per_tuple = cpu_tuple_cost + foreignrel->baserestrictcost.per_tuple;
3488			run_cost += cpu_per_tuple * foreignrel->tuples;
3489
3490			/* Add in tlist eval cost for each output row */ view japanese
3491			startup_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.startup;
3492			run_cost += foreignrel->reltarget->cost.per_tuple * rows;
3493		}
3494
3495		/*
3496		 * Without remote estimates, we have no real way to estimate the cost
3497		 * of generating sorted output.  It could be free if the query plan
3498		 * the remote side would have chosen generates properly-sorted output
3499		 * anyway, but in most cases it will cost something.  Estimate a value
3500		 * high enough that we won't pick the sorted path when the ordering
3501		 * isn't locally useful, but low enough that we'll err on the side of
3502		 * pushing down the ORDER BY clause when it's useful to do so.
3503		 */ view japanese
3504		if (pathkeys != NIL)
3505		{
3506			if (IS_UPPER_REL(foreignrel))
3507			{
3508				Assert(foreignrel->reloptkind == RELOPT_UPPER_REL &&
3509					   fpinfo->stage == UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG);
3510				adjust_foreign_grouping_path_cost(root, pathkeys,
3511												  retrieved_rows, width,
3512												  fpextra->limit_tuples,
3513												  &disabled_nodes,
3514												  &startup_cost, &run_cost);
3515			}
3516			else
3517			{
3518				startup_cost *= DEFAULT_FDW_SORT_MULTIPLIER;
3519				run_cost *= DEFAULT_FDW_SORT_MULTIPLIER;
3520			}
3521		}
3522
3523		total_cost = startup_cost + run_cost;
3524
3525		/* Adjust the cost estimates if we have LIMIT */ view japanese
3526		if (fpextra && fpextra->has_limit)
3527		{
3528			adjust_limit_rows_costs(&rows, &startup_cost, &total_cost,
3529									fpextra->offset_est, fpextra->count_est);
3530			retrieved_rows = rows;
3531		}
3532	}
3533
3534	/*
3535	 * If this includes the final sort step, the given target, which will be
3536	 * applied to the resulting path, might have different expressions from
3537	 * the foreignrel's reltarget (see make_sort_input_target()); adjust tlist
3538	 * eval costs.
3539	 */ view japanese
3540	if (fpextra && fpextra->has_final_sort &&
3541		fpextra->target != foreignrel->reltarget)
3542	{
3543		QualCost	oldcost = foreignrel->reltarget->cost;
3544		QualCost	newcost = fpextra->target->cost;
3545
3546		startup_cost += newcost.startup - oldcost.startup;
3547		total_cost += newcost.startup - oldcost.startup;
3548		total_cost += (newcost.per_tuple - oldcost.per_tuple) * rows;
3549	}
3550
3551	/*
3552	 * Cache the retrieved rows and cost estimates for scans, joins, or
3553	 * groupings without any parameterization, pathkeys, or additional
3554	 * post-scan/join-processing steps, before adding the costs for
3555	 * transferring data from the foreign server.  These estimates are useful
3556	 * for costing remote joins involving this relation or costing other
3557	 * remote operations on this relation such as remote sorts and remote
3558	 * LIMIT restrictions, when the costs can not be obtained from the foreign
3559	 * server.  This function will be called at least once for every foreign
3560	 * relation without any parameterization, pathkeys, or additional
3561	 * post-scan/join-processing steps.
3562	 */ view japanese
3563	if (pathkeys == NIL && param_join_conds == NIL && fpextra == NULL)
3564	{
3565		fpinfo->retrieved_rows = retrieved_rows;
3566		fpinfo->rel_startup_cost = startup_cost;
3567		fpinfo->rel_total_cost = total_cost;
3568	}
3569
3570	/*
3571	 * Add some additional cost factors to account for connection overhead
3572	 * (fdw_startup_cost), transferring data across the network
3573	 * (fdw_tuple_cost per retrieved row), and local manipulation of the data
3574	 * (cpu_tuple_cost per retrieved row).
3575	 */ view japanese
3576	startup_cost += fpinfo->fdw_startup_cost;
3577	total_cost += fpinfo->fdw_startup_cost;
3578	total_cost += fpinfo->fdw_tuple_cost * retrieved_rows;
3579	total_cost += cpu_tuple_cost * retrieved_rows;
3580
3581	/*
3582	 * If we have LIMIT, we should prefer performing the restriction remotely
3583	 * rather than locally, as the former avoids extra row fetches from the
3584	 * remote that the latter might cause.  But since the core code doesn't
3585	 * account for such fetches when estimating the costs of the local
3586	 * restriction (see create_limit_path()), there would be no difference
3587	 * between the costs of the local restriction and the costs of the remote
3588	 * restriction estimated above if we don't use remote estimates (except
3589	 * for the case where the foreignrel is a grouping relation, the given
3590	 * pathkeys is not NIL, and the effects of a bounded sort for that rel is
3591	 * accounted for in costing the remote restriction).  Tweak the costs of
3592	 * the remote restriction to ensure we'll prefer it if LIMIT is a useful
3593	 * one.
3594	 */ view japanese
3595	if (!fpinfo->use_remote_estimate &&
3596		fpextra && fpextra->has_limit &&
3597		fpextra->limit_tuples > 0 &&
3598		fpextra->limit_tuples < fpinfo->rows)
3599	{
3600		Assert(fpinfo->rows > 0);
3601		total_cost -= (total_cost - startup_cost) * 0.05 *
3602			(fpinfo->rows - fpextra->limit_tuples) / fpinfo->rows;
3603	}
3604
3605	/* Return results. */ view japanese
3606	*p_rows = rows;
3607	*p_width = width;
3608	*p_disabled_nodes = disabled_nodes;
3609	*p_startup_cost = startup_cost;
3610	*p_total_cost = total_cost;
3611}
3612
3613/*
3614 * Estimate costs of executing a SQL statement remotely.
3615 * The given "sql" must be an EXPLAIN command.
3616 */ view japanese
3617static void
3618get_remote_estimate(const char *sql, PGconn *conn,
3619					double *rows, int *width,
3620					Cost *startup_cost, Cost *total_cost)
3621{
3622	PGresult   *volatile res = NULL;
3623
3624	/* PGresult must be released before leaving this function. */
3625	PG_TRY();
3626	{
3627		char	   *line;
3628		char	   *p;
3629		int			n;
3630
3631		/*
3632		 * Execute EXPLAIN remotely.
3633		 */ view japanese
3634		res = pgfdw_exec_query(conn, sql, NULL);
3635		if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
3636			pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, false, sql);
3637
3638		/*
3639		 * Extract cost numbers for topmost plan node.  Note we search for a
3640		 * left paren from the end of the line to avoid being confused by
3641		 * other uses of parentheses.
3642		 */ view japanese
3643		line = PQgetvalue(res, 0, 0);
3644		p = strrchr(line, '(');
3645		if (p == NULL)
3646			elog(ERROR, "could not interpret EXPLAIN output: \"%s\"", line);
3647		n = sscanf(p, "(cost=%lf..%lf rows=%lf width=%d)",
3648				   startup_cost, total_cost, rows, width);
3649		if (n != 4)
3650			elog(ERROR, "could not interpret EXPLAIN output: \"%s\"", line);
3651	}
3652	PG_FINALLY();
3653	{
3654		PQclear(res);
3655	}
3656	PG_END_TRY();
3657}
3658
3659/*
3660 * Adjust the cost estimates of a foreign grouping path to include the cost of
3661 * generating properly-sorted output.
3662 */ view japanese
3663static void
3664adjust_foreign_grouping_path_cost(PlannerInfo *root,
3665								  List *pathkeys,
3666								  double retrieved_rows,
3667								  double width,
3668								  double limit_tuples,
3669								  int *p_disabled_nodes,
3670								  Cost *p_startup_cost,
3671								  Cost *p_run_cost)
3672{
3673	/*
3674	 * If the GROUP BY clause isn't sort-able, the plan chosen by the remote
3675	 * side is unlikely to generate properly-sorted output, so it would need
3676	 * an explicit sort; adjust the given costs with cost_sort().  Likewise,
3677	 * if the GROUP BY clause is sort-able but isn't a superset of the given
3678	 * pathkeys, adjust the costs with that function.  Otherwise, adjust the
3679	 * costs by applying the same heuristic as for the scan or join case.
3680	 */ view japanese
3681	if (!grouping_is_sortable(root->processed_groupClause) ||
3682		!pathkeys_contained_in(pathkeys, root->group_pathkeys))
3683	{
3684		Path		sort_path;	/* dummy for result of cost_sort */ view japanese
3685
3686		cost_sort(&sort_path,
3687				  root,
3688				  pathkeys,
3689				  0,
3690				  *p_startup_cost + *p_run_cost,
3691				  retrieved_rows,
3692				  width,
3693				  0.0,
3694				  work_mem,
3695				  limit_tuples);
3696
3697		*p_startup_cost = sort_path.startup_cost;
3698		*p_run_cost = sort_path.total_cost - sort_path.startup_cost;
3699	}
3700	else
3701	{
3702		/*
3703		 * The default extra cost seems too large for foreign-grouping cases;
3704		 * add 1/4th of that default.
3705		 */ view japanese
3706		double		sort_multiplier = 1.0 + (DEFAULT_FDW_SORT_MULTIPLIER
3707											 - 1.0) * 0.25;
3708
3709		*p_startup_cost *= sort_multiplier;
3710		*p_run_cost *= sort_multiplier;
3711	}
3712}
3713
3714/*
3715 * Detect whether we want to process an EquivalenceClass member.
3716 *
3717 * This is a callback for use by generate_implied_equalities_for_column.
3718 */ view japanese
3719static bool
3720ec_member_matches_foreign(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *rel,
3721						  EquivalenceClass *ec, EquivalenceMember *em,
3722						  void *arg)
3723{
3724	ec_member_foreign_arg *state = (ec_member_foreign_arg *) arg;
3725	Expr	   *expr = em->em_expr;
3726
3727	/*
3728	 * If we've identified what we're processing in the current scan, we only
3729	 * want to match that expression.
3730	 */ view japanese
3731	if (state->current != NULL)
3732		return equal(expr, state->current);
3733
3734	/*
3735	 * Otherwise, ignore anything we've already processed.
3736	 */ view japanese
3737	if (list_member(state->already_used, expr))
3738		return false;
3739
3740	/* This is the new target to process. */ view japanese
3741	state->current = expr;
3742	return true;
3743}
3744
3745/*
3746 * Create cursor for node's query with current parameter values.
3747 */ view japanese
3748static void
3749create_cursor(ForeignScanState *node)
3750{
3751	PgFdwScanState *fsstate = (PgFdwScanState *) node->fdw_state;
3752	ExprContext *econtext = node->ss.ps.ps_ExprContext;
3753	int			numParams = fsstate->numParams;
3754	const char **values = fsstate->param_values;
3755	PGconn	   *conn = fsstate->conn;
3756	StringInfoData buf;
3757	PGresult   *res;
3758
3759	/* First, process a pending asynchronous request, if any. */
3760	if (fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq)
3761		process_pending_request(fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq);
3762
3763	/*
3764	 * Construct array of query parameter values in text format.  We do the
3765	 * conversions in the short-lived per-tuple context, so as not to cause a
3766	 * memory leak over repeated scans.
3767	 */ view japanese
3768	if (numParams > 0)
3769	{
3770		MemoryContext oldcontext;
3771
3772		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(econtext->ecxt_per_tuple_memory);
3773
3774		process_query_params(econtext,
3775							 fsstate->param_flinfo,
3776							 fsstate->param_exprs,
3777							 values);
3778
3779		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
3780	}
3781
3782	/* Construct the DECLARE CURSOR command */ view japanese
3783	initStringInfo(&buf);
3784	appendStringInfo(&buf, "DECLARE c%u CURSOR FOR\n%s",
3785					 fsstate->cursor_number, fsstate->query);
3786
3787	/*
3788	 * Notice that we pass NULL for paramTypes, thus forcing the remote server
3789	 * to infer types for all parameters.  Since we explicitly cast every
3790	 * parameter (see deparse.c), the "inference" is trivial and will produce
3791	 * the desired result.  This allows us to avoid assuming that the remote
3792	 * server has the same OIDs we do for the parameters' types.
3793	 */
3794	if (!PQsendQueryParams(conn, buf.data, numParams,
3795						   NULL, values, NULL, NULL, 0))
3796		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, NULL, conn, false, buf.data);
3797
3798	/*
3799	 * Get the result, and check for success.
3800	 *
3801	 * We don't use a PG_TRY block here, so be careful not to throw error
3802	 * without releasing the PGresult.
3803	 */
3804	res = pgfdw_get_result(conn);
3805	if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
3806		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, true, fsstate->query);
3807	PQclear(res);
3808
3809	/* Mark the cursor as created, and show no tuples have been retrieved */ view japanese
3810	fsstate->cursor_exists = true;
3811	fsstate->tuples = NULL;
3812	fsstate->num_tuples = 0;
3813	fsstate->next_tuple = 0;
3814	fsstate->fetch_ct_2 = 0;
3815	fsstate->eof_reached = false;
3816
3817	/* Clean up */
3818	pfree(buf.data);
3819}
3820
3821/*
3822 * Fetch some more rows from the node's cursor.
3823 */ view japanese
3824static void
3825fetch_more_data(ForeignScanState *node)
3826{
3827	PgFdwScanState *fsstate = (PgFdwScanState *) node->fdw_state;
3828	PGresult   *volatile res = NULL;
3829	MemoryContext oldcontext;
3830
3831	/*
3832	 * We'll store the tuples in the batch_cxt.  First, flush the previous
3833	 * batch.
3834	 */ view japanese
3835	fsstate->tuples = NULL;
3836	MemoryContextReset(fsstate->batch_cxt);
3837	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(fsstate->batch_cxt);
3838
3839	/* PGresult must be released before leaving this function. */ view japanese
3840	PG_TRY();
3841	{
3842		PGconn	   *conn = fsstate->conn;
3843		int			numrows;
3844		int			i;
3845
3846		if (fsstate->async_capable)
3847		{
3848			Assert(fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq);
3849
3850			/*
3851			 * The query was already sent by an earlier call to
3852			 * fetch_more_data_begin.  So now we just fetch the result.
3853			 */ view japanese
3854			res = pgfdw_get_result(conn);
3855			/* On error, report the original query, not the FETCH. */
3856			if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
3857				pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, false, fsstate->query);
3858
3859			/* Reset per-connection state */ view japanese
3860			fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq = NULL;
3861		}
3862		else
3863		{
3864			char		sql[64];
3865
3866			/* This is a regular synchronous fetch. */ view japanese
3867			snprintf(sql, sizeof(sql), "FETCH %d FROM c%u",
3868					 fsstate->fetch_size, fsstate->cursor_number);
3869
3870			res = pgfdw_exec_query(conn, sql, fsstate->conn_state);
3871			/* On error, report the original query, not the FETCH. */
3872			if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
3873				pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, false, fsstate->query);
3874		}
3875
3876		/* Convert the data into HeapTuples */ view japanese
3877		numrows = PQntuples(res);
3878		fsstate->tuples = (HeapTuple *) palloc0(numrows * sizeof(HeapTuple));
3879		fsstate->num_tuples = numrows;
3880		fsstate->next_tuple = 0;
3881
3882		for (i = 0; i < numrows; i++)
3883		{
3884			Assert(IsA(node->ss.ps.plan, ForeignScan));
3885
3886			fsstate->tuples[i] =
3887				make_tuple_from_result_row(res, i,
3888										   fsstate->rel,
3889										   fsstate->attinmeta,
3890										   fsstate->retrieved_attrs,
3891										   node,
3892										   fsstate->temp_cxt);
3893		}
3894
3895		/* Update fetch_ct_2 */ view japanese
3896		if (fsstate->fetch_ct_2 < 2)
3897			fsstate->fetch_ct_2++;
3898
3899		/* Must be EOF if we didn't get as many tuples as we asked for. */ view japanese
3900		fsstate->eof_reached = (numrows < fsstate->fetch_size);
3901	}
3902	PG_FINALLY();
3903	{
3904		PQclear(res);
3905	}
3906	PG_END_TRY();
3907
3908	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
3909}
3910
3911/*
3912 * Force assorted GUC parameters to settings that ensure that we'll output
3913 * data values in a form that is unambiguous to the remote server.
3914 *
3915 * This is rather expensive and annoying to do once per row, but there's
3916 * little choice if we want to be sure values are transmitted accurately;
3917 * we can't leave the settings in place between rows for fear of affecting
3918 * user-visible computations.
3919 *
3920 * We use the equivalent of a function SET option to allow the settings to
3921 * persist only until the caller calls reset_transmission_modes().  If an
3922 * error is thrown in between, guc.c will take care of undoing the settings.
3923 *
3924 * The return value is the nestlevel that must be passed to
3925 * reset_transmission_modes() to undo things.
3926 */ view japanese
3927int
3928set_transmission_modes(void)
3929{
3930	int			nestlevel = NewGUCNestLevel();
3931
3932	/*
3933	 * The values set here should match what pg_dump does.  See also
3934	 * configure_remote_session in connection.c.
3935	 */ view japanese
3936	if (DateStyle != USE_ISO_DATES)
3937		(void) set_config_option("datestyle", "ISO",
3938								 PGC_USERSET, PGC_S_SESSION,
3939								 GUC_ACTION_SAVE, true, 0, false);
3940	if (IntervalStyle != INTSTYLE_POSTGRES)
3941		(void) set_config_option("intervalstyle", "postgres",
3942								 PGC_USERSET, PGC_S_SESSION,
3943								 GUC_ACTION_SAVE, true, 0, false);
3944	if (extra_float_digits < 3)
3945		(void) set_config_option("extra_float_digits", "3",
3946								 PGC_USERSET, PGC_S_SESSION,
3947								 GUC_ACTION_SAVE, true, 0, false);
3948
3949	/*
3950	 * In addition force restrictive search_path, in case there are any
3951	 * regproc or similar constants to be printed.
3952	 */ view japanese
3953	(void) set_config_option("search_path", "pg_catalog",
3954							 PGC_USERSET, PGC_S_SESSION,
3955							 GUC_ACTION_SAVE, true, 0, false);
3956
3957	return nestlevel;
3958}
3959
3960/*
3961 * Undo the effects of set_transmission_modes().
3962 */ view japanese
3963void
3964reset_transmission_modes(int nestlevel)
3965{
3966	AtEOXact_GUC(true, nestlevel);
3967}
3968
3969/*
3970 * Utility routine to close a cursor.
3971 */ view japanese
3972static void
3973close_cursor(PGconn *conn, unsigned int cursor_number,
3974			 PgFdwConnState *conn_state)
3975{
3976	char		sql[64];
3977	PGresult   *res;
3978
3979	snprintf(sql, sizeof(sql), "CLOSE c%u", cursor_number);
3980
3981	/*
3982	 * We don't use a PG_TRY block here, so be careful not to throw error
3983	 * without releasing the PGresult.
3984	 */
3985	res = pgfdw_exec_query(conn, sql, conn_state);
3986	if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
3987		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, true, sql);
3988	PQclear(res);
3989}
3990
3991/*
3992 * create_foreign_modify
3993 *		Construct an execution state of a foreign insert/update/delete
3994 *		operation
3995 */ view japanese
3996static PgFdwModifyState *
3997create_foreign_modify(EState *estate,
3998					  RangeTblEntry *rte,
3999					  ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo,
4000					  CmdType operation,
4001					  Plan *subplan,
4002					  char *query,
4003					  List *target_attrs,
4004					  int values_end,
4005					  bool has_returning,
4006					  List *retrieved_attrs)
4007{
4008	PgFdwModifyState *fmstate;
4009	Relation	rel = resultRelInfo->ri_RelationDesc;
4010	TupleDesc	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
4011	Oid			userid;
4012	ForeignTable *table;
4013	UserMapping *user;
4014	AttrNumber	n_params;
4015	Oid			typefnoid;
4016	bool		isvarlena;
4017	ListCell   *lc;
4018
4019	/* Begin constructing PgFdwModifyState. */ view japanese
4020	fmstate = (PgFdwModifyState *) palloc0(sizeof(PgFdwModifyState));
4021	fmstate->rel = rel;
4022
4023	/* Identify which user to do the remote access as. */ view japanese
4024	userid = ExecGetResultRelCheckAsUser(resultRelInfo, estate);
4025
4026	/* Get info about foreign table. */ view japanese
4027	table = GetForeignTable(RelationGetRelid(rel));
4028	user = GetUserMapping(userid, table->serverid);
4029
4030	/* Open connection; report that we'll create a prepared statement. */ view japanese
4031	fmstate->conn = GetConnection(user, true, &fmstate->conn_state);
4032	fmstate->p_name = NULL;		/* prepared statement not made yet */ view japanese
4033
4034	/* Set up remote query information. */ view japanese
4035	fmstate->query = query;
4036	if (operation == CMD_INSERT)
4037	{
4038		fmstate->query = pstrdup(fmstate->query);
4039		fmstate->orig_query = pstrdup(fmstate->query);
4040	}
4041	fmstate->target_attrs = target_attrs;
4042	fmstate->values_end = values_end;
4043	fmstate->has_returning = has_returning;
4044	fmstate->retrieved_attrs = retrieved_attrs;
4045
4046	/* Create context for per-tuple temp workspace. */ view japanese
4047	fmstate->temp_cxt = AllocSetContextCreate(estate->es_query_cxt,
4048											  "postgres_fdw temporary data",
4049											  ALLOCSET_SMALL_SIZES);
4050
4051	/* Prepare for input conversion of RETURNING results. */ view japanese
4052	if (fmstate->has_returning)
4053		fmstate->attinmeta = TupleDescGetAttInMetadata(tupdesc);
4054
4055	/* Prepare for output conversion of parameters used in prepared stmt. */ view japanese
4056	n_params = list_length(fmstate->target_attrs) + 1;
4057	fmstate->p_flinfo = (FmgrInfo *) palloc0(sizeof(FmgrInfo) * n_params);
4058	fmstate->p_nums = 0;
4059
4060	if (operation == CMD_UPDATE || operation == CMD_DELETE)
4061	{
4062		Assert(subplan != NULL);
4063
4064		/* Find the ctid resjunk column in the subplan's result */ view japanese
4065		fmstate->ctidAttno = ExecFindJunkAttributeInTlist(subplan->targetlist,
4066														  "ctid");
4067		if (!AttributeNumberIsValid(fmstate->ctidAttno))
4068			elog(ERROR, "could not find junk ctid column");
4069
4070		/* First transmittable parameter will be ctid */ view japanese
4071		getTypeOutputInfo(TIDOID, &typefnoid, &isvarlena);
4072		fmgr_info(typefnoid, &fmstate->p_flinfo[fmstate->p_nums]);
4073		fmstate->p_nums++;
4074	}
4075
4076	if (operation == CMD_INSERT || operation == CMD_UPDATE)
4077	{
4078		/* Set up for remaining transmittable parameters */ view japanese
4079		foreach(lc, fmstate->target_attrs)
4080		{
4081			int			attnum = lfirst_int(lc);
4082			Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, attnum - 1);
4083
4084			Assert(!attr->attisdropped);
4085
4086			/* Ignore generated columns; they are set to DEFAULT */
4087			if (attr->attgenerated)
4088				continue;
4089			getTypeOutputInfo(attr->atttypid, &typefnoid, &isvarlena);
4090			fmgr_info(typefnoid, &fmstate->p_flinfo[fmstate->p_nums]);
4091			fmstate->p_nums++;
4092		}
4093	}
4094
4095	Assert(fmstate->p_nums <= n_params);
4096
4097	/* Set batch_size from foreign server/table options. */ view japanese
4098	if (operation == CMD_INSERT)
4099		fmstate->batch_size = get_batch_size_option(rel);
4100
4101	fmstate->num_slots = 1;
4102
4103	/* Initialize auxiliary state */ view japanese
4104	fmstate->aux_fmstate = NULL;
4105
4106	return fmstate;
4107}
4108
4109/*
4110 * execute_foreign_modify
4111 *		Perform foreign-table modification as required, and fetch RETURNING
4112 *		result if any.  (This is the shared guts of postgresExecForeignInsert,
4113 *		postgresExecForeignBatchInsert, postgresExecForeignUpdate, and
4114 *		postgresExecForeignDelete.)
4115 */ view japanese
4116static TupleTableSlot **
4117execute_foreign_modify(EState *estate,
4118					   ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo,
4119					   CmdType operation,
4120					   TupleTableSlot **slots,
4121					   TupleTableSlot **planSlots,
4122					   int *numSlots)
4123{
4124	PgFdwModifyState *fmstate = (PgFdwModifyState *) resultRelInfo->ri_FdwState;
4125	ItemPointer ctid = NULL;
4126	const char **p_values;
4127	PGresult   *res;
4128	int			n_rows;
4129	StringInfoData sql;
4130
4131	/* The operation should be INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE */ view japanese
4132	Assert(operation == CMD_INSERT ||
4133		   operation == CMD_UPDATE ||
4134		   operation == CMD_DELETE);
4135
4136	/* First, process a pending asynchronous request, if any. */
4137	if (fmstate->conn_state->pendingAreq)
4138		process_pending_request(fmstate->conn_state->pendingAreq);
4139
4140	/*
4141	 * If the existing query was deparsed and prepared for a different number
4142	 * of rows, rebuild it for the proper number.
4143	 */ view japanese
4144	if (operation == CMD_INSERT && fmstate->num_slots != *numSlots)
4145	{
4146		/* Destroy the prepared statement created previously */ view japanese
4147		if (fmstate->p_name)
4148			deallocate_query(fmstate);
4149
4150		/* Build INSERT string with numSlots records in its VALUES clause. */ view japanese
4151		initStringInfo(&sql);
4152		rebuildInsertSql(&sql, fmstate->rel,
4153						 fmstate->orig_query, fmstate->target_attrs,
4154						 fmstate->values_end, fmstate->p_nums,
4155						 *numSlots - 1);
4156		pfree(fmstate->query);
4157		fmstate->query = sql.data;
4158		fmstate->num_slots = *numSlots;
4159	}
4160
4161	/* Set up the prepared statement on the remote server, if we didn't yet */ view japanese
4162	if (!fmstate->p_name)
4163		prepare_foreign_modify(fmstate);
4164
4165	/*
4166	 * For UPDATE/DELETE, get the ctid that was passed up as a resjunk column
4167	 */ view japanese
4168	if (operation == CMD_UPDATE || operation == CMD_DELETE)
4169	{
4170		Datum		datum;
4171		bool		isNull;
4172
4173		datum = ExecGetJunkAttribute(planSlots[0],
4174									 fmstate->ctidAttno,
4175									 &isNull);
4176		/* shouldn't ever get a null result... */ view japanese
4177		if (isNull)
4178			elog(ERROR, "ctid is NULL");
4179		ctid = (ItemPointer) DatumGetPointer(datum);
4180	}
4181
4182	/* Convert parameters needed by prepared statement to text form */ view japanese
4183	p_values = convert_prep_stmt_params(fmstate, ctid, slots, *numSlots);
4184
4185	/*
4186	 * Execute the prepared statement.
4187	 */ view japanese
4188	if (!PQsendQueryPrepared(fmstate->conn,
4189							 fmstate->p_name,
4190							 fmstate->p_nums * (*numSlots),
4191							 p_values,
4192							 NULL,
4193							 NULL,
4194							 0))
4195		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, NULL, fmstate->conn, false, fmstate->query);
4196
4197	/*
4198	 * Get the result, and check for success.
4199	 *
4200	 * We don't use a PG_TRY block here, so be careful not to throw error
4201	 * without releasing the PGresult.
4202	 */
4203	res = pgfdw_get_result(fmstate->conn);
4204	if (PQresultStatus(res) !=
4205		(fmstate->has_returning ? PGRES_TUPLES_OK : PGRES_COMMAND_OK))
4206		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, fmstate->conn, true, fmstate->query);
4207
4208	/* Check number of rows affected, and fetch RETURNING tuple if any */ view japanese
4209	if (fmstate->has_returning)
4210	{
4211		Assert(*numSlots == 1);
4212		n_rows = PQntuples(res);
4213		if (n_rows > 0)
4214			store_returning_result(fmstate, slots[0], res);
4215	}
4216	else
4217		n_rows = atoi(PQcmdTuples(res));
4218
4219	/* And clean up */ view japanese
4220	PQclear(res);
4221
4222	MemoryContextReset(fmstate->temp_cxt);
4223
4224	*numSlots = n_rows;
4225
4226	/*
4227	 * Return NULL if nothing was inserted/updated/deleted on the remote end
4228	 */ view japanese
4229	return (n_rows > 0) ? slots : NULL;
4230}
4231
4232/*
4233 * prepare_foreign_modify
4234 *		Establish a prepared statement for execution of INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
4235 */ view japanese
4236static void
4237prepare_foreign_modify(PgFdwModifyState *fmstate)
4238{
4239	char		prep_name[NAMEDATALEN];
4240	char	   *p_name;
4241	PGresult   *res;
4242
4243	/*
4244	 * The caller would already have processed a pending asynchronous request
4245	 * if any, so no need to do it here.
4246	 */ view japanese
4247
4248	/* Construct name we'll use for the prepared statement. */ view japanese
4249	snprintf(prep_name, sizeof(prep_name), "pgsql_fdw_prep_%u",
4250			 GetPrepStmtNumber(fmstate->conn));
4251	p_name = pstrdup(prep_name);
4252
4253	/*
4254	 * We intentionally do not specify parameter types here, but leave the
4255	 * remote server to derive them by default.  This avoids possible problems
4256	 * with the remote server using different type OIDs than we do.  All of
4257	 * the prepared statements we use in this module are simple enough that
4258	 * the remote server will make the right choices.
4259	 */ view japanese
4260	if (!PQsendPrepare(fmstate->conn,
4261					   p_name,
4262					   fmstate->query,
4263					   0,
4264					   NULL))
4265		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, NULL, fmstate->conn, false, fmstate->query);
4266
4267	/*
4268	 * Get the result, and check for success.
4269	 *
4270	 * We don't use a PG_TRY block here, so be careful not to throw error
4271	 * without releasing the PGresult.
4272	 */ view japanese
4273	res = pgfdw_get_result(fmstate->conn);
4274	if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
4275		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, fmstate->conn, true, fmstate->query);
4276	PQclear(res);
4277
4278	/* This action shows that the prepare has been done. */ view japanese
4279	fmstate->p_name = p_name;
4280}
4281
4282/*
4283 * convert_prep_stmt_params
4284 *		Create array of text strings representing parameter values
4285 *
4286 * tupleid is ctid to send, or NULL if none
4287 * slot is slot to get remaining parameters from, or NULL if none
4288 *
4289 * Data is constructed in temp_cxt; caller should reset that after use.
4290 */ view japanese
4291static const char **
4292convert_prep_stmt_params(PgFdwModifyState *fmstate,
4293						 ItemPointer tupleid,
4294						 TupleTableSlot **slots,
4295						 int numSlots)
4296{
4297	const char **p_values;
4298	int			i;
4299	int			j;
4300	int			pindex = 0;
4301	MemoryContext oldcontext;
4302
4303	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(fmstate->temp_cxt);
4304
4305	p_values = (const char **) palloc(sizeof(char *) * fmstate->p_nums * numSlots);
4306
4307	/* ctid is provided only for UPDATE/DELETE, which don't allow batching */ view japanese
4308	Assert(!(tupleid != NULL && numSlots > 1));
4309
4310	/* 1st parameter should be ctid, if it's in use */ view japanese
4311	if (tupleid != NULL)
4312	{
4313		Assert(numSlots == 1);
4314		/* don't need set_transmission_modes for TID output */ view japanese
4315		p_values[pindex] = OutputFunctionCall(&fmstate->p_flinfo[pindex],
4316											  PointerGetDatum(tupleid));
4317		pindex++;
4318	}
4319
4320	/* get following parameters from slots */ view japanese
4321	if (slots != NULL && fmstate->target_attrs != NIL)
4322	{
4323		TupleDesc	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(fmstate->rel);
4324		int			nestlevel;
4325		ListCell   *lc;
4326
4327		nestlevel = set_transmission_modes();
4328
4329		for (i = 0; i < numSlots; i++)
4330		{
4331			j = (tupleid != NULL) ? 1 : 0;
4332			foreach(lc, fmstate->target_attrs)
4333			{
4334				int			attnum = lfirst_int(lc);
4335				CompactAttribute *attr = TupleDescCompactAttr(tupdesc, attnum - 1);
4336				Datum		value;
4337				bool		isnull;
4338
4339				/* Ignore generated columns; they are set to DEFAULT */ view japanese
4340				if (attr->attgenerated)
4341					continue;
4342				value = slot_getattr(slots[i], attnum, &isnull);
4343				if (isnull)
4344					p_values[pindex] = NULL;
4345				else
4346					p_values[pindex] = OutputFunctionCall(&fmstate->p_flinfo[j],
4347														  value);
4348				pindex++;
4349				j++;
4350			}
4351		}
4352
4353		reset_transmission_modes(nestlevel);
4354	}
4355
4356	Assert(pindex == fmstate->p_nums * numSlots);
4357
4358	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
4359
4360	return p_values;
4361}
4362
4363/*
4364 * store_returning_result
4365 *		Store the result of a RETURNING clause
4366 *
4367 * On error, be sure to release the PGresult on the way out.  Callers do not
4368 * have PG_TRY blocks to ensure this happens.
4369 */ view japanese
4370static void
4371store_returning_result(PgFdwModifyState *fmstate,
4372					   TupleTableSlot *slot, PGresult *res)
4373{
4374	PG_TRY();
4375	{
4376		HeapTuple	newtup;
4377
4378		newtup = make_tuple_from_result_row(res, 0,
4379											fmstate->rel,
4380											fmstate->attinmeta,
4381											fmstate->retrieved_attrs,
4382											NULL,
4383											fmstate->temp_cxt);
4384
4385		/*
4386		 * The returning slot will not necessarily be suitable to store
4387		 * heaptuples directly, so allow for conversion.
4388		 */ view japanese
4389		ExecForceStoreHeapTuple(newtup, slot, true);
4390	}
4391	PG_CATCH();
4392	{
4393		PQclear(res);
4394		PG_RE_THROW();
4395	}
4396	PG_END_TRY();
4397}
4398
4399/*
4400 * finish_foreign_modify
4401 *		Release resources for a foreign insert/update/delete operation
4402 */ view japanese
4403static void
4404finish_foreign_modify(PgFdwModifyState *fmstate)
4405{
4406	Assert(fmstate != NULL);
4407
4408	/* If we created a prepared statement, destroy it */ view japanese
4409	deallocate_query(fmstate);
4410
4411	/* Release remote connection */ view japanese
4412	ReleaseConnection(fmstate->conn);
4413	fmstate->conn = NULL;
4414}
4415
4416/*
4417 * deallocate_query
4418 *		Deallocate a prepared statement for a foreign insert/update/delete
4419 *		operation
4420 */ view japanese
4421static void
4422deallocate_query(PgFdwModifyState *fmstate)
4423{
4424	char		sql[64];
4425	PGresult   *res;
4426
4427	/* do nothing if the query is not allocated */ view japanese
4428	if (!fmstate->p_name)
4429		return;
4430
4431	snprintf(sql, sizeof(sql), "DEALLOCATE %s", fmstate->p_name);
4432
4433	/*
4434	 * We don't use a PG_TRY block here, so be careful not to throw error
4435	 * without releasing the PGresult.
4436	 */ view japanese
4437	res = pgfdw_exec_query(fmstate->conn, sql, fmstate->conn_state);
4438	if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
4439		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, fmstate->conn, true, sql);
4440	PQclear(res);
4441	pfree(fmstate->p_name);
4442	fmstate->p_name = NULL;
4443}
4444
4445/*
4446 * build_remote_returning
4447 *		Build a RETURNING targetlist of a remote query for performing an
4448 *		UPDATE/DELETE .. RETURNING on a join directly
4449 */ view japanese
4450static List *
4451build_remote_returning(Index rtindex, Relation rel, List *returningList)
4452{
4453	bool		have_wholerow = false;
4454	List	   *tlist = NIL;
4455	List	   *vars;
4456	ListCell   *lc;
4457
4458	Assert(returningList);
4459
4460	vars = pull_var_clause((Node *) returningList, PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
4461
4462	/*
4463	 * If there's a whole-row reference to the target relation, then we'll
4464	 * need all the columns of the relation.
4465	 */ view japanese
4466	foreach(lc, vars)
4467	{
4468		Var		   *var = (Var *) lfirst(lc);
4469
4470		if (IsA(var, Var) &&
4471			var->varno == rtindex &&
4472			var->varattno == InvalidAttrNumber)
4473		{
4474			have_wholerow = true;
4475			break;
4476		}
4477	}
4478
4479	if (have_wholerow)
4480	{
4481		TupleDesc	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
4482		int			i;
4483
4484		for (i = 1; i <= tupdesc->natts; i++)
4485		{
4486			Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, i - 1);
4487			Var		   *var;
4488
4489			/* Ignore dropped attributes. */ view japanese
4490			if (attr->attisdropped)
4491				continue;
4492
4493			var = makeVar(rtindex,
4494						  i,
4495						  attr->atttypid,
4496						  attr->atttypmod,
4497						  attr->attcollation,
4498						  0);
4499
4500			tlist = lappend(tlist,
4501							makeTargetEntry((Expr *) var,
4502											list_length(tlist) + 1,
4503											NULL,
4504											false));
4505		}
4506	}
4507
4508	/* Now add any remaining columns to tlist. */ view japanese
4509	foreach(lc, vars)
4510	{
4511		Var		   *var = (Var *) lfirst(lc);
4512
4513		/*
4514		 * No need for whole-row references to the target relation.  We don't
4515		 * need system columns other than ctid and oid either, since those are
4516		 * set locally.
4517		 */ view japanese
4518		if (IsA(var, Var) &&
4519			var->varno == rtindex &&
4520			var->varattno <= InvalidAttrNumber &&
4521			var->varattno != SelfItemPointerAttributeNumber)
4522			continue;			/* don't need it */ view japanese
4523
4524		if (tlist_member((Expr *) var, tlist))
4525			continue;			/* already got it */
4526
4527		tlist = lappend(tlist,
4528						makeTargetEntry((Expr *) var,
4529										list_length(tlist) + 1,
4530										NULL,
4531										false));
4532	}
4533
4534	list_free(vars);
4535
4536	return tlist;
4537}
4538
4539/*
4540 * rebuild_fdw_scan_tlist
4541 *		Build new fdw_scan_tlist of given foreign-scan plan node from given
4542 *		tlist
4543 *
4544 * There might be columns that the fdw_scan_tlist of the given foreign-scan
4545 * plan node contains that the given tlist doesn't.  The fdw_scan_tlist would
4546 * have contained resjunk columns such as 'ctid' of the target relation and
4547 * 'wholerow' of non-target relations, but the tlist might not contain them,
4548 * for example.  So, adjust the tlist so it contains all the columns specified
4549 * in the fdw_scan_tlist; else setrefs.c will get confused.
4550 */ view japanese
4551static void
4552rebuild_fdw_scan_tlist(ForeignScan *fscan, List *tlist)
4553{
4554	List	   *new_tlist = tlist;
4555	List	   *old_tlist = fscan->fdw_scan_tlist;
4556	ListCell   *lc;
4557
4558	foreach(lc, old_tlist)
4559	{
4560		TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lc);
4561
4562		if (tlist_member(tle->expr, new_tlist))
4563			continue;			/* already got it */ view japanese
4564
4565		new_tlist = lappend(new_tlist,
