4566							makeTargetEntry(tle->expr,
4567											list_length(new_tlist) + 1,
4568											NULL,
4569											false));
4570	}
4571	fscan->fdw_scan_tlist = new_tlist;
4572}
4573
4574/*
4575 * Execute a direct UPDATE/DELETE statement.
4576 */ view japanese
4577static void
4578execute_dml_stmt(ForeignScanState *node)
4579{
4580	PgFdwDirectModifyState *dmstate = (PgFdwDirectModifyState *) node->fdw_state;
4581	ExprContext *econtext = node->ss.ps.ps_ExprContext;
4582	int			numParams = dmstate->numParams;
4583	const char **values = dmstate->param_values;
4584
4585	/* First, process a pending asynchronous request, if any. */ view japanese
4586	if (dmstate->conn_state->pendingAreq)
4587		process_pending_request(dmstate->conn_state->pendingAreq);
4588
4589	/*
4590	 * Construct array of query parameter values in text format.
4591	 */ view japanese
4592	if (numParams > 0)
4593		process_query_params(econtext,
4594							 dmstate->param_flinfo,
4595							 dmstate->param_exprs,
4596							 values);
4597
4598	/*
4599	 * Notice that we pass NULL for paramTypes, thus forcing the remote server
4600	 * to infer types for all parameters.  Since we explicitly cast every
4601	 * parameter (see deparse.c), the "inference" is trivial and will produce
4602	 * the desired result.  This allows us to avoid assuming that the remote
4603	 * server has the same OIDs we do for the parameters' types.
4604	 */ view japanese
4605	if (!PQsendQueryParams(dmstate->conn, dmstate->query, numParams,
4606						   NULL, values, NULL, NULL, 0))
4607		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, NULL, dmstate->conn, false, dmstate->query);
4608
4609	/*
4610	 * Get the result, and check for success.
4611	 *
4612	 * We use a memory context callback to ensure that the PGresult will be
4613	 * released, even if the query fails somewhere that's outside our control.
4614	 * The callback is already registered, just need to fill in its arg.
4615	 */ view japanese
4616	Assert(dmstate->result == NULL);
4617	dmstate->result = pgfdw_get_result(dmstate->conn);
4618	dmstate->result_cb.arg = dmstate->result;
4619
4620	if (PQresultStatus(dmstate->result) !=
4621		(dmstate->has_returning ? PGRES_TUPLES_OK : PGRES_COMMAND_OK))
4622		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, dmstate->result, dmstate->conn, false,
4623						   dmstate->query);
4624
4625	/* Get the number of rows affected. */ view japanese
4626	if (dmstate->has_returning)
4627		dmstate->num_tuples = PQntuples(dmstate->result);
4628	else
4629		dmstate->num_tuples = atoi(PQcmdTuples(dmstate->result));
4630}
4631
4632/*
4633 * Get the result of a RETURNING clause.
4634 */ view japanese
4635static TupleTableSlot *
4636get_returning_data(ForeignScanState *node)
4637{
4638	PgFdwDirectModifyState *dmstate = (PgFdwDirectModifyState *) node->fdw_state;
4639	EState	   *estate = node->ss.ps.state;
4640	ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo = node->resultRelInfo;
4641	TupleTableSlot *slot = node->ss.ss_ScanTupleSlot;
4642	TupleTableSlot *resultSlot;
4643
4644	Assert(resultRelInfo->ri_projectReturning);
4645
4646	/* If we didn't get any tuples, must be end of data. */ view japanese
4647	if (dmstate->next_tuple >= dmstate->num_tuples)
4648		return ExecClearTuple(slot);
4649
4650	/* Increment the command es_processed count if necessary. */ view japanese
4651	if (dmstate->set_processed)
4652		estate->es_processed += 1;
4653
4654	/*
4655	 * Store a RETURNING tuple.  If has_returning is false, just emit a dummy
4656	 * tuple.  (has_returning is false when the local query is of the form
4657	 * "UPDATE/DELETE .. RETURNING 1" for example.)
4658	 */ view japanese
4659	if (!dmstate->has_returning)
4660	{
4661		ExecStoreAllNullTuple(slot);
4662		resultSlot = slot;
4663	}
4664	else
4665	{
4666		HeapTuple	newtup;
4667
4668		newtup = make_tuple_from_result_row(dmstate->result,
4669											dmstate->next_tuple,
4670											dmstate->rel,
4671											dmstate->attinmeta,
4672											dmstate->retrieved_attrs,
4673											node,
4674											dmstate->temp_cxt);
4675		ExecStoreHeapTuple(newtup, slot, false);
4676		/* Get the updated/deleted tuple. */ view japanese
4677		if (dmstate->rel)
4678			resultSlot = slot;
4679		else
4680			resultSlot = apply_returning_filter(dmstate, resultRelInfo, slot, estate);
4681	}
4682	dmstate->next_tuple++;
4683
4684	/* Make slot available for evaluation of the local query RETURNING list. */ view japanese
4685	resultRelInfo->ri_projectReturning->pi_exprContext->ecxt_scantuple =
4686		resultSlot;
4687
4688	return slot;
4689}
4690
4691/*
4692 * Initialize a filter to extract an updated/deleted tuple from a scan tuple.
4693 */ view japanese
4694static void
4695init_returning_filter(PgFdwDirectModifyState *dmstate,
4696					  List *fdw_scan_tlist,
4697					  Index rtindex)
4698{
4699	TupleDesc	resultTupType = RelationGetDescr(dmstate->resultRel);
4700	ListCell   *lc;
4701	int			i;
4702
4703	/*
4704	 * Calculate the mapping between the fdw_scan_tlist's entries and the
4705	 * result tuple's attributes.
4706	 *
4707	 * The "map" is an array of indexes of the result tuple's attributes in
4708	 * fdw_scan_tlist, i.e., one entry for every attribute of the result
4709	 * tuple.  We store zero for any attributes that don't have the
4710	 * corresponding entries in that list, marking that a NULL is needed in
4711	 * the result tuple.
4712	 *
4713	 * Also get the indexes of the entries for ctid and oid if any.
4714	 */ view japanese
4715	dmstate->attnoMap = (AttrNumber *)
4716		palloc0(resultTupType->natts * sizeof(AttrNumber));
4717
4718	dmstate->ctidAttno = dmstate->oidAttno = 0;
4719
4720	i = 1;
4721	dmstate->hasSystemCols = false;
4722	foreach(lc, fdw_scan_tlist)
4723	{
4724		TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lc);
4725		Var		   *var = (Var *) tle->expr;
4726
4727		Assert(IsA(var, Var));
4728
4729		/*
4730		 * If the Var is a column of the target relation to be retrieved from
4731		 * the foreign server, get the index of the entry.
4732		 */ view japanese
4733		if (var->varno == rtindex &&
4734			list_member_int(dmstate->retrieved_attrs, i))
4735		{
4736			int			attrno = var->varattno;
4737
4738			if (attrno < 0)
4739			{
4740				/*
4741				 * We don't retrieve system columns other than ctid and oid.
4742				 */ view japanese
4743				if (attrno == SelfItemPointerAttributeNumber)
4744					dmstate->ctidAttno = i;
4745				else
4746					Assert(false);
4747				dmstate->hasSystemCols = true;
4748			}
4749			else
4750			{
4751				/*
4752				 * We don't retrieve whole-row references to the target
4753				 * relation either.
4754				 */ view japanese
4755				Assert(attrno > 0);
4756
4757				dmstate->attnoMap[attrno - 1] = i;
4758			}
4759		}
4760		i++;
4761	}
4762}
4763
4764/*
4765 * Extract and return an updated/deleted tuple from a scan tuple.
4766 */ view japanese
4767static TupleTableSlot *
4768apply_returning_filter(PgFdwDirectModifyState *dmstate,
4769					   ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo,
4770					   TupleTableSlot *slot,
4771					   EState *estate)
4772{
4773	TupleDesc	resultTupType = RelationGetDescr(dmstate->resultRel);
4774	TupleTableSlot *resultSlot;
4775	Datum	   *values;
4776	bool	   *isnull;
4777	Datum	   *old_values;
4778	bool	   *old_isnull;
4779	int			i;
4780
4781	/*
4782	 * Use the return tuple slot as a place to store the result tuple.
4783	 */ view japanese
4784	resultSlot = ExecGetReturningSlot(estate, resultRelInfo);
4785
4786	/*
4787	 * Extract all the values of the scan tuple.
4788	 */ view japanese
4789	slot_getallattrs(slot);
4790	old_values = slot->tts_values;
4791	old_isnull = slot->tts_isnull;
4792
4793	/*
4794	 * Prepare to build the result tuple.
4795	 */ view japanese
4796	ExecClearTuple(resultSlot);
4797	values = resultSlot->tts_values;
4798	isnull = resultSlot->tts_isnull;
4799
4800	/*
4801	 * Transpose data into proper fields of the result tuple.
4802	 */ view japanese
4803	for (i = 0; i < resultTupType->natts; i++)
4804	{
4805		int			j = dmstate->attnoMap[i];
4806
4807		if (j == 0)
4808		{
4809			values[i] = (Datum) 0;
4810			isnull[i] = true;
4811		}
4812		else
4813		{
4814			values[i] = old_values[j - 1];
4815			isnull[i] = old_isnull[j - 1];
4816		}
4817	}
4818
4819	/*
4820	 * Build the virtual tuple.
4821	 */ view japanese
4822	ExecStoreVirtualTuple(resultSlot);
4823
4824	/*
4825	 * If we have any system columns to return, materialize a heap tuple in
4826	 * the slot from column values set above and install system columns in
4827	 * that tuple.
4828	 */ view japanese
4829	if (dmstate->hasSystemCols)
4830	{
4831		HeapTuple	resultTup = ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(resultSlot, true, NULL);
4832
4833		/* ctid */
4834		if (dmstate->ctidAttno)
4835		{
4836			ItemPointer ctid = NULL;
4837
4838			ctid = (ItemPointer) DatumGetPointer(old_values[dmstate->ctidAttno - 1]);
4839			resultTup->t_self = *ctid;
4840		}
4841
4842		/*
4843		 * And remaining columns
4844		 *
4845		 * Note: since we currently don't allow the target relation to appear
4846		 * on the nullable side of an outer join, any system columns wouldn't
4847		 * go to NULL.
4848		 *
4849		 * Note: no need to care about tableoid here because it will be
4850		 * initialized in ExecProcessReturning().
4851		 */ view japanese
4852		HeapTupleHeaderSetXmin(resultTup->t_data, InvalidTransactionId);
4853		HeapTupleHeaderSetXmax(resultTup->t_data, InvalidTransactionId);
4854		HeapTupleHeaderSetCmin(resultTup->t_data, InvalidTransactionId);
4855	}
4856
4857	/*
4858	 * And return the result tuple.
4859	 */ view japanese
4860	return resultSlot;
4861}
4862
4863/*
4864 * Prepare for processing of parameters used in remote query.
4865 */ view japanese
4866static void
4867prepare_query_params(PlanState *node,
4868					 List *fdw_exprs,
4869					 int numParams,
4870					 FmgrInfo **param_flinfo,
4871					 List **param_exprs,
4872					 const char ***param_values)
4873{
4874	int			i;
4875	ListCell   *lc;
4876
4877	Assert(numParams > 0);
4878
4879	/* Prepare for output conversion of parameters used in remote query. */ view japanese
4880	*param_flinfo = (FmgrInfo *) palloc0(sizeof(FmgrInfo) * numParams);
4881
4882	i = 0;
4883	foreach(lc, fdw_exprs)
4884	{
4885		Node	   *param_expr = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
4886		Oid			typefnoid;
4887		bool		isvarlena;
4888
4889		getTypeOutputInfo(exprType(param_expr), &typefnoid, &isvarlena);
4890		fmgr_info(typefnoid, &(*param_flinfo)[i]);
4891		i++;
4892	}
4893
4894	/*
4895	 * Prepare remote-parameter expressions for evaluation.  (Note: in
4896	 * practice, we expect that all these expressions will be just Params, so
4897	 * we could possibly do something more efficient than using the full
4898	 * expression-eval machinery for this.  But probably there would be little
4899	 * benefit, and it'd require postgres_fdw to know more than is desirable
4900	 * about Param evaluation.)
4901	 */ view japanese
4902	*param_exprs = ExecInitExprList(fdw_exprs, node);
4903
4904	/* Allocate buffer for text form of query parameters. */ view japanese
4905	*param_values = (const char **) palloc0(numParams * sizeof(char *));
4906}
4907
4908/*
4909 * Construct array of query parameter values in text format.
4910 */ view japanese
4911static void
4912process_query_params(ExprContext *econtext,
4913					 FmgrInfo *param_flinfo,
4914					 List *param_exprs,
4915					 const char **param_values)
4916{
4917	int			nestlevel;
4918	int			i;
4919	ListCell   *lc;
4920
4921	nestlevel = set_transmission_modes();
4922
4923	i = 0;
4924	foreach(lc, param_exprs)
4925	{
4926		ExprState  *expr_state = (ExprState *) lfirst(lc);
4927		Datum		expr_value;
4928		bool		isNull;
4929
4930		/* Evaluate the parameter expression */ view japanese
4931		expr_value = ExecEvalExpr(expr_state, econtext, &isNull);
4932
4933		/*
4934		 * Get string representation of each parameter value by invoking
4935		 * type-specific output function, unless the value is null.
4936		 */ view japanese
4937		if (isNull)
4938			param_values[i] = NULL;
4939		else
4940			param_values[i] = OutputFunctionCall(&param_flinfo[i], expr_value);
4941
4942		i++;
4943	}
4944
4945	reset_transmission_modes(nestlevel);
4946}
4947
4948/*
4949 * postgresAnalyzeForeignTable
4950 *		Test whether analyzing this foreign table is supported
4951 */ view japanese
4952static bool
4953postgresAnalyzeForeignTable(Relation relation,
4954							AcquireSampleRowsFunc *func,
4955							BlockNumber *totalpages)
4956{
4957	ForeignTable *table;
4958	UserMapping *user;
4959	PGconn	   *conn;
4960	StringInfoData sql;
4961	PGresult   *volatile res = NULL;
4962
4963	/* Return the row-analysis function pointer */ view japanese
4964	*func = postgresAcquireSampleRowsFunc;
4965
4966	/*
4967	 * Now we have to get the number of pages.  It's annoying that the ANALYZE
4968	 * API requires us to return that now, because it forces some duplication
4969	 * of effort between this routine and postgresAcquireSampleRowsFunc.  But
4970	 * it's probably not worth redefining that API at this point.
4971	 */ view japanese
4972
4973	/*
4974	 * Get the connection to use.  We do the remote access as the table's
4975	 * owner, even if the ANALYZE was started by some other user.
4976	 */
4977	table = GetForeignTable(RelationGetRelid(relation));
4978	user = GetUserMapping(relation->rd_rel->relowner, table->serverid);
4979	conn = GetConnection(user, false, NULL);
4980
4981	/*
4982	 * Construct command to get page count for relation.
4983	 */
4984	initStringInfo(&sql);
4985	deparseAnalyzeSizeSql(&sql, relation);
4986
4987	/* In what follows, do not risk leaking any PGresults. */
4988	PG_TRY();
4989	{
4990		res = pgfdw_exec_query(conn, sql.data, NULL);
4991		if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
4992			pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, false, sql.data);
4993
4994		if (PQntuples(res) != 1 || PQnfields(res) != 1)
4995			elog(ERROR, "unexpected result from deparseAnalyzeSizeSql query");
4996		*totalpages = strtoul(PQgetvalue(res, 0, 0), NULL, 10);
4997	}
4998	PG_FINALLY();
4999	{
5000		PQclear(res);
5001	}
5002	PG_END_TRY();
5003
5004	ReleaseConnection(conn);
5005
5006	return true;
5007}
5008
5009/*
5010 * postgresGetAnalyzeInfoForForeignTable
5011 *		Count tuples in foreign table (just get pg_class.reltuples).
5012 *
5013 * can_tablesample determines if the remote relation supports acquiring the
5014 * sample using TABLESAMPLE.
5015 */ view japanese
5016static double
5017postgresGetAnalyzeInfoForForeignTable(Relation relation, bool *can_tablesample)
5018{
5019	ForeignTable *table;
5020	UserMapping *user;
5021	PGconn	   *conn;
5022	StringInfoData sql;
5023	PGresult   *volatile res = NULL;
5024	volatile double reltuples = -1;
5025	volatile char relkind = 0;
5026
5027	/* assume the remote relation does not support TABLESAMPLE */ view japanese
5028	*can_tablesample = false;
5029
5030	/*
5031	 * Get the connection to use.  We do the remote access as the table's
5032	 * owner, even if the ANALYZE was started by some other user.
5033	 */
5034	table = GetForeignTable(RelationGetRelid(relation));
5035	user = GetUserMapping(relation->rd_rel->relowner, table->serverid);
5036	conn = GetConnection(user, false, NULL);
5037
5038	/*
5039	 * Construct command to get page count for relation.
5040	 */ view japanese
5041	initStringInfo(&sql);
5042	deparseAnalyzeInfoSql(&sql, relation);
5043
5044	/* In what follows, do not risk leaking any PGresults. */
5045	PG_TRY();
5046	{
5047		res = pgfdw_exec_query(conn, sql.data, NULL);
5048		if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
5049			pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, false, sql.data);
5050
5051		if (PQntuples(res) != 1 || PQnfields(res) != 2)
5052			elog(ERROR, "unexpected result from deparseAnalyzeInfoSql query");
5053		reltuples = strtod(PQgetvalue(res, 0, 0), NULL);
5054		relkind = *(PQgetvalue(res, 0, 1));
5055	}
5056	PG_FINALLY();
5057	{
5058		if (res)
5059			PQclear(res);
5060	}
5061	PG_END_TRY();
5062
5063	ReleaseConnection(conn);
5064
5065	/* TABLESAMPLE is supported only for regular tables and matviews */ view japanese
5066	*can_tablesample = (relkind == RELKIND_RELATION ||
5067						relkind == RELKIND_MATVIEW ||
5068						relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE);
5069
5070	return reltuples;
5071}
5072
5073/*
5074 * Acquire a random sample of rows from foreign table managed by postgres_fdw.
5075 *
5076 * Selected rows are returned in the caller-allocated array rows[],
5077 * which must have at least targrows entries.
5078 * The actual number of rows selected is returned as the function result.
5079 * We also count the total number of rows in the table and return it into
5080 * *totalrows.  Note that *totaldeadrows is always set to 0.
5081 *
5082 * Note that the returned list of rows is not always in order by physical
5083 * position in the table.  Therefore, correlation estimates derived later
5084 * may be meaningless, but it's OK because we don't use the estimates
5085 * currently (the planner only pays attention to correlation for indexscans).
5086 */ view japanese
5087static int
5088postgresAcquireSampleRowsFunc(Relation relation, int elevel,
5089							  HeapTuple *rows, int targrows,
5090							  double *totalrows,
5091							  double *totaldeadrows)
5092{
5093	PgFdwAnalyzeState astate;
5094	ForeignTable *table;
5095	ForeignServer *server;
5096	UserMapping *user;
5097	PGconn	   *conn;
5098	int			server_version_num;
5099	PgFdwSamplingMethod method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_AUTO;	/* auto is default */ view japanese
5100	double		sample_frac = -1.0;
5101	double		reltuples;
5102	unsigned int cursor_number;
5103	StringInfoData sql;
5104	PGresult   *volatile res = NULL;
5105	ListCell   *lc;
5106
5107	/* Initialize workspace state */ view japanese
5108	astate.rel = relation;
5109	astate.attinmeta = TupleDescGetAttInMetadata(RelationGetDescr(relation));
5110
5111	astate.rows = rows;
5112	astate.targrows = targrows;
5113	astate.numrows = 0;
5114	astate.samplerows = 0;
5115	astate.rowstoskip = -1;		/* -1 means not set yet */ view japanese
5116	reservoir_init_selection_state(&astate.rstate, targrows);
5117
5118	/* Remember ANALYZE context, and create a per-tuple temp context */ view japanese
5119	astate.anl_cxt = CurrentMemoryContext;
5120	astate.temp_cxt = AllocSetContextCreate(CurrentMemoryContext,
5121											"postgres_fdw temporary data",
5122											ALLOCSET_SMALL_SIZES);
5123
5124	/*
5125	 * Get the connection to use.  We do the remote access as the table's
5126	 * owner, even if the ANALYZE was started by some other user.
5127	 */ view japanese
5128	table = GetForeignTable(RelationGetRelid(relation));
5129	server = GetForeignServer(table->serverid);
5130	user = GetUserMapping(relation->rd_rel->relowner, table->serverid);
5131	conn = GetConnection(user, false, NULL);
5132
5133	/* We'll need server version, so fetch it now. */ view japanese
5134	server_version_num = PQserverVersion(conn);
5135
5136	/*
5137	 * What sampling method should we use?
5138	 */ view japanese
5139	foreach(lc, server->options)
5140	{
5141		DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
5142
5143		if (strcmp(def->defname, "analyze_sampling") == 0)
5144		{
5145			char	   *value = defGetString(def);
5146
5147			if (strcmp(value, "off") == 0)
5148				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_OFF;
5149			else if (strcmp(value, "auto") == 0)
5150				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_AUTO;
5151			else if (strcmp(value, "random") == 0)
5152				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_RANDOM;
5153			else if (strcmp(value, "system") == 0)
5154				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_SYSTEM;
5155			else if (strcmp(value, "bernoulli") == 0)
5156				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_BERNOULLI;
5157
5158			break;
5159		}
5160	}
5161
5162	foreach(lc, table->options)
5163	{
5164		DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
5165
5166		if (strcmp(def->defname, "analyze_sampling") == 0)
5167		{
5168			char	   *value = defGetString(def);
5169
5170			if (strcmp(value, "off") == 0)
5171				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_OFF;
5172			else if (strcmp(value, "auto") == 0)
5173				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_AUTO;
5174			else if (strcmp(value, "random") == 0)
5175				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_RANDOM;
5176			else if (strcmp(value, "system") == 0)
5177				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_SYSTEM;
5178			else if (strcmp(value, "bernoulli") == 0)
5179				method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_BERNOULLI;
5180
5181			break;
5182		}
5183	}
5184
5185	/*
5186	 * Error-out if explicitly required one of the TABLESAMPLE methods, but
5187	 * the server does not support it.
5188	 */ view japanese
5189	if ((server_version_num < 95000) &&
5190		(method == ANALYZE_SAMPLE_SYSTEM ||
5191		 method == ANALYZE_SAMPLE_BERNOULLI))
5192		ereport(ERROR,
5193				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
5194				 errmsg("remote server does not support TABLESAMPLE feature")));
5195
5196	/*
5197	 * If we've decided to do remote sampling, calculate the sampling rate. We
5198	 * need to get the number of tuples from the remote server, but skip that
5199	 * network round-trip if not needed.
5200	 */ view japanese
5201	if (method != ANALYZE_SAMPLE_OFF)
5202	{
5203		bool		can_tablesample;
5204
5205		reltuples = postgresGetAnalyzeInfoForForeignTable(relation,
5206														  &can_tablesample);
5207
5208		/*
5209		 * Make sure we're not choosing TABLESAMPLE when the remote relation
5210		 * does not support that. But only do this for "auto" - if the user
5211		 * explicitly requested BERNOULLI/SYSTEM, it's better to fail.
5212		 */ view japanese
5213		if (!can_tablesample && (method == ANALYZE_SAMPLE_AUTO))
5214			method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_RANDOM;
5215
5216		/*
5217		 * Remote's reltuples could be 0 or -1 if the table has never been
5218		 * vacuumed/analyzed.  In that case, disable sampling after all.
5219		 */ view japanese
5220		if ((reltuples <= 0) || (targrows >= reltuples))
5221			method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_OFF;
5222		else
5223		{
5224			/*
5225			 * All supported sampling methods require sampling rate, not
5226			 * target rows directly, so we calculate that using the remote
5227			 * reltuples value. That's imperfect, because it might be off a
5228			 * good deal, but that's not something we can (or should) address
5229			 * here.
5230			 *
5231			 * If reltuples is too low (i.e. when table grew), we'll end up
5232			 * sampling more rows - but then we'll apply the local sampling,
5233			 * so we get the expected sample size. This is the same outcome as
5234			 * without remote sampling.
5235			 *
5236			 * If reltuples is too high (e.g. after bulk DELETE), we will end
5237			 * up sampling too few rows.
5238			 *
5239			 * We can't really do much better here - we could try sampling a
5240			 * bit more rows, but we don't know how off the reltuples value is
5241			 * so how much is "a bit more"?
5242			 *
5243			 * Furthermore, the targrows value for partitions is determined
5244			 * based on table size (relpages), which can be off in different
5245			 * ways too. Adjusting the sampling rate here might make the issue
5246			 * worse.
5247			 */ view japanese
5248			sample_frac = targrows / reltuples;
5249
5250			/*
5251			 * We should never get sampling rate outside the valid range
5252			 * (between 0.0 and 1.0), because those cases should be covered by
5253			 * the previous branch that sets ANALYZE_SAMPLE_OFF.
5254			 */ view japanese
5255			Assert(sample_frac >= 0.0 && sample_frac <= 1.0);
5256		}
5257	}
5258
5259	/*
5260	 * For "auto" method, pick the one we believe is best. For servers with
5261	 * TABLESAMPLE support we pick BERNOULLI, for old servers we fall-back to
5262	 * random() to at least reduce network transfer.
5263	 */ view japanese
5264	if (method == ANALYZE_SAMPLE_AUTO)
5265	{
5266		if (server_version_num < 95000)
5267			method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_RANDOM;
5268		else
5269			method = ANALYZE_SAMPLE_BERNOULLI;
5270	}
5271
5272	/*
5273	 * Construct cursor that retrieves whole rows from remote.
5274	 */ view japanese
5275	cursor_number = GetCursorNumber(conn);
5276	initStringInfo(&sql);
5277	appendStringInfo(&sql, "DECLARE c%u CURSOR FOR ", cursor_number);
5278
5279	deparseAnalyzeSql(&sql, relation, method, sample_frac, &astate.retrieved_attrs);
5280
5281	/* In what follows, do not risk leaking any PGresults. */
5282	PG_TRY();
5283	{
5284		char		fetch_sql[64];
5285		int			fetch_size;
5286
5287		res = pgfdw_exec_query(conn, sql.data, NULL);
5288		if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
5289			pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, false, sql.data);
5290		PQclear(res);
5291		res = NULL;
5292
5293		/*
5294		 * Determine the fetch size.  The default is arbitrary, but shouldn't
5295		 * be enormous.
5296		 */ view japanese
5297		fetch_size = 100;
5298		foreach(lc, server->options)
5299		{
5300			DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
5301
5302			if (strcmp(def->defname, "fetch_size") == 0)
5303			{
5304				(void) parse_int(defGetString(def), &fetch_size, 0, NULL);
5305				break;
5306			}
5307		}
5308		foreach(lc, table->options)
5309		{
5310			DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
5311
5312			if (strcmp(def->defname, "fetch_size") == 0)
5313			{
5314				(void) parse_int(defGetString(def), &fetch_size, 0, NULL);
5315				break;
5316			}
5317		}
5318
5319		/* Construct command to fetch rows from remote. */ view japanese
5320		snprintf(fetch_sql, sizeof(fetch_sql), "FETCH %d FROM c%u",
5321				 fetch_size, cursor_number);
5322
5323		/* Retrieve and process rows a batch at a time. */ view japanese
5324		for (;;)
5325		{
5326			int			numrows;
5327			int			i;
5328
5329			/* Allow users to cancel long query */ view japanese
5330			CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
5331
5332			/*
5333			 * XXX possible future improvement: if rowstoskip is large, we
5334			 * could issue a MOVE rather than physically fetching the rows,
5335			 * then just adjust rowstoskip and samplerows appropriately.
5336			 */ view japanese
5337
5338			/* Fetch some rows */ view japanese
5339			res = pgfdw_exec_query(conn, fetch_sql, NULL);
5340			/* On error, report the original query, not the FETCH. */
5341			if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
5342				pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, false, sql.data);
5343
5344			/* Process whatever we got. */ view japanese
5345			numrows = PQntuples(res);
5346			for (i = 0; i < numrows; i++)
5347				analyze_row_processor(res, i, &astate);
5348
5349			PQclear(res);
5350			res = NULL;
5351
5352			/* Must be EOF if we didn't get all the rows requested. */ view japanese
5353			if (numrows < fetch_size)
5354				break;
5355		}
5356
5357		/* Close the cursor, just to be tidy. */ view japanese
5358		close_cursor(conn, cursor_number, NULL);
5359	}
5360	PG_CATCH();
5361	{
5362		PQclear(res);
5363		PG_RE_THROW();
5364	}
5365	PG_END_TRY();
5366
5367	ReleaseConnection(conn);
5368
5369	/* We assume that we have no dead tuple. */ view japanese
5370	*totaldeadrows = 0.0;
5371
5372	/*
5373	 * Without sampling, we've retrieved all living tuples from foreign
5374	 * server, so report that as totalrows.  Otherwise use the reltuples
5375	 * estimate we got from the remote side.
5376	 */ view japanese
5377	if (method == ANALYZE_SAMPLE_OFF)
5378		*totalrows = astate.samplerows;
5379	else
5380		*totalrows = reltuples;
5381
5382	/*
5383	 * Emit some interesting relation info
5384	 */ view japanese
5385	ereport(elevel,
5386			(errmsg("\"%s\": table contains %.0f rows, %d rows in sample",
5387					RelationGetRelationName(relation),
5388					*totalrows, astate.numrows)));
5389
5390	return astate.numrows;
5391}
5392
5393/*
5394 * Collect sample rows from the result of query.
5395 *	 - Use all tuples in sample until target # of samples are collected.
5396 *	 - Subsequently, replace already-sampled tuples randomly.
5397 */ view japanese
5398static void
5399analyze_row_processor(PGresult *res, int row, PgFdwAnalyzeState *astate)
5400{
5401	int			targrows = astate->targrows;
5402	int			pos;			/* array index to store tuple in */ view japanese
5403	MemoryContext oldcontext;
5404
5405	/* Always increment sample row counter. */ view japanese
5406	astate->samplerows += 1;
5407
5408	/*
5409	 * Determine the slot where this sample row should be stored.  Set pos to
5410	 * negative value to indicate the row should be skipped.
5411	 */ view japanese
5412	if (astate->numrows < targrows)
5413	{
5414		/* First targrows rows are always included into the sample */ view japanese
5415		pos = astate->numrows++;
5416	}
5417	else
5418	{
5419		/*
5420		 * Now we start replacing tuples in the sample until we reach the end
5421		 * of the relation.  Same algorithm as in acquire_sample_rows in
5422		 * analyze.c; see Jeff Vitter's paper.
5423		 */ view japanese
5424		if (astate->rowstoskip < 0)
5425			astate->rowstoskip = reservoir_get_next_S(&astate->rstate, astate->samplerows, targrows);
5426
5427		if (astate->rowstoskip <= 0)
5428		{
5429			/* Choose a random reservoir element to replace. */ view japanese
5430			pos = (int) (targrows * sampler_random_fract(&astate->rstate.randstate));
5431			Assert(pos >= 0 && pos < targrows);
5432			heap_freetuple(astate->rows[pos]);
5433		}
5434		else
5435		{
5436			/* Skip this tuple. */ view japanese
5437			pos = -1;
5438		}
5439
5440		astate->rowstoskip -= 1;
5441	}
5442
5443	if (pos >= 0)
5444	{
5445		/*
5446		 * Create sample tuple from current result row, and store it in the
5447		 * position determined above.  The tuple has to be created in anl_cxt.
5448		 */ view japanese
5449		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(astate->anl_cxt);
5450
5451		astate->rows[pos] = make_tuple_from_result_row(res, row,
5452													   astate->rel,
5453													   astate->attinmeta,
5454													   astate->retrieved_attrs,
5455													   NULL,
5456													   astate->temp_cxt);
5457
5458		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
5459	}
5460}
5461
5462/*
5463 * Import a foreign schema
5464 */ view japanese
5465static List *
5466postgresImportForeignSchema(ImportForeignSchemaStmt *stmt, Oid serverOid)
5467{
5468	List	   *commands = NIL;
5469	bool		import_collate = true;
5470	bool		import_default = false;
5471	bool		import_generated = true;
5472	bool		import_not_null = true;
5473	ForeignServer *server;
5474	UserMapping *mapping;
5475	PGconn	   *conn;
5476	StringInfoData buf;
5477	PGresult   *volatile res = NULL;
5478	int			numrows,
5479				i;
5480	ListCell   *lc;
5481
5482	/* Parse statement options */ view japanese
5483	foreach(lc, stmt->options)
5484	{
5485		DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
5486
5487		if (strcmp(def->defname, "import_collate") == 0)
5488			import_collate = defGetBoolean(def);
5489		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "import_default") == 0)
5490			import_default = defGetBoolean(def);
5491		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "import_generated") == 0)
5492			import_generated = defGetBoolean(def);
5493		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "import_not_null") == 0)
5494			import_not_null = defGetBoolean(def);
5495		else
5496			ereport(ERROR,
5497					(errcode(ERRCODE_FDW_INVALID_OPTION_NAME),
5498					 errmsg("invalid option \"%s\"", def->defname)));
5499	}
5500
5501	/*
5502	 * Get connection to the foreign server.  Connection manager will
5503	 * establish new connection if necessary.
5504	 */ view japanese
5505	server = GetForeignServer(serverOid);
5506	mapping = GetUserMapping(GetUserId(), server->serverid);
5507	conn = GetConnection(mapping, false, NULL);
5508
5509	/* Don't attempt to import collation if remote server hasn't got it */ view japanese
5510	if (PQserverVersion(conn) < 90100)
5511		import_collate = false;
5512
5513	/* Create workspace for strings */ view japanese
5514	initStringInfo(&buf);
5515
5516	/* In what follows, do not risk leaking any PGresults. */ view japanese
5517	PG_TRY();
5518	{
5519		/* Check that the schema really exists */ view japanese
5520		appendStringInfoString(&buf, "SELECT 1 FROM pg_catalog.pg_namespace WHERE nspname = ");
5521		deparseStringLiteral(&buf, stmt->remote_schema);
5522
5523		res = pgfdw_exec_query(conn, buf.data, NULL);
5524		if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
5525			pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, false, buf.data);
5526
5527		if (PQntuples(res) != 1)
5528			ereport(ERROR,
5529					(errcode(ERRCODE_FDW_SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND),
5530					 errmsg("schema \"%s\" is not present on foreign server \"%s\"",
5531							stmt->remote_schema, server->servername)));
5532
5533		PQclear(res);
5534		res = NULL;
5535		resetStringInfo(&buf);
5536
5537		/*
5538		 * Fetch all table data from this schema, possibly restricted by
5539		 * EXCEPT or LIMIT TO.  (We don't actually need to pay any attention
5540		 * to EXCEPT/LIMIT TO here, because the core code will filter the
5541		 * statements we return according to those lists anyway.  But it
5542		 * should save a few cycles to not process excluded tables in the
5543		 * first place.)
5544		 *
5545		 * Import table data for partitions only when they are explicitly
5546		 * specified in LIMIT TO clause. Otherwise ignore them and only
5547		 * include the definitions of the root partitioned tables to allow
5548		 * access to the complete remote data set locally in the schema
5549		 * imported.
5550		 *
5551		 * Note: because we run the connection with search_path restricted to
5552		 * pg_catalog, the format_type() and pg_get_expr() outputs will always
5553		 * include a schema name for types/functions in other schemas, which
5554		 * is what we want.
5555		 */ view japanese
5556		appendStringInfoString(&buf,
5557							   "SELECT relname, "
5558							   "  attname, "
5559							   "  format_type(atttypid, atttypmod), "
5560							   "  attnotnull, "
5561							   "  pg_get_expr(adbin, adrelid), ");
5562
5563		/* Generated columns are supported since Postgres 12 */ view japanese
5564		if (PQserverVersion(conn) >= 120000)
5565			appendStringInfoString(&buf,
5566								   "  attgenerated, ");
5567		else
5568			appendStringInfoString(&buf,
5569								   "  NULL, ");
5570
5571		if (import_collate)
5572			appendStringInfoString(&buf,
5573								   "  collname, "
5574								   "  collnsp.nspname ");
5575		else
5576			appendStringInfoString(&buf,
5577								   "  NULL, NULL ");
5578
5579		appendStringInfoString(&buf,
5580							   "FROM pg_class c "
5581							   "  JOIN pg_namespace n ON "
5582							   "    relnamespace = n.oid "
5583							   "  LEFT JOIN pg_attribute a ON "
5584							   "    attrelid = c.oid AND attnum > 0 "
5585							   "      AND NOT attisdropped "
5586							   "  LEFT JOIN pg_attrdef ad ON "
5587							   "    adrelid = c.oid AND adnum = attnum ");
5588
5589		if (import_collate)
5590			appendStringInfoString(&buf,
5591								   "  LEFT JOIN pg_collation coll ON "
5592								   "    coll.oid = attcollation "
5593								   "  LEFT JOIN pg_namespace collnsp ON "
5594								   "    collnsp.oid = collnamespace ");
5595
5596		appendStringInfoString(&buf,
5597							   "WHERE c.relkind IN ("
5598							   CppAsString2(RELKIND_RELATION) ","
5599							   CppAsString2(RELKIND_VIEW) ","
5600							   CppAsString2(RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE) ","
5601							   CppAsString2(RELKIND_MATVIEW) ","
5602							   CppAsString2(RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE) ") "
5603							   "  AND n.nspname = ");
5604		deparseStringLiteral(&buf, stmt->remote_schema);
5605
5606		/* Partitions are supported since Postgres 10 */ view japanese
5607		if (PQserverVersion(conn) >= 100000 &&
5608			stmt->list_type != FDW_IMPORT_SCHEMA_LIMIT_TO)
5609			appendStringInfoString(&buf, " AND NOT c.relispartition ");
5610
5611		/* Apply restrictions for LIMIT TO and EXCEPT */ view japanese
5612		if (stmt->list_type == FDW_IMPORT_SCHEMA_LIMIT_TO ||
5613			stmt->list_type == FDW_IMPORT_SCHEMA_EXCEPT)
5614		{
5615			bool		first_item = true;
5616
5617			appendStringInfoString(&buf, " AND c.relname ");
5618			if (stmt->list_type == FDW_IMPORT_SCHEMA_EXCEPT)
5619				appendStringInfoString(&buf, "NOT ");
5620			appendStringInfoString(&buf, "IN (");
5621
5622			/* Append list of table names within IN clause */ view japanese
5623			foreach(lc, stmt->table_list)
5624			{
5625				RangeVar   *rv = (RangeVar *) lfirst(lc);
5626
5627				if (first_item)
5628					first_item = false;
5629				else
5630					appendStringInfoString(&buf, ", ");
5631				deparseStringLiteral(&buf, rv->relname);
5632			}
5633			appendStringInfoChar(&buf, ')');
5634		}
5635
5636		/* Append ORDER BY at the end of query to ensure output ordering */ view japanese
5637		appendStringInfoString(&buf, " ORDER BY c.relname, a.attnum");
5638
5639		/* Fetch the data */ view japanese
5640		res = pgfdw_exec_query(conn, buf.data, NULL);
5641		if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
5642			pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, res, conn, false, buf.data);
5643
5644		/* Process results */ view japanese
5645		numrows = PQntuples(res);
5646		/* note: incrementation of i happens in inner loop's while() test */ view japanese
5647		for (i = 0; i < numrows;)
5648		{
5649			char	   *tablename = PQgetvalue(res, i, 0);
5650			bool		first_item = true;
5651
5652			resetStringInfo(&buf);
5653			appendStringInfo(&buf, "CREATE FOREIGN TABLE %s (\n",
5654							 quote_identifier(tablename));
5655
5656			/* Scan all rows for this table */ view japanese
5657			do
5658			{
5659				char	   *attname;
5660				char	   *typename;
5661				char	   *attnotnull;
5662				char	   *attgenerated;
5663				char	   *attdefault;
5664				char	   *collname;
5665				char	   *collnamespace;
5666
5667				/* If table has no columns, we'll see nulls here */ view japanese
5668				if (PQgetisnull(res, i, 1))
5669					continue;
5670
5671				attname = PQgetvalue(res, i, 1);
5672				typename = PQgetvalue(res, i, 2);
5673				attnotnull = PQgetvalue(res, i, 3);
5674				attdefault = PQgetisnull(res, i, 4) ? NULL :
5675					PQgetvalue(res, i, 4);
5676				attgenerated = PQgetisnull(res, i, 5) ? NULL :
5677					PQgetvalue(res, i, 5);
5678				collname = PQgetisnull(res, i, 6) ? NULL :
5679					PQgetvalue(res, i, 6);
5680				collnamespace = PQgetisnull(res, i, 7) ? NULL :
5681					PQgetvalue(res, i, 7);
5682
5683				if (first_item)
5684					first_item = false;
5685				else
5686					appendStringInfoString(&buf, ",\n");
5687
5688				/* Print column name and type */ view japanese
5689				appendStringInfo(&buf, "  %s %s",
5690								 quote_identifier(attname),
5691								 typename);
5692
5693				/*
5694				 * Add column_name option so that renaming the foreign table's
5695				 * column doesn't break the association to the underlying
5696				 * column.
5697				 */ view japanese
5698				appendStringInfoString(&buf, " OPTIONS (column_name ");
5699				deparseStringLiteral(&buf, attname);
5700				appendStringInfoChar(&buf, ')');
5701
5702				/* Add COLLATE if needed */ view japanese
5703				if (import_collate && collname != NULL && collnamespace != NULL)
5704					appendStringInfo(&buf, " COLLATE %s.%s",
5705									 quote_identifier(collnamespace),
5706									 quote_identifier(collname));
5707
5708				/* Add DEFAULT if needed */ view japanese
5709				if (import_default && attdefault != NULL &&
5710					(!attgenerated || !attgenerated[0]))
5711					appendStringInfo(&buf, " DEFAULT %s", attdefault);
5712
5713				/* Add GENERATED if needed */ view japanese
5714				if (import_generated && attgenerated != NULL &&
5715					attgenerated[0] == ATTRIBUTE_GENERATED_STORED)
5716				{
5717					Assert(attdefault != NULL);
5718					appendStringInfo(&buf,
5719									 " GENERATED ALWAYS AS (%s) STORED",
5720									 attdefault);
5721				}
5722
5723				/* Add NOT NULL if needed */ view japanese
5724				if (import_not_null && attnotnull[0] == 't')
5725					appendStringInfoString(&buf, " NOT NULL");
5726			}
5727			while (++i < numrows &&
5728				   strcmp(PQgetvalue(res, i, 0), tablename) == 0);
5729
5730			/*
5731			 * Add server name and table-level options.  We specify remote
5732			 * schema and table name as options (the latter to ensure that
5733			 * renaming the foreign table doesn't break the association).
5734			 */ view japanese
5735			appendStringInfo(&buf, "\n) SERVER %s\nOPTIONS (",
5736							 quote_identifier(server->servername));
5737
5738			appendStringInfoString(&buf, "schema_name ");
5739			deparseStringLiteral(&buf, stmt->remote_schema);
5740			appendStringInfoString(&buf, ", table_name ");
5741			deparseStringLiteral(&buf, tablename);
5742
5743			appendStringInfoString(&buf, ");");
5744
5745			commands = lappend(commands, pstrdup(buf.data));
5746		}
5747	}
5748	PG_FINALLY();
5749	{
5750		PQclear(res);
5751	}
5752	PG_END_TRY();
5753
5754	ReleaseConnection(conn);
5755
5756	return commands;
5757}
5758
5759/*
5760 * Check if reltarget is safe enough to push down semi-join.  Reltarget is not
5761 * safe, if it contains references to inner rel relids, which do not belong to
5762 * outer rel.
5763 */ view japanese
5764static bool
5765semijoin_target_ok(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *joinrel, RelOptInfo *outerrel, RelOptInfo *innerrel)
5766{
5767	List	   *vars;
5768	ListCell   *lc;
5769	bool		ok = true;
5770
5771	Assert(joinrel->reltarget);
5772
5773	vars = pull_var_clause((Node *) joinrel->reltarget->exprs, PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
5774
5775	foreach(lc, vars)
5776	{
5777		Var		   *var = (Var *) lfirst(lc);
5778
5779		if (!IsA(var, Var))
5780			continue;
5781
5782		if (bms_is_member(var->varno, innerrel->relids))
5783		{
5784			/*
5785			 * The planner can create semi-join, which refers to inner rel
5786			 * vars in its target list. However, we deparse semi-join as an
5787			 * exists() subquery, so can't handle references to inner rel in
5788			 * the target list.
5789			 */ view japanese
5790			Assert(!bms_is_member(var->varno, outerrel->relids));
5791			ok = false;
5792			break;
5793		}
5794	}
5795	return ok;
5796}
5797
5798/*
5799 * Assess whether the join between inner and outer relations can be pushed down
5800 * to the foreign server. As a side effect, save information we obtain in this
5801 * function to PgFdwRelationInfo passed in.
5802 */ view japanese
5803static bool
5804foreign_join_ok(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *joinrel, JoinType jointype,
5805				RelOptInfo *outerrel, RelOptInfo *innerrel,
5806				JoinPathExtraData *extra)
5807{
5808	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo;
5809	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo_o;
5810	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo_i;
5811	ListCell   *lc;
5812	List	   *joinclauses;
5813
5814	/*
5815	 * We support pushing down INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER and SEMI joins.
5816	 * Constructing queries representing ANTI joins is hard, hence not
5817	 * considered right now.
5818	 */ view japanese
5819	if (jointype != JOIN_INNER && jointype != JOIN_LEFT &&
5820		jointype != JOIN_RIGHT && jointype != JOIN_FULL &&
5821		jointype != JOIN_SEMI)
5822		return false;
5823
5824	/*
5825	 * We can't push down semi-join if its reltarget is not safe
5826	 */ view japanese
5827	if ((jointype == JOIN_SEMI) && !semijoin_target_ok(root, joinrel, outerrel, innerrel))
5828		return false;
5829
5830	/*
5831	 * If either of the joining relations is marked as unsafe to pushdown, the
5832	 * join can not be pushed down.
5833	 */ view japanese
5834	fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) joinrel->fdw_private;
5835	fpinfo_o = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) outerrel->fdw_private;
5836	fpinfo_i = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) innerrel->fdw_private;
5837	if (!fpinfo_o || !fpinfo_o->pushdown_safe ||
5838		!fpinfo_i || !fpinfo_i->pushdown_safe)
5839		return false;
5840
5841	/*
5842	 * If joining relations have local conditions, those conditions are
5843	 * required to be applied before joining the relations. Hence the join can
5844	 * not be pushed down.
5845	 */ view japanese
5846	if (fpinfo_o->local_conds || fpinfo_i->local_conds)
5847		return false;
5848
5849	/*
5850	 * Merge FDW options.  We might be tempted to do this after we have deemed
5851	 * the foreign join to be OK.  But we must do this beforehand so that we
5852	 * know which quals can be evaluated on the foreign server, which might
5853	 * depend on shippable_extensions.
5854	 */ view japanese
5855	fpinfo->server = fpinfo_o->server;
5856	merge_fdw_options(fpinfo, fpinfo_o, fpinfo_i);
5857
5858	/*
5859	 * Separate restrict list into join quals and pushed-down (other) quals.
5860	 *
5861	 * Join quals belonging to an outer join must all be shippable, else we
5862	 * cannot execute the join remotely.  Add such quals to 'joinclauses'.
5863	 *
5864	 * Add other quals to fpinfo->remote_conds if they are shippable, else to
5865	 * fpinfo->local_conds.  In an inner join it's okay to execute conditions
5866	 * either locally or remotely; the same is true for pushed-down conditions
5867	 * at an outer join.
5868	 *
5869	 * Note we might return failure after having already scribbled on
5870	 * fpinfo->remote_conds and fpinfo->local_conds.  That's okay because we
5871	 * won't consult those lists again if we deem the join unshippable.
5872	 */ view japanese
5873	joinclauses = NIL;
5874	foreach(lc, extra->restrictlist)
5875	{
5876		RestrictInfo *rinfo = lfirst_node(RestrictInfo, lc);
5877		bool		is_remote_clause = is_foreign_expr(root, joinrel,
5878													   rinfo->clause);
5879
5880		if (IS_OUTER_JOIN(jointype) &&
5881			!RINFO_IS_PUSHED_DOWN(rinfo, joinrel->relids))
5882		{
5883			if (!is_remote_clause)
5884				return false;
5885			joinclauses = lappend(joinclauses, rinfo);
5886		}
5887		else
5888		{
5889			if (is_remote_clause)
5890				fpinfo->remote_conds = lappend(fpinfo->remote_conds, rinfo);
5891			else
5892				fpinfo->local_conds = lappend(fpinfo->local_conds, rinfo);
5893		}
5894	}
5895
5896	/*
5897	 * deparseExplicitTargetList() isn't smart enough to handle anything other
5898	 * than a Var.  In particular, if there's some PlaceHolderVar that would
5899	 * need to be evaluated within this join tree (because there's an upper
5900	 * reference to a quantity that may go to NULL as a result of an outer
5901	 * join), then we can't try to push the join down because we'll fail when
5902	 * we get to deparseExplicitTargetList().  However, a PlaceHolderVar that
5903	 * needs to be evaluated *at the top* of this join tree is OK, because we
5904	 * can do that locally after fetching the results from the remote side.
5905	 */ view japanese
5906	foreach(lc, root->placeholder_list)
5907	{
5908		PlaceHolderInfo *phinfo = lfirst(lc);
5909		Relids		relids;
5910
5911		/* PlaceHolderInfo refers to parent relids, not child relids. */ view japanese
5912		relids = IS_OTHER_REL(joinrel) ?
5913			joinrel->top_parent_relids : joinrel->relids;
5914
5915		if (bms_is_subset(phinfo->ph_eval_at, relids) &&
5916			bms_nonempty_difference(relids, phinfo->ph_eval_at))
5917			return false;
5918	}
5919
5920	/* Save the join clauses, for later use. */ view japanese
5921	fpinfo->joinclauses = joinclauses;
5922
5923	fpinfo->outerrel = outerrel;
5924	fpinfo->innerrel = innerrel;
5925	fpinfo->jointype = jointype;
5926
5927	/*
5928	 * By default, both the input relations are not required to be deparsed as
5929	 * subqueries, but there might be some relations covered by the input
5930	 * relations that are required to be deparsed as subqueries, so save the
5931	 * relids of those relations for later use by the deparser.
5932	 */ view japanese
5933	fpinfo->make_outerrel_subquery = false;
5934	fpinfo->make_innerrel_subquery = false;
5935	Assert(bms_is_subset(fpinfo_o->lower_subquery_rels, outerrel->relids));
5936	Assert(bms_is_subset(fpinfo_i->lower_subquery_rels, innerrel->relids));
5937	fpinfo->lower_subquery_rels = bms_union(fpinfo_o->lower_subquery_rels,
5938											fpinfo_i->lower_subquery_rels);
5939	fpinfo->hidden_subquery_rels = bms_union(fpinfo_o->hidden_subquery_rels,
5940											 fpinfo_i->hidden_subquery_rels);
5941
5942	/*
5943	 * Pull the other remote conditions from the joining relations into join
5944	 * clauses or other remote clauses (remote_conds) of this relation
5945	 * wherever possible. This avoids building subqueries at every join step.
5946	 *
5947	 * For an inner join, clauses from both the relations are added to the
5948	 * other remote clauses. For LEFT and RIGHT OUTER join, the clauses from
5949	 * the outer side are added to remote_conds since those can be evaluated
5950	 * after the join is evaluated. The clauses from inner side are added to
5951	 * the joinclauses, since they need to be evaluated while constructing the
5952	 * join.
5953	 *
5954	 * For SEMI-JOIN clauses from inner relation can not be added to
5955	 * remote_conds, but should be treated as join clauses (as they are
5956	 * deparsed to EXISTS subquery, where inner relation can be referred). A
5957	 * list of relation ids, which can't be referred to from higher levels, is
5958	 * preserved as a hidden_subquery_rels list.
5959	 *
5960	 * For a FULL OUTER JOIN, the other clauses from either relation can not
5961	 * be added to the joinclauses or remote_conds, since each relation acts
5962	 * as an outer relation for the other.
5963	 *
5964	 * The joining sides can not have local conditions, thus no need to test
5965	 * shippability of the clauses being pulled up.
5966	 */ view japanese
5967	switch (jointype)
5968	{
5969		case JOIN_INNER:
5970			fpinfo->remote_conds = list_concat(fpinfo->remote_conds,
5971											   fpinfo_i->remote_conds);
5972			fpinfo->remote_conds = list_concat(fpinfo->remote_conds,
5973											   fpinfo_o->remote_conds);
5974			break;
5975
5976		case JOIN_LEFT:
5977
5978			/*
5979			 * When semi-join is involved in the inner or outer part of the
5980			 * left join, it's deparsed as a subquery, and we can't refer to
5981			 * its vars on the upper level.
5982			 */ view japanese
5983			if (bms_is_empty(fpinfo_i->hidden_subquery_rels))
5984				fpinfo->joinclauses = list_concat(fpinfo->joinclauses,
5985												  fpinfo_i->remote_conds);
5986			if (bms_is_empty(fpinfo_o->hidden_subquery_rels))
5987				fpinfo->remote_conds = list_concat(fpinfo->remote_conds,
5988												   fpinfo_o->remote_conds);
5989			break;
5990
5991		case JOIN_RIGHT:
5992
5993			/*
5994			 * When semi-join is involved in the inner or outer part of the
5995			 * right join, it's deparsed as a subquery, and we can't refer to
5996			 * its vars on the upper level.
5997			 */ view japanese
5998			if (bms_is_empty(fpinfo_o->hidden_subquery_rels))
5999				fpinfo->joinclauses = list_concat(fpinfo->joinclauses,
6000												  fpinfo_o->remote_conds);
6001			if (bms_is_empty(fpinfo_i->hidden_subquery_rels))
6002				fpinfo->remote_conds = list_concat(fpinfo->remote_conds,
6003												   fpinfo_i->remote_conds);
6004			break;
6005
6006		case JOIN_SEMI:
6007			fpinfo->joinclauses = list_concat(fpinfo->joinclauses,
6008											  fpinfo_i->remote_conds);
6009			fpinfo->joinclauses = list_concat(fpinfo->joinclauses,
6010											  fpinfo->remote_conds);
6011			fpinfo->remote_conds = list_copy(fpinfo_o->remote_conds);
6012			fpinfo->hidden_subquery_rels = bms_union(fpinfo->hidden_subquery_rels,
6013													 innerrel->relids);
6014			break;
6015
6016		case JOIN_FULL:
6017
6018			/*
6019			 * In this case, if any of the input relations has conditions, we
6020			 * need to deparse that relation as a subquery so that the
6021			 * conditions can be evaluated before the join.  Remember it in
6022			 * the fpinfo of this relation so that the deparser can take
6023			 * appropriate action.  Also, save the relids of base relations
6024			 * covered by that relation for later use by the deparser.
6025			 */ view japanese
6026			if (fpinfo_o->remote_conds)
6027			{
6028				fpinfo->make_outerrel_subquery = true;
6029				fpinfo->lower_subquery_rels =
6030					bms_add_members(fpinfo->lower_subquery_rels,
6031									outerrel->relids);
6032			}
6033			if (fpinfo_i->remote_conds)
6034			{
6035				fpinfo->make_innerrel_subquery = true;
6036				fpinfo->lower_subquery_rels =
6037					bms_add_members(fpinfo->lower_subquery_rels,
6038									innerrel->relids);
6039			}
6040			break;
6041
6042		default:
6043			/* Should not happen, we have just checked this above */ view japanese
6044			elog(ERROR, "unsupported join type %d", jointype);
6045	}
6046
6047	/*
6048	 * For an inner join, all restrictions can be treated alike. Treating the
6049	 * pushed down conditions as join conditions allows a top level full outer
6050	 * join to be deparsed without requiring subqueries.
6051	 */ view japanese
6052	if (jointype == JOIN_INNER)
6053	{
6054		Assert(!fpinfo->joinclauses);
6055		fpinfo->joinclauses = fpinfo->remote_conds;
6056		fpinfo->remote_conds = NIL;
6057	}
6058	else if (jointype == JOIN_LEFT || jointype == JOIN_RIGHT || jointype == JOIN_FULL)
6059	{
6060		/*
6061		 * Conditions, generated from semi-joins, should be evaluated before
6062		 * LEFT/RIGHT/FULL join.
6063		 */ view japanese
6064		if (!bms_is_empty(fpinfo_o->hidden_subquery_rels))
6065		{
6066			fpinfo->make_outerrel_subquery = true;
6067			fpinfo->lower_subquery_rels = bms_add_members(fpinfo->lower_subquery_rels, outerrel->relids);
6068		}
6069
6070		if (!bms_is_empty(fpinfo_i->hidden_subquery_rels))
6071		{
6072			fpinfo->make_innerrel_subquery = true;
6073			fpinfo->lower_subquery_rels = bms_add_members(fpinfo->lower_subquery_rels, innerrel->relids);
6074		}
6075	}
6076
6077	/* Mark that this join can be pushed down safely */ view japanese
6078	fpinfo->pushdown_safe = true;
6079
6080	/* Get user mapping */ view japanese
6081	if (fpinfo->use_remote_estimate)
6082	{
6083		if (fpinfo_o->use_remote_estimate)
6084			fpinfo->user = fpinfo_o->user;
6085		else
6086			fpinfo->user = fpinfo_i->user;
6087	}
6088	else
6089		fpinfo->user = NULL;
6090
6091	/*
6092	 * Set # of retrieved rows and cached relation costs to some negative
6093	 * value, so that we can detect when they are set to some sensible values,
6094	 * during one (usually the first) of the calls to estimate_path_cost_size.
6095	 */
6096	fpinfo->retrieved_rows = -1;
6097	fpinfo->rel_startup_cost = -1;
6098	fpinfo->rel_total_cost = -1;
6099
6100	/*
6101	 * Set the string describing this join relation to be used in EXPLAIN
6102	 * output of corresponding ForeignScan.  Note that the decoration we add
6103	 * to the base relation names mustn't include any digits, or it'll confuse
6104	 * postgresExplainForeignScan.
6105	 */ view japanese
6106	fpinfo->relation_name = psprintf("(%s) %s JOIN (%s)",
6107									 fpinfo_o->relation_name,
6108									 get_jointype_name(fpinfo->jointype),
6109									 fpinfo_i->relation_name);
6110
6111	/*
6112	 * Set the relation index.  This is defined as the position of this
6113	 * joinrel in the join_rel_list list plus the length of the rtable list.
6114	 * Note that since this joinrel is at the end of the join_rel_list list
6115	 * when we are called, we can get the position by list_length.
6116	 */ view japanese
6117	Assert(fpinfo->relation_index == 0);	/* shouldn't be set yet */ view japanese
6118	fpinfo->relation_index =
6119		list_length(root->parse->rtable) + list_length(root->join_rel_list);
6120
6121	return true;
6122}
6123
6124static void
6125add_paths_with_pathkeys_for_rel(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *rel,
6126								Path *epq_path, List *restrictlist)
6127{
6128	List	   *useful_pathkeys_list = NIL; /* List of all pathkeys */ view japanese
6129	ListCell   *lc;
6130
6131	useful_pathkeys_list = get_useful_pathkeys_for_relation(root, rel);
6132
6133	/*
6134	 * Before creating sorted paths, arrange for the passed-in EPQ path, if
6135	 * any, to return columns needed by the parent ForeignScan node so that
6136	 * they will propagate up through Sort nodes injected below, if necessary.
6137	 */ view japanese
6138	if (epq_path != NULL && useful_pathkeys_list != NIL)
6139	{
6140		PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) rel->fdw_private;
6141		PathTarget *target = copy_pathtarget(epq_path->pathtarget);
6142
6143		/* Include columns required for evaluating PHVs in the tlist. */ view japanese
6144		add_new_columns_to_pathtarget(target,
6145									  pull_var_clause((Node *) target->exprs,
6146													  PVC_RECURSE_PLACEHOLDERS));
6147
6148		/* Include columns required for evaluating the local conditions. */ view japanese
6149		foreach(lc, fpinfo->local_conds)
6150		{
6151			RestrictInfo *rinfo = lfirst_node(RestrictInfo, lc);
6152
6153			add_new_columns_to_pathtarget(target,
6154										  pull_var_clause((Node *) rinfo->clause,
6155														  PVC_RECURSE_PLACEHOLDERS));
6156		}
6157
6158		/*
6159		 * If we have added any new columns, adjust the tlist of the EPQ path.
6160		 *
6161		 * Note: the plan created using this path will only be used to execute
6162		 * EPQ checks, where accuracy of the plan cost and width estimates
6163		 * would not be important, so we do not do set_pathtarget_cost_width()
6164		 * for the new pathtarget here.  See also postgresGetForeignPlan().
6165		 */ view japanese
6166		if (list_length(target->exprs) > list_length(epq_path->pathtarget->exprs))
6167		{
6168			/* The EPQ path is a join path, so it is projection-capable. */ view japanese
6169			Assert(is_projection_capable_path(epq_path));
6170
6171			/*
6172			 * Use create_projection_path() here, so as to avoid modifying it
6173			 * in place.
6174			 */ view japanese
6175			epq_path = (Path *) create_projection_path(root,
6176													   rel,
6177													   epq_path,
6178													   target);
6179		}
6180	}
6181
6182	/* Create one path for each set of pathkeys we found above. */ view japanese
6183	foreach(lc, useful_pathkeys_list)
6184	{
6185		double		rows;
6186		int			width;
6187		int			disabled_nodes;
6188		Cost		startup_cost;
6189		Cost		total_cost;
6190		List	   *useful_pathkeys = lfirst(lc);
6191		Path	   *sorted_epq_path;
6192
6193		estimate_path_cost_size(root, rel, NIL, useful_pathkeys, NULL,
6194								&rows, &width, &disabled_nodes,
6195								&startup_cost, &total_cost);
6196
6197		/*
6198		 * The EPQ path must be at least as well sorted as the path itself, in
6199		 * case it gets used as input to a mergejoin.
6200		 */ view japanese
6201		sorted_epq_path = epq_path;
6202		if (sorted_epq_path != NULL &&
6203			!pathkeys_contained_in(useful_pathkeys,
6204								   sorted_epq_path->pathkeys))
6205			sorted_epq_path = (Path *)
6206				create_sort_path(root,
6207								 rel,
6208								 sorted_epq_path,
6209								 useful_pathkeys,
6210								 -1.0);
6211
6212		if (IS_SIMPLE_REL(rel))
6213			add_path(rel, (Path *)
6214					 create_foreignscan_path(root, rel,
6215											 NULL,
6216											 rows,
6217											 disabled_nodes,
6218											 startup_cost,
6219											 total_cost,
6220											 useful_pathkeys,
6221											 rel->lateral_relids,
6222											 sorted_epq_path,
6223											 NIL,	/* no fdw_restrictinfo
6224													 * list */
6225											 NIL));
6226		else
6227			add_path(rel, (Path *)
6228					 create_foreign_join_path(root, rel,
6229											  NULL,
6230											  rows,
6231											  disabled_nodes,
6232											  startup_cost,
6233											  total_cost,
6234											  useful_pathkeys,
6235											  rel->lateral_relids,
6236											  sorted_epq_path,
6237											  restrictlist,
6238											  NIL));
6239	}
6240}
6241
6242/*
6243 * Parse options from foreign server and apply them to fpinfo.
6244 *
6245 * New options might also require tweaking merge_fdw_options().
6246 */ view japanese
6247static void
6248apply_server_options(PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo)
6249{
6250	ListCell   *lc;
6251
6252	foreach(lc, fpinfo->server->options)
6253	{
6254		DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
6255
6256		if (strcmp(def->defname, "use_remote_estimate") == 0)
6257			fpinfo->use_remote_estimate = defGetBoolean(def);
6258		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "fdw_startup_cost") == 0)
6259			(void) parse_real(defGetString(def), &fpinfo->fdw_startup_cost, 0,
6260							  NULL);
6261		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "fdw_tuple_cost") == 0)
6262			(void) parse_real(defGetString(def), &fpinfo->fdw_tuple_cost, 0,
6263							  NULL);
6264		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "extensions") == 0)
6265			fpinfo->shippable_extensions =
6266				ExtractExtensionList(defGetString(def), false);
6267		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "fetch_size") == 0)
6268			(void) parse_int(defGetString(def), &fpinfo->fetch_size, 0, NULL);
6269		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "async_capable") == 0)
6270			fpinfo->async_capable = defGetBoolean(def);
6271	}
6272}
6273
6274/*
6275 * Parse options from foreign table and apply them to fpinfo.
6276 *
6277 * New options might also require tweaking merge_fdw_options().
6278 */ view japanese
6279static void
6280apply_table_options(PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo)
6281{
6282	ListCell   *lc;
6283
6284	foreach(lc, fpinfo->table->options)
6285	{
6286		DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
6287
6288		if (strcmp(def->defname, "use_remote_estimate") == 0)
6289			fpinfo->use_remote_estimate = defGetBoolean(def);
6290		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "fetch_size") == 0)
6291			(void) parse_int(defGetString(def), &fpinfo->fetch_size, 0, NULL);
6292		else if (strcmp(def->defname, "async_capable") == 0)
6293			fpinfo->async_capable = defGetBoolean(def);
6294	}
6295}
6296
6297/*
6298 * Merge FDW options from input relations into a new set of options for a join
6299 * or an upper rel.
6300 *
6301 * For a join relation, FDW-specific information about the inner and outer
6302 * relations is provided using fpinfo_i and fpinfo_o.  For an upper relation,
6303 * fpinfo_o provides the information for the input relation; fpinfo_i is
6304 * expected to NULL.
6305 */ view japanese
6306static void
6307merge_fdw_options(PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo,
6308				  const PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo_o,
6309				  const PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo_i)
6310{
6311	/* We must always have fpinfo_o. */ view japanese
6312	Assert(fpinfo_o);
6313
6314	/* fpinfo_i may be NULL, but if present the servers must both match. */ view japanese
6315	Assert(!fpinfo_i ||
6316		   fpinfo_i->server->serverid == fpinfo_o->server->serverid);
6317
6318	/*
6319	 * Copy the server specific FDW options.  (For a join, both relations come
6320	 * from the same server, so the server options should have the same value
6321	 * for both relations.)
6322	 */ view japanese
6323	fpinfo->fdw_startup_cost = fpinfo_o->fdw_startup_cost;
6324	fpinfo->fdw_tuple_cost = fpinfo_o->fdw_tuple_cost;
6325	fpinfo->shippable_extensions = fpinfo_o->shippable_extensions;
6326	fpinfo->use_remote_estimate = fpinfo_o->use_remote_estimate;
6327	fpinfo->fetch_size = fpinfo_o->fetch_size;
6328	fpinfo->async_capable = fpinfo_o->async_capable;
6329
6330	/* Merge the table level options from either side of the join. */ view japanese
6331	if (fpinfo_i)
6332	{
6333		/*
6334		 * We'll prefer to use remote estimates for this join if any table
6335		 * from either side of the join is using remote estimates.  This is
6336		 * most likely going to be preferred since they're already willing to
6337		 * pay the price of a round trip to get the remote EXPLAIN.  In any
6338		 * case it's not entirely clear how we might otherwise handle this
6339		 * best.
6340		 */ view japanese
6341		fpinfo->use_remote_estimate = fpinfo_o->use_remote_estimate ||
6342			fpinfo_i->use_remote_estimate;
6343
6344		/*
6345		 * Set fetch size to maximum of the joining sides, since we are
6346		 * expecting the rows returned by the join to be proportional to the
6347		 * relation sizes.
6348		 */ view japanese
6349		fpinfo->fetch_size = Max(fpinfo_o->fetch_size, fpinfo_i->fetch_size);
6350
6351		/*
6352		 * We'll prefer to consider this join async-capable if any table from
6353		 * either side of the join is considered async-capable.  This would be
6354		 * reasonable because in that case the foreign server would have its
6355		 * own resources to scan that table asynchronously, and the join could
6356		 * also be computed asynchronously using the resources.
6357		 */ view japanese
6358		fpinfo->async_capable = fpinfo_o->async_capable ||
6359			fpinfo_i->async_capable;
6360	}
6361}
6362
6363/*
6364 * postgresGetForeignJoinPaths
6365 *		Add possible ForeignPath to joinrel, if join is safe to push down.
6366 */ view japanese
6367static void
6368postgresGetForeignJoinPaths(PlannerInfo *root,
6369							RelOptInfo *joinrel,
6370							RelOptInfo *outerrel,
6371							RelOptInfo *innerrel,
6372							JoinType jointype,
6373							JoinPathExtraData *extra)
6374{
6375	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo;
6376	ForeignPath *joinpath;
6377	double		rows;
6378	int			width;
6379	int			disabled_nodes;
6380	Cost		startup_cost;
6381	Cost		total_cost;
6382	Path	   *epq_path;		/* Path to create plan to be executed when
6383								 * EvalPlanQual gets triggered. */ view japanese
6384
6385	/*
6386	 * Skip if this join combination has been considered already.
6387	 */ view japanese
6388	if (joinrel->fdw_private)
6389		return;
6390
6391	/*
6392	 * This code does not work for joins with lateral references, since those
6393	 * must have parameterized paths, which we don't generate yet.
6394	 */ view japanese
6395	if (!bms_is_empty(joinrel->lateral_relids))
6396		return;
6397
6398	/*
6399	 * Create unfinished PgFdwRelationInfo entry which is used to indicate
6400	 * that the join relation is already considered, so that we won't waste
6401	 * time in judging safety of join pushdown and adding the same paths again
6402	 * if found safe. Once we know that this join can be pushed down, we fill
6403	 * the entry.
6404	 */ view japanese
6405	fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) palloc0(sizeof(PgFdwRelationInfo));
6406	fpinfo->pushdown_safe = false;
6407	joinrel->fdw_private = fpinfo;
6408	/* attrs_used is only for base relations. */ view japanese
6409	fpinfo->attrs_used = NULL;
6410
6411	/*
6412	 * If there is a possibility that EvalPlanQual will be executed, we need
6413	 * to be able to reconstruct the row using scans of the base relations.
6414	 * GetExistingLocalJoinPath will find a suitable path for this purpose in
6415	 * the path list of the joinrel, if one exists.  We must be careful to
6416	 * call it before adding any ForeignPath, since the ForeignPath might
6417	 * dominate the only suitable local path available.  We also do it before
6418	 * calling foreign_join_ok(), since that function updates fpinfo and marks
6419	 * it as pushable if the join is found to be pushable.
6420	 */ view japanese
6421	if (root->parse->commandType == CMD_DELETE ||
6422		root->parse->commandType == CMD_UPDATE ||
6423		root->rowMarks)
6424	{
6425		epq_path = GetExistingLocalJoinPath(joinrel);
6426		if (!epq_path)
6427		{
6428			elog(DEBUG3, "could not push down foreign join because a local path suitable for EPQ checks was not found");
6429			return;
6430		}
6431	}
6432	else
6433		epq_path = NULL;
6434
6435	if (!foreign_join_ok(root, joinrel, jointype, outerrel, innerrel, extra))
6436	{
6437		/* Free path required for EPQ if we copied one; we don't need it now */ view japanese
6438		if (epq_path)
6439			pfree(epq_path);
6440		return;
6441	}
6442
6443	/*
6444	 * Compute the selectivity and cost of the local_conds, so we don't have
6445	 * to do it over again for each path. The best we can do for these
6446	 * conditions is to estimate selectivity on the basis of local statistics.
6447	 * The local conditions are applied after the join has been computed on
6448	 * the remote side like quals in WHERE clause, so pass jointype as
6449	 * JOIN_INNER.
6450	 */ view japanese
6451	fpinfo->local_conds_sel = clauselist_selectivity(root,
6452													 fpinfo->local_conds,
6453													 0,
6454													 JOIN_INNER,
6455													 NULL);
6456	cost_qual_eval(&fpinfo->local_conds_cost, fpinfo->local_conds, root);
6457
6458	/*
6459	 * If we are going to estimate costs locally, estimate the join clause
6460	 * selectivity here while we have special join info.
6461	 */ view japanese
6462	if (!fpinfo->use_remote_estimate)
6463		fpinfo->joinclause_sel = clauselist_selectivity(root, fpinfo->joinclauses,
6464														0, fpinfo->jointype,
6465														extra->sjinfo);
6466
6467	/* Estimate costs for bare join relation */ view japanese
6468	estimate_path_cost_size(root, joinrel, NIL, NIL, NULL,
6469							&rows, &width, &disabled_nodes,
6470							&startup_cost, &total_cost);
6471	/* Now update this information in the joinrel */ view japanese
6472	joinrel->rows = rows;
6473	joinrel->reltarget->width = width;
6474	fpinfo->rows = rows;
6475	fpinfo->width = width;
6476	fpinfo->disabled_nodes = disabled_nodes;
6477	fpinfo->startup_cost = startup_cost;
6478	fpinfo->total_cost = total_cost;
6479
6480	/*
6481	 * Create a new join path and add it to the joinrel which represents a
6482	 * join between foreign tables.
6483	 */ view japanese
6484	joinpath = create_foreign_join_path(root,
6485										joinrel,
6486										NULL,	/* default pathtarget */ view japanese
6487										rows,
6488										disabled_nodes,
6489										startup_cost,
6490										total_cost,
6491										NIL,	/* no pathkeys */
6492										joinrel->lateral_relids,
6493										epq_path,
6494										extra->restrictlist,
6495										NIL);	/* no fdw_private */
6496
6497	/* Add generated path into joinrel by add_path(). */ view japanese
6498	add_path(joinrel, (Path *) joinpath);
6499
6500	/* Consider pathkeys for the join relation */ view japanese
6501	add_paths_with_pathkeys_for_rel(root, joinrel, epq_path,
6502									extra->restrictlist);
6503
6504	/* XXX Consider parameterized paths for the join relation */ view japanese
6505}
6506
6507/*
6508 * Assess whether the aggregation, grouping and having operations can be pushed
6509 * down to the foreign server.  As a side effect, save information we obtain in
6510 * this function to PgFdwRelationInfo of the input relation.
6511 */ view japanese
6512static bool
6513foreign_grouping_ok(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
6514					Node *havingQual)
6515{
6516	Query	   *query = root->parse;
6517	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) grouped_rel->fdw_private;
6518	PathTarget *grouping_target = grouped_rel->reltarget;
6519	PgFdwRelationInfo *ofpinfo;
6520	ListCell   *lc;
6521	int			i;
6522	List	   *tlist = NIL;
6523
6524	/* We currently don't support pushing Grouping Sets. */ view japanese
6525	if (query->groupingSets)
6526		return false;
6527
6528	/* Get the fpinfo of the underlying scan relation. */ view japanese
6529	ofpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) fpinfo->outerrel->fdw_private;
6530
6531	/*
6532	 * If underlying scan relation has any local conditions, those conditions
6533	 * are required to be applied before performing aggregation.  Hence the
6534	 * aggregate cannot be pushed down.
6535	 */ view japanese
6536	if (ofpinfo->local_conds)
6537		return false;
6538
6539	/*
6540	 * Examine grouping expressions, as well as other expressions we'd need to
6541	 * compute, and check whether they are safe to push down to the foreign
6542	 * server.  All GROUP BY expressions will be part of the grouping target
6543	 * and thus there is no need to search for them separately.  Add grouping
6544	 * expressions into target list which will be passed to foreign server.
6545	 *
6546	 * A tricky fine point is that we must not put any expression into the
6547	 * target list that is just a foreign param (that is, something that
6548	 * deparse.c would conclude has to be sent to the foreign server).  If we
6549	 * do, the expression will also appear in the fdw_exprs list of the plan
6550	 * node, and setrefs.c will get confused and decide that the fdw_exprs
6551	 * entry is actually a reference to the fdw_scan_tlist entry, resulting in
6552	 * a broken plan.  Somewhat oddly, it's OK if the expression contains such
6553	 * a node, as long as it's not at top level; then no match is possible.
6554	 */ view japanese
6555	i = 0;
6556	foreach(lc, grouping_target->exprs)
6557	{
6558		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
6559		Index		sgref = get_pathtarget_sortgroupref(grouping_target, i);
6560		ListCell   *l;
6561
6562		/*
6563		 * Check whether this expression is part of GROUP BY clause.  Note we
6564		 * check the whole GROUP BY clause not just processed_groupClause,
6565		 * because we will ship all of it, cf. appendGroupByClause.
6566		 */ view japanese
6567		if (sgref && get_sortgroupref_clause_noerr(sgref, query->groupClause))
6568		{
6569			TargetEntry *tle;
6570
6571			/*
6572			 * If any GROUP BY expression is not shippable, then we cannot
6573			 * push down aggregation to the foreign server.
6574			 */ view japanese
6575			if (!is_foreign_expr(root, grouped_rel, expr))
6576				return false;
6577
6578			/*
6579			 * If it would be a foreign param, we can't put it into the tlist,
6580			 * so we have to fail.
6581			 */ view japanese
6582			if (is_foreign_param(root, grouped_rel, expr))
6583				return false;
6584
6585			/*
6586			 * Pushable, so add to tlist.  We need to create a TLE for this
6587			 * expression and apply the sortgroupref to it.  We cannot use
6588			 * add_to_flat_tlist() here because that avoids making duplicate
6589			 * entries in the tlist.  If there are duplicate entries with
6590			 * distinct sortgrouprefs, we have to duplicate that situation in
6591			 * the output tlist.
6592			 */ view japanese
6593			tle = makeTargetEntry(expr, list_length(tlist) + 1, NULL, false);
6594			tle->ressortgroupref = sgref;
6595			tlist = lappend(tlist, tle);
6596		}
6597		else
6598		{
6599			/*
6600			 * Non-grouping expression we need to compute.  Can we ship it
6601			 * as-is to the foreign server?
6602			 */ view japanese
6603			if (is_foreign_expr(root, grouped_rel, expr) &&
6604				!is_foreign_param(root, grouped_rel, expr))
6605			{
6606				/* Yes, so add to tlist as-is; OK to suppress duplicates */ view japanese
6607				tlist = add_to_flat_tlist(tlist, list_make1(expr));
6608			}
6609			else
6610			{
6611				/* Not pushable as a whole; extract its Vars and aggregates */ view japanese
6612				List	   *aggvars;
6613
6614				aggvars = pull_var_clause((Node *) expr,
6615										  PVC_INCLUDE_AGGREGATES);
6616
6617				/*
6618				 * If any aggregate expression is not shippable, then we
6619				 * cannot push down aggregation to the foreign server.  (We
6620				 * don't have to check is_foreign_param, since that certainly
6621				 * won't return true for any such expression.)
6622				 */ view japanese
6623				if (!is_foreign_expr(root, grouped_rel, (Expr *) aggvars))
6624					return false;
6625
6626				/*
6627				 * Add aggregates, if any, into the targetlist.  Plain Vars
6628				 * outside an aggregate can be ignored, because they should be
6629				 * either same as some GROUP BY column or part of some GROUP
6630				 * BY expression.  In either case, they are already part of
6631				 * the targetlist and thus no need to add them again.  In fact
6632				 * including plain Vars in the tlist when they do not match a
6633				 * GROUP BY column would cause the foreign server to complain
6634				 * that the shipped query is invalid.
6635				 */ view japanese
6636				foreach(l, aggvars)
6637				{
6638					Expr	   *aggref = (Expr *) lfirst(l);
6639
6640					if (IsA(aggref, Aggref))
6641						tlist = add_to_flat_tlist(tlist, list_make1(aggref));
6642				}
6643			}
6644		}
6645
6646		i++;
6647	}
6648
6649	/*
6650	 * Classify the pushable and non-pushable HAVING clauses and save them in
6651	 * remote_conds and local_conds of the grouped rel's fpinfo.
6652	 */ view japanese
6653	if (havingQual)
6654	{
6655		foreach(lc, (List *) havingQual)
6656		{
6657			Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
6658			RestrictInfo *rinfo;
6659
6660			/*
6661			 * Currently, the core code doesn't wrap havingQuals in
6662			 * RestrictInfos, so we must make our own.
6663			 */ view japanese
6664			Assert(!IsA(expr, RestrictInfo));
6665			rinfo = make_restrictinfo(root,
6666									  expr,
6667									  true,
6668									  false,
6669									  false,
6670									  false,
6671									  root->qual_security_level,
6672									  grouped_rel->relids,
6673									  NULL,
6674									  NULL);
6675			if (is_foreign_expr(root, grouped_rel, expr))
6676				fpinfo->remote_conds = lappend(fpinfo->remote_conds, rinfo);
6677			else
6678				fpinfo->local_conds = lappend(fpinfo->local_conds, rinfo);
6679		}
6680	}
6681
6682	/*
6683	 * If there are any local conditions, pull Vars and aggregates from it and
6684	 * check whether they are safe to pushdown or not.
6685	 */ view japanese
6686	if (fpinfo->local_conds)
6687	{
6688		List	   *aggvars = NIL;
6689
6690		foreach(lc, fpinfo->local_conds)
6691		{
6692			RestrictInfo *rinfo = lfirst_node(RestrictInfo, lc);
6693
6694			aggvars = list_concat(aggvars,
6695								  pull_var_clause((Node *) rinfo->clause,
6696												  PVC_INCLUDE_AGGREGATES));
6697		}
6698
6699		foreach(lc, aggvars)
6700		{
6701			Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
6702
6703			/*
6704			 * If aggregates within local conditions are not safe to push
6705			 * down, then we cannot push down the query.  Vars are already
6706			 * part of GROUP BY clause which are checked above, so no need to
6707			 * access them again here.  Again, we need not check
6708			 * is_foreign_param for a foreign aggregate.
6709			 */ view japanese
6710			if (IsA(expr, Aggref))
6711			{
6712				if (!is_foreign_expr(root, grouped_rel, expr))
6713					return false;
6714
6715				tlist = add_to_flat_tlist(tlist, list_make1(expr));
6716			}
6717		}
6718	}
6719
6720	/* Store generated targetlist */ view japanese
6721	fpinfo->grouped_tlist = tlist;
6722
6723	/* Safe to pushdown */ view japanese
6724	fpinfo->pushdown_safe = true;
6725
6726	/*
6727	 * Set # of retrieved rows and cached relation costs to some negative
6728	 * value, so that we can detect when they are set to some sensible values,
6729	 * during one (usually the first) of the calls to estimate_path_cost_size.
6730	 */ view japanese
6731	fpinfo->retrieved_rows = -1;
6732	fpinfo->rel_startup_cost = -1;
6733	fpinfo->rel_total_cost = -1;
6734
6735	/*
6736	 * Set the string describing this grouped relation to be used in EXPLAIN
6737	 * output of corresponding ForeignScan.  Note that the decoration we add
6738	 * to the base relation name mustn't include any digits, or it'll confuse
6739	 * postgresExplainForeignScan.
6740	 */ view japanese
6741	fpinfo->relation_name = psprintf("Aggregate on (%s)",
6742									 ofpinfo->relation_name);
6743
6744	return true;
6745}
6746
6747/*
6748 * postgresGetForeignUpperPaths
6749 *		Add paths for post-join operations like aggregation, grouping etc. if
6750 *		corresponding operations are safe to push down.
6751 */ view japanese
6752static void
6753postgresGetForeignUpperPaths(PlannerInfo *root, UpperRelationKind stage,
6754							 RelOptInfo *input_rel, RelOptInfo *output_rel,
6755							 void *extra)
6756{
6757	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo;
6758
6759	/*
6760	 * If input rel is not safe to pushdown, then simply return as we cannot
6761	 * perform any post-join operations on the foreign server.
6762	 */ view japanese
6763	if (!input_rel->fdw_private ||
6764		!((PgFdwRelationInfo *) input_rel->fdw_private)->pushdown_safe)
6765		return;
6766
6767	/* Ignore stages we don't support; and skip any duplicate calls. */ view japanese
6768	if ((stage != UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG &&
6769		 stage != UPPERREL_ORDERED &&
6770		 stage != UPPERREL_FINAL) ||
6771		output_rel->fdw_private)
6772		return;
6773
6774	fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) palloc0(sizeof(PgFdwRelationInfo));
6775	fpinfo->pushdown_safe = false;
6776	fpinfo->stage = stage;
6777	output_rel->fdw_private = fpinfo;
6778
6779	switch (stage)
6780	{
6781		case UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG:
6782			add_foreign_grouping_paths(root, input_rel, output_rel,
6783									   (GroupPathExtraData *) extra);
6784			break;
6785		case UPPERREL_ORDERED:
6786			add_foreign_ordered_paths(root, input_rel, output_rel);
6787			break;
6788		case UPPERREL_FINAL:
6789			add_foreign_final_paths(root, input_rel, output_rel,
6790									(FinalPathExtraData *) extra);
6791			break;
6792		default:
6793			elog(ERROR, "unexpected upper relation: %d", (int) stage);
6794			break;
6795	}
6796}
6797
6798/*
6799 * add_foreign_grouping_paths
6800 *		Add foreign path for grouping and/or aggregation.
6801 *
6802 * Given input_rel represents the underlying scan.  The paths are added to the
6803 * given grouped_rel.
6804 */ view japanese
6805static void
6806add_foreign_grouping_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
6807						   RelOptInfo *grouped_rel,
6808						   GroupPathExtraData *extra)
6809{
6810	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
