6811	PgFdwRelationInfo *ifpinfo = input_rel->fdw_private;
6812	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo = grouped_rel->fdw_private;
6813	ForeignPath *grouppath;
6814	double		rows;
6815	int			width;
6816	int			disabled_nodes;
6817	Cost		startup_cost;
6818	Cost		total_cost;
6819
6820	/* Nothing to be done, if there is no grouping or aggregation required. */ view japanese
6821	if (!parse->groupClause && !parse->groupingSets && !parse->hasAggs &&
6822		!root->hasHavingQual)
6823		return;
6824
6825	Assert(extra->patype == PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_NONE ||
6826		   extra->patype == PARTITIONWISE_AGGREGATE_FULL);
6827
6828	/* save the input_rel as outerrel in fpinfo */ view japanese
6829	fpinfo->outerrel = input_rel;
6830
6831	/*
6832	 * Copy foreign table, foreign server, user mapping, FDW options etc.
6833	 * details from the input relation's fpinfo.
6834	 */
6835	fpinfo->table = ifpinfo->table;
6836	fpinfo->server = ifpinfo->server;
6837	fpinfo->user = ifpinfo->user;
6838	merge_fdw_options(fpinfo, ifpinfo, NULL);
6839
6840	/*
6841	 * Assess if it is safe to push down aggregation and grouping.
6842	 *
6843	 * Use HAVING qual from extra. In case of child partition, it will have
6844	 * translated Vars.
6845	 */ view japanese
6846	if (!foreign_grouping_ok(root, grouped_rel, extra->havingQual))
6847		return;
6848
6849	/*
6850	 * Compute the selectivity and cost of the local_conds, so we don't have
6851	 * to do it over again for each path.  (Currently we create just a single
6852	 * path here, but in future it would be possible that we build more paths
6853	 * such as pre-sorted paths as in postgresGetForeignPaths and
6854	 * postgresGetForeignJoinPaths.)  The best we can do for these conditions
6855	 * is to estimate selectivity on the basis of local statistics.
6856	 */ view japanese
6857	fpinfo->local_conds_sel = clauselist_selectivity(root,
6858													 fpinfo->local_conds,
6859													 0,
6860													 JOIN_INNER,
6861													 NULL);
6862
6863	cost_qual_eval(&fpinfo->local_conds_cost, fpinfo->local_conds, root);
6864
6865	/* Estimate the cost of push down */ view japanese
6866	estimate_path_cost_size(root, grouped_rel, NIL, NIL, NULL,
6867							&rows, &width, &disabled_nodes,
6868							&startup_cost, &total_cost);
6869
6870	/* Now update this information in the fpinfo */ view japanese
6871	fpinfo->rows = rows;
6872	fpinfo->width = width;
6873	fpinfo->disabled_nodes = disabled_nodes;
6874	fpinfo->startup_cost = startup_cost;
6875	fpinfo->total_cost = total_cost;
6876
6877	/* Create and add foreign path to the grouping relation. */ view japanese
6878	grouppath = create_foreign_upper_path(root,
6879										  grouped_rel,
6880										  grouped_rel->reltarget,
6881										  rows,
6882										  disabled_nodes,
6883										  startup_cost,
6884										  total_cost,
6885										  NIL,	/* no pathkeys */ view japanese
6886										  NULL,
6887										  NIL,	/* no fdw_restrictinfo list */
6888										  NIL); /* no fdw_private */
6889
6890	/* Add generated path into grouped_rel by add_path(). */ view japanese
6891	add_path(grouped_rel, (Path *) grouppath);
6892}
6893
6894/*
6895 * add_foreign_ordered_paths
6896 *		Add foreign paths for performing the final sort remotely.
6897 *
6898 * Given input_rel contains the source-data Paths.  The paths are added to the
6899 * given ordered_rel.
6900 */ view japanese
6901static void
6902add_foreign_ordered_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
6903						  RelOptInfo *ordered_rel)
6904{
6905	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
6906	PgFdwRelationInfo *ifpinfo = input_rel->fdw_private;
6907	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo = ordered_rel->fdw_private;
6908	PgFdwPathExtraData *fpextra;
6909	double		rows;
6910	int			width;
6911	int			disabled_nodes;
6912	Cost		startup_cost;
6913	Cost		total_cost;
6914	List	   *fdw_private;
6915	ForeignPath *ordered_path;
6916	ListCell   *lc;
6917
6918	/* Shouldn't get here unless the query has ORDER BY */ view japanese
6919	Assert(parse->sortClause);
6920
6921	/* We don't support cases where there are any SRFs in the targetlist */
6922	if (parse->hasTargetSRFs)
6923		return;
6924
6925	/* Save the input_rel as outerrel in fpinfo */
6926	fpinfo->outerrel = input_rel;
6927
6928	/*
6929	 * Copy foreign table, foreign server, user mapping, FDW options etc.
6930	 * details from the input relation's fpinfo.
6931	 */
6932	fpinfo->table = ifpinfo->table;
6933	fpinfo->server = ifpinfo->server;
6934	fpinfo->user = ifpinfo->user;
6935	merge_fdw_options(fpinfo, ifpinfo, NULL);
6936
6937	/*
6938	 * If the input_rel is a base or join relation, we would already have
6939	 * considered pushing down the final sort to the remote server when
6940	 * creating pre-sorted foreign paths for that relation, because the
6941	 * query_pathkeys is set to the root->sort_pathkeys in that case (see
6942	 * standard_qp_callback()).
6943	 */ view japanese
6944	if (input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_BASEREL ||
6945		input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_JOINREL)
6946	{
6947		Assert(root->query_pathkeys == root->sort_pathkeys);
6948
6949		/* Safe to push down if the query_pathkeys is safe to push down */ view japanese
6950		fpinfo->pushdown_safe = ifpinfo->qp_is_pushdown_safe;
6951
6952		return;
6953	}
6954
6955	/* The input_rel should be a grouping relation */ view japanese
6956	Assert(input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_UPPER_REL &&
6957		   ifpinfo->stage == UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG);
6958
6959	/*
6960	 * We try to create a path below by extending a simple foreign path for
6961	 * the underlying grouping relation to perform the final sort remotely,
6962	 * which is stored into the fdw_private list of the resulting path.
6963	 */ view japanese
6964
6965	/* Assess if it is safe to push down the final sort */ view japanese
6966	foreach(lc, root->sort_pathkeys)
6967	{
6968		PathKey    *pathkey = (PathKey *) lfirst(lc);
6969		EquivalenceClass *pathkey_ec = pathkey->pk_eclass;
6970
6971		/*
6972		 * is_foreign_expr would detect volatile expressions as well, but
6973		 * checking ec_has_volatile here saves some cycles.
6974		 */ view japanese
6975		if (pathkey_ec->ec_has_volatile)
6976			return;
6977
6978		/*
6979		 * Can't push down the sort if pathkey's opfamily is not shippable.
6980		 */ view japanese
6981		if (!is_shippable(pathkey->pk_opfamily, OperatorFamilyRelationId,
6982						  fpinfo))
6983			return;
6984
6985		/*
6986		 * The EC must contain a shippable EM that is computed in input_rel's
6987		 * reltarget, else we can't push down the sort.
6988		 */ view japanese
6989		if (find_em_for_rel_target(root,
6990								   pathkey_ec,
6991								   input_rel) == NULL)
6992			return;
6993	}
6994
6995	/* Safe to push down */
6996	fpinfo->pushdown_safe = true;
6997
6998	/* Construct PgFdwPathExtraData */
6999	fpextra = (PgFdwPathExtraData *) palloc0(sizeof(PgFdwPathExtraData));
7000	fpextra->target = root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_ORDERED];
7001	fpextra->has_final_sort = true;
7002
7003	/* Estimate the costs of performing the final sort remotely */ view japanese
7004	estimate_path_cost_size(root, input_rel, NIL, root->sort_pathkeys, fpextra,
7005							&rows, &width, &disabled_nodes,
7006							&startup_cost, &total_cost);
7007
7008	/*
7009	 * Build the fdw_private list that will be used by postgresGetForeignPlan.
7010	 * Items in the list must match order in enum FdwPathPrivateIndex.
7011	 */
7012	fdw_private = list_make2(makeBoolean(true), makeBoolean(false));
7013
7014	/* Create foreign ordering path */ view japanese
7015	ordered_path = create_foreign_upper_path(root,
7016											 input_rel,
7017											 root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_ORDERED],
7018											 rows,
7019											 disabled_nodes,
7020											 startup_cost,
7021											 total_cost,
7022											 root->sort_pathkeys,
7023											 NULL,	/* no extra plan */
7024											 NIL,	/* no fdw_restrictinfo
7025													 * list */ view japanese
7026											 fdw_private);
7027
7028	/* and add it to the ordered_rel */ view japanese
7029	add_path(ordered_rel, (Path *) ordered_path);
7030}
7031
7032/*
7033 * add_foreign_final_paths
7034 *		Add foreign paths for performing the final processing remotely.
7035 *
7036 * Given input_rel contains the source-data Paths.  The paths are added to the
7037 * given final_rel.
7038 */ view japanese
7039static void
7040add_foreign_final_paths(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *input_rel,
7041						RelOptInfo *final_rel,
7042						FinalPathExtraData *extra)
7043{
7044	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
7045	PgFdwRelationInfo *ifpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) input_rel->fdw_private;
7046	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) final_rel->fdw_private;
7047	bool		has_final_sort = false;
7048	List	   *pathkeys = NIL;
7049	PgFdwPathExtraData *fpextra;
7050	bool		save_use_remote_estimate = false;
7051	double		rows;
7052	int			width;
7053	int			disabled_nodes;
7054	Cost		startup_cost;
7055	Cost		total_cost;
7056	List	   *fdw_private;
7057	ForeignPath *final_path;
7058
7059	/*
7060	 * Currently, we only support this for SELECT commands
7061	 */ view japanese
7062	if (parse->commandType != CMD_SELECT)
7063		return;
7064
7065	/*
7066	 * No work if there is no FOR UPDATE/SHARE clause and if there is no need
7067	 * to add a LIMIT node
7068	 */ view japanese
7069	if (!parse->rowMarks && !extra->limit_needed)
7070		return;
7071
7072	/* We don't support cases where there are any SRFs in the targetlist */ view japanese
7073	if (parse->hasTargetSRFs)
7074		return;
7075
7076	/* Save the input_rel as outerrel in fpinfo */ view japanese
7077	fpinfo->outerrel = input_rel;
7078
7079	/*
7080	 * Copy foreign table, foreign server, user mapping, FDW options etc.
7081	 * details from the input relation's fpinfo.
7082	 */ view japanese
7083	fpinfo->table = ifpinfo->table;
7084	fpinfo->server = ifpinfo->server;
7085	fpinfo->user = ifpinfo->user;
7086	merge_fdw_options(fpinfo, ifpinfo, NULL);
7087
7088	/*
7089	 * If there is no need to add a LIMIT node, there might be a ForeignPath
7090	 * in the input_rel's pathlist that implements all behavior of the query.
7091	 * Note: we would already have accounted for the query's FOR UPDATE/SHARE
7092	 * (if any) before we get here.
7093	 */ view japanese
7094	if (!extra->limit_needed)
7095	{
7096		ListCell   *lc;
7097
7098		Assert(parse->rowMarks);
7099
7100		/*
7101		 * Grouping and aggregation are not supported with FOR UPDATE/SHARE,
7102		 * so the input_rel should be a base, join, or ordered relation; and
7103		 * if it's an ordered relation, its input relation should be a base or
7104		 * join relation.
7105		 */ view japanese
7106		Assert(input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_BASEREL ||
7107			   input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_JOINREL ||
7108			   (input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_UPPER_REL &&
7109				ifpinfo->stage == UPPERREL_ORDERED &&
7110				(ifpinfo->outerrel->reloptkind == RELOPT_BASEREL ||
7111				 ifpinfo->outerrel->reloptkind == RELOPT_JOINREL)));
7112
7113		foreach(lc, input_rel->pathlist)
7114		{
7115			Path	   *path = (Path *) lfirst(lc);
7116
7117			/*
7118			 * apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths() uses create_projection_path()
7119			 * to adjust each of its input paths if needed, whereas
7120			 * create_ordered_paths() uses apply_projection_to_path() to do
7121			 * that.  So the former might have put a ProjectionPath on top of
7122			 * the ForeignPath; look through ProjectionPath and see if the
7123			 * path underneath it is ForeignPath.
7124			 */ view japanese
7125			if (IsA(path, ForeignPath) ||
7126				(IsA(path, ProjectionPath) &&
7127				 IsA(((ProjectionPath *) path)->subpath, ForeignPath)))
7128			{
7129				/*
7130				 * Create foreign final path; this gets rid of a
7131				 * no-longer-needed outer plan (if any), which makes the
7132				 * EXPLAIN output look cleaner
7133				 */ view japanese
7134				final_path = create_foreign_upper_path(root,
7135													   path->parent,
7136													   path->pathtarget,
7137													   path->rows,
7138													   path->disabled_nodes,
7139													   path->startup_cost,
7140													   path->total_cost,
7141													   path->pathkeys,
7142													   NULL,	/* no extra plan */
7143													   NIL, /* no fdw_restrictinfo
7144															 * list */ view japanese
7145													   NIL);	/* no fdw_private */ view japanese
7146
7147				/* and add it to the final_rel */
7148				add_path(final_rel, (Path *) final_path);
7149
7150				/* Safe to push down */
7151				fpinfo->pushdown_safe = true;
7152
7153				return;
7154			}
7155		}
7156
7157		/*
7158		 * If we get here it means no ForeignPaths; since we would already
7159		 * have considered pushing down all operations for the query to the
7160		 * remote server, give up on it.
7161		 */ view japanese
7162		return;
7163	}
7164
7165	Assert(extra->limit_needed);
7166
7167	/*
7168	 * If the input_rel is an ordered relation, replace the input_rel with its
7169	 * input relation
7170	 */ view japanese
7171	if (input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_UPPER_REL &&
7172		ifpinfo->stage == UPPERREL_ORDERED)
7173	{
7174		input_rel = ifpinfo->outerrel;
7175		ifpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) input_rel->fdw_private;
7176		has_final_sort = true;
7177		pathkeys = root->sort_pathkeys;
7178	}
7179
7180	/* The input_rel should be a base, join, or grouping relation */ view japanese
7181	Assert(input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_BASEREL ||
7182		   input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_JOINREL ||
7183		   (input_rel->reloptkind == RELOPT_UPPER_REL &&
7184			ifpinfo->stage == UPPERREL_GROUP_AGG));
7185
7186	/*
7187	 * We try to create a path below by extending a simple foreign path for
7188	 * the underlying base, join, or grouping relation to perform the final
7189	 * sort (if has_final_sort) and the LIMIT restriction remotely, which is
7190	 * stored into the fdw_private list of the resulting path.  (We
7191	 * re-estimate the costs of sorting the underlying relation, if
7192	 * has_final_sort.)
7193	 */ view japanese
7194
7195	/*
7196	 * Assess if it is safe to push down the LIMIT and OFFSET to the remote
7197	 * server
7198	 */ view japanese
7199
7200	/*
7201	 * If the underlying relation has any local conditions, the LIMIT/OFFSET
7202	 * cannot be pushed down.
7203	 */ view japanese
7204	if (ifpinfo->local_conds)
7205		return;
7206
7207	/*
7208	 * If the query has FETCH FIRST .. WITH TIES, 1) it must have ORDER BY as
7209	 * well, which is used to determine which additional rows tie for the last
7210	 * place in the result set, and 2) ORDER BY must already have been
7211	 * determined to be safe to push down before we get here.  So in that case
7212	 * the FETCH clause is safe to push down with ORDER BY if the remote
7213	 * server is v13 or later, but if not, the remote query will fail entirely
7214	 * for lack of support for it.  Since we do not currently have a way to do
7215	 * a remote-version check (without accessing the remote server), disable
7216	 * pushing the FETCH clause for now.
7217	 */ view japanese
7218	if (parse->limitOption == LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES)
7219		return;
7220
7221	/*
7222	 * Also, the LIMIT/OFFSET cannot be pushed down, if their expressions are
7223	 * not safe to remote.
7224	 */ view japanese
7225	if (!is_foreign_expr(root, input_rel, (Expr *) parse->limitOffset) ||
7226		!is_foreign_expr(root, input_rel, (Expr *) parse->limitCount))
7227		return;
7228
7229	/* Safe to push down */ view japanese
7230	fpinfo->pushdown_safe = true;
7231
7232	/* Construct PgFdwPathExtraData */ view japanese
7233	fpextra = (PgFdwPathExtraData *) palloc0(sizeof(PgFdwPathExtraData));
7234	fpextra->target = root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_FINAL];
7235	fpextra->has_final_sort = has_final_sort;
7236	fpextra->has_limit = extra->limit_needed;
7237	fpextra->limit_tuples = extra->limit_tuples;
7238	fpextra->count_est = extra->count_est;
7239	fpextra->offset_est = extra->offset_est;
7240
7241	/*
7242	 * Estimate the costs of performing the final sort and the LIMIT
7243	 * restriction remotely.  If has_final_sort is false, we wouldn't need to
7244	 * execute EXPLAIN anymore if use_remote_estimate, since the costs can be
7245	 * roughly estimated using the costs we already have for the underlying
7246	 * relation, in the same way as when use_remote_estimate is false.  Since
7247	 * it's pretty expensive to execute EXPLAIN, force use_remote_estimate to
7248	 * false in that case.
7249	 */ view japanese
7250	if (!fpextra->has_final_sort)
7251	{
7252		save_use_remote_estimate = ifpinfo->use_remote_estimate;
7253		ifpinfo->use_remote_estimate = false;
7254	}
7255	estimate_path_cost_size(root, input_rel, NIL, pathkeys, fpextra,
7256							&rows, &width, &disabled_nodes,
7257							&startup_cost, &total_cost);
7258	if (!fpextra->has_final_sort)
7259		ifpinfo->use_remote_estimate = save_use_remote_estimate;
7260
7261	/*
7262	 * Build the fdw_private list that will be used by postgresGetForeignPlan.
7263	 * Items in the list must match order in enum FdwPathPrivateIndex.
7264	 */ view japanese
7265	fdw_private = list_make2(makeBoolean(has_final_sort),
7266							 makeBoolean(extra->limit_needed));
7267
7268	/*
7269	 * Create foreign final path; this gets rid of a no-longer-needed outer
7270	 * plan (if any), which makes the EXPLAIN output look cleaner
7271	 */ view japanese
7272	final_path = create_foreign_upper_path(root,
7273										   input_rel,
7274										   root->upper_targets[UPPERREL_FINAL],
7275										   rows,
7276										   disabled_nodes,
7277										   startup_cost,
7278										   total_cost,
7279										   pathkeys,
7280										   NULL,	/* no extra plan */ view japanese
7281										   NIL, /* no fdw_restrictinfo list */ view japanese
7282										   fdw_private);
7283
7284	/* and add it to the final_rel */ view japanese
7285	add_path(final_rel, (Path *) final_path);
7286}
7287
7288/*
7289 * postgresIsForeignPathAsyncCapable
7290 *		Check whether a given ForeignPath node is async-capable.
7291 */ view japanese
7292static bool
7293postgresIsForeignPathAsyncCapable(ForeignPath *path)
7294{
7295	RelOptInfo *rel = ((Path *) path)->parent;
7296	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) rel->fdw_private;
7297
7298	return fpinfo->async_capable;
7299}
7300
7301/*
7302 * postgresForeignAsyncRequest
7303 *		Asynchronously request next tuple from a foreign PostgreSQL table.
7304 */ view japanese
7305static void
7306postgresForeignAsyncRequest(AsyncRequest *areq)
7307{
7308	produce_tuple_asynchronously(areq, true);
7309}
7310
7311/*
7312 * postgresForeignAsyncConfigureWait
7313 *		Configure a file descriptor event for which we wish to wait.
7314 */ view japanese
7315static void
7316postgresForeignAsyncConfigureWait(AsyncRequest *areq)
7317{
7318	ForeignScanState *node = (ForeignScanState *) areq->requestee;
7319	PgFdwScanState *fsstate = (PgFdwScanState *) node->fdw_state;
7320	AsyncRequest *pendingAreq = fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq;
7321	AppendState *requestor = (AppendState *) areq->requestor;
7322	WaitEventSet *set = requestor->as_eventset;
7323
7324	/* This should not be called unless callback_pending */ view japanese
7325	Assert(areq->callback_pending);
7326
7327	/*
7328	 * If process_pending_request() has been invoked on the given request
7329	 * before we get here, we might have some tuples already; in which case
7330	 * complete the request
7331	 */ view japanese
7332	if (fsstate->next_tuple < fsstate->num_tuples)
7333	{
7334		complete_pending_request(areq);
7335		if (areq->request_complete)
7336			return;
7337		Assert(areq->callback_pending);
7338	}
7339
7340	/* We must have run out of tuples */
7341	Assert(fsstate->next_tuple >= fsstate->num_tuples);
7342
7343	/* The core code would have registered postmaster death event */ view japanese
7344	Assert(GetNumRegisteredWaitEvents(set) >= 1);
7345
7346	/* Begin an asynchronous data fetch if not already done */ view japanese
7347	if (!pendingAreq)
7348		fetch_more_data_begin(areq);
7349	else if (pendingAreq->requestor != areq->requestor)
7350	{
7351		/*
7352		 * This is the case when the in-process request was made by another
7353		 * Append.  Note that it might be useless to process the request made
7354		 * by that Append, because the query might not need tuples from that
7355		 * Append anymore; so we avoid processing it to begin a fetch for the
7356		 * given request if possible.  If there are any child subplans of the
7357		 * same parent that are ready for new requests, skip the given
7358		 * request.  Likewise, if there are any configured events other than
7359		 * the postmaster death event, skip it.  Otherwise, process the
7360		 * in-process request, then begin a fetch to configure the event
7361		 * below, because we might otherwise end up with no configured events
7362		 * other than the postmaster death event.
7363		 */ view japanese
7364		if (!bms_is_empty(requestor->as_needrequest))
7365			return;
7366		if (GetNumRegisteredWaitEvents(set) > 1)
7367			return;
7368		process_pending_request(pendingAreq);
7369		fetch_more_data_begin(areq);
7370	}
7371	else if (pendingAreq->requestee != areq->requestee)
7372	{
7373		/*
7374		 * This is the case when the in-process request was made by the same
7375		 * parent but for a different child.  Since we configure only the
7376		 * event for the request made for that child, skip the given request.
7377		 */ view japanese
7378		return;
7379	}
7380	else
7381		Assert(pendingAreq == areq);
7382
7383	AddWaitEventToSet(set, WL_SOCKET_READABLE, PQsocket(fsstate->conn),
7384					  NULL, areq);
7385}
7386
7387/*
7388 * postgresForeignAsyncNotify
7389 *		Fetch some more tuples from a file descriptor that becomes ready,
7390 *		requesting next tuple.
7391 */ view japanese
7392static void
7393postgresForeignAsyncNotify(AsyncRequest *areq)
7394{
7395	ForeignScanState *node = (ForeignScanState *) areq->requestee;
7396	PgFdwScanState *fsstate = (PgFdwScanState *) node->fdw_state;
7397
7398	/* The core code would have initialized the callback_pending flag */ view japanese
7399	Assert(!areq->callback_pending);
7400
7401	/*
7402	 * If process_pending_request() has been invoked on the given request
7403	 * before we get here, we might have some tuples already; in which case
7404	 * produce the next tuple
7405	 */ view japanese
7406	if (fsstate->next_tuple < fsstate->num_tuples)
7407	{
7408		produce_tuple_asynchronously(areq, true);
7409		return;
7410	}
7411
7412	/* We must have run out of tuples */
7413	Assert(fsstate->next_tuple >= fsstate->num_tuples);
7414
7415	/* The request should be currently in-process */
7416	Assert(fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq == areq);
7417
7418	/* On error, report the original query, not the FETCH. */ view japanese
7419	if (!PQconsumeInput(fsstate->conn))
7420		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, NULL, fsstate->conn, false, fsstate->query);
7421
7422	fetch_more_data(node);
7423
7424	produce_tuple_asynchronously(areq, true);
7425}
7426
7427/*
7428 * Asynchronously produce next tuple from a foreign PostgreSQL table.
7429 */ view japanese
7430static void
7431produce_tuple_asynchronously(AsyncRequest *areq, bool fetch)
7432{
7433	ForeignScanState *node = (ForeignScanState *) areq->requestee;
7434	PgFdwScanState *fsstate = (PgFdwScanState *) node->fdw_state;
7435	AsyncRequest *pendingAreq = fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq;
7436	TupleTableSlot *result;
7437
7438	/* This should not be called if the request is currently in-process */ view japanese
7439	Assert(areq != pendingAreq);
7440
7441	/* Fetch some more tuples, if we've run out */ view japanese
7442	if (fsstate->next_tuple >= fsstate->num_tuples)
7443	{
7444		/* No point in another fetch if we already detected EOF, though */ view japanese
7445		if (!fsstate->eof_reached)
7446		{
7447			/* Mark the request as pending for a callback */
7448			ExecAsyncRequestPending(areq);
7449			/* Begin another fetch if requested and if no pending request */
7450			if (fetch && !pendingAreq)
7451				fetch_more_data_begin(areq);
7452		}
7453		else
7454		{
7455			/* There's nothing more to do; just return a NULL pointer */
7456			result = NULL;
7457			/* Mark the request as complete */
7458			ExecAsyncRequestDone(areq, result);
7459		}
7460		return;
7461	}
7462
7463	/* Get a tuple from the ForeignScan node */ view japanese
7464	result = areq->requestee->ExecProcNodeReal(areq->requestee);
7465	if (!TupIsNull(result))
7466	{
7467		/* Mark the request as complete */
7468		ExecAsyncRequestDone(areq, result);
7469		return;
7470	}
7471
7472	/* We must have run out of tuples */ view japanese
7473	Assert(fsstate->next_tuple >= fsstate->num_tuples);
7474
7475	/* Fetch some more tuples, if we've not detected EOF yet */ view japanese
7476	if (!fsstate->eof_reached)
7477	{
7478		/* Mark the request as pending for a callback */ view japanese
7479		ExecAsyncRequestPending(areq);
7480		/* Begin another fetch if requested and if no pending request */ view japanese
7481		if (fetch && !pendingAreq)
7482			fetch_more_data_begin(areq);
7483	}
7484	else
7485	{
7486		/* There's nothing more to do; just return a NULL pointer */ view japanese
7487		result = NULL;
7488		/* Mark the request as complete */
7489		ExecAsyncRequestDone(areq, result);
7490	}
7491}
7492
7493/*
7494 * Begin an asynchronous data fetch.
7495 *
7496 * Note: this function assumes there is no currently-in-progress asynchronous
7497 * data fetch.
7498 *
7499 * Note: fetch_more_data must be called to fetch the result.
7500 */ view japanese
7501static void
7502fetch_more_data_begin(AsyncRequest *areq)
7503{
7504	ForeignScanState *node = (ForeignScanState *) areq->requestee;
7505	PgFdwScanState *fsstate = (PgFdwScanState *) node->fdw_state;
7506	char		sql[64];
7507
7508	Assert(!fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq);
7509
7510	/* Create the cursor synchronously. */ view japanese
7511	if (!fsstate->cursor_exists)
7512		create_cursor(node);
7513
7514	/* We will send this query, but not wait for the response. */ view japanese
7515	snprintf(sql, sizeof(sql), "FETCH %d FROM c%u",
7516			 fsstate->fetch_size, fsstate->cursor_number);
7517
7518	if (!PQsendQuery(fsstate->conn, sql))
7519		pgfdw_report_error(ERROR, NULL, fsstate->conn, false, fsstate->query);
7520
7521	/* Remember that the request is in process */ view japanese
7522	fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq = areq;
7523}
7524
7525/*
7526 * Process a pending asynchronous request.
7527 */ view japanese
7528void
7529process_pending_request(AsyncRequest *areq)
7530{
7531	ForeignScanState *node = (ForeignScanState *) areq->requestee;
7532	PgFdwScanState *fsstate = (PgFdwScanState *) node->fdw_state;
7533
7534	/* The request would have been pending for a callback */
7535	Assert(areq->callback_pending);
7536
7537	/* The request should be currently in-process */ view japanese
7538	Assert(fsstate->conn_state->pendingAreq == areq);
7539
7540	fetch_more_data(node);
7541
7542	/*
7543	 * If we didn't get any tuples, must be end of data; complete the request
7544	 * now.  Otherwise, we postpone completing the request until we are called
7545	 * from postgresForeignAsyncConfigureWait()/postgresForeignAsyncNotify().
7546	 */ view japanese
7547	if (fsstate->next_tuple >= fsstate->num_tuples)
7548	{
7549		/* Unlike AsyncNotify, we unset callback_pending ourselves */
7550		areq->callback_pending = false;
7551		/* Mark the request as complete */ view japanese
7552		ExecAsyncRequestDone(areq, NULL);
7553		/* Unlike AsyncNotify, we call ExecAsyncResponse ourselves */
7554		ExecAsyncResponse(areq);
7555	}
7556}
7557
7558/*
7559 * Complete a pending asynchronous request.
7560 */ view japanese
7561static void
7562complete_pending_request(AsyncRequest *areq)
7563{
7564	/* The request would have been pending for a callback */ view japanese
7565	Assert(areq->callback_pending);
7566
7567	/* Unlike AsyncNotify, we unset callback_pending ourselves */ view japanese
7568	areq->callback_pending = false;
7569
7570	/* We begin a fetch afterwards if necessary; don't fetch */ view japanese
7571	produce_tuple_asynchronously(areq, false);
7572
7573	/* Unlike AsyncNotify, we call ExecAsyncResponse ourselves */ view japanese
7574	ExecAsyncResponse(areq);
7575
7576	/* Also, we do instrumentation ourselves, if required */ view japanese
7577	if (areq->requestee->instrument)
7578		InstrUpdateTupleCount(areq->requestee->instrument,
7579							  TupIsNull(areq->result) ? 0.0 : 1.0);
7580}
7581
7582/*
7583 * Create a tuple from the specified row of the PGresult.
7584 *
7585 * rel is the local representation of the foreign table, attinmeta is
7586 * conversion data for the rel's tupdesc, and retrieved_attrs is an
7587 * integer list of the table column numbers present in the PGresult.
7588 * fsstate is the ForeignScan plan node's execution state.
7589 * temp_context is a working context that can be reset after each tuple.
7590 *
7591 * Note: either rel or fsstate, but not both, can be NULL.  rel is NULL
7592 * if we're processing a remote join, while fsstate is NULL in a non-query
7593 * context such as ANALYZE, or if we're processing a non-scan query node.
7594 */ view japanese
7595static HeapTuple
7596make_tuple_from_result_row(PGresult *res,
7597						   int row,
7598						   Relation rel,
7599						   AttInMetadata *attinmeta,
7600						   List *retrieved_attrs,
7601						   ForeignScanState *fsstate,
7602						   MemoryContext temp_context)
7603{
7604	HeapTuple	tuple;
7605	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
7606	Datum	   *values;
7607	bool	   *nulls;
7608	ItemPointer ctid = NULL;
7609	ConversionLocation errpos;
7610	ErrorContextCallback errcallback;
7611	MemoryContext oldcontext;
7612	ListCell   *lc;
7613	int			j;
7614
7615	Assert(row < PQntuples(res));
7616
7617	/*
7618	 * Do the following work in a temp context that we reset after each tuple.
7619	 * This cleans up not only the data we have direct access to, but any
7620	 * cruft the I/O functions might leak.
7621	 */ view japanese
7622	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(temp_context);
7623
7624	/*
7625	 * Get the tuple descriptor for the row.  Use the rel's tupdesc if rel is
7626	 * provided, otherwise look to the scan node's ScanTupleSlot.
7627	 */ view japanese
7628	if (rel)
7629		tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
7630	else
7631	{
7632		Assert(fsstate);
7633		tupdesc = fsstate->ss.ss_ScanTupleSlot->tts_tupleDescriptor;
7634	}
7635
7636	values = (Datum *) palloc0(tupdesc->natts * sizeof(Datum));
7637	nulls = (bool *) palloc(tupdesc->natts * sizeof(bool));
7638	/* Initialize to nulls for any columns not present in result */ view japanese
7639	memset(nulls, true, tupdesc->natts * sizeof(bool));
7640
7641	/*
7642	 * Set up and install callback to report where conversion error occurs.
7643	 */ view japanese
7644	errpos.cur_attno = 0;
7645	errpos.rel = rel;
7646	errpos.fsstate = fsstate;
7647	errcallback.callback = conversion_error_callback;
7648	errcallback.arg = &errpos;
7649	errcallback.previous = error_context_stack;
7650	error_context_stack = &errcallback;
7651
7652	/*
7653	 * i indexes columns in the relation, j indexes columns in the PGresult.
7654	 */ view japanese
7655	j = 0;
7656	foreach(lc, retrieved_attrs)
7657	{
7658		int			i = lfirst_int(lc);
7659		char	   *valstr;
7660
7661		/* fetch next column's textual value */ view japanese
7662		if (PQgetisnull(res, row, j))
7663			valstr = NULL;
7664		else
7665			valstr = PQgetvalue(res, row, j);
7666
7667		/*
7668		 * convert value to internal representation
7669		 *
7670		 * Note: we ignore system columns other than ctid and oid in result
7671		 */ view japanese
7672		errpos.cur_attno = i;
7673		if (i > 0)
7674		{
7675			/* ordinary column */ view japanese
7676			Assert(i <= tupdesc->natts);
7677			nulls[i - 1] = (valstr == NULL);
7678			/* Apply the input function even to nulls, to support domains */ view japanese
7679			values[i - 1] = InputFunctionCall(&attinmeta->attinfuncs[i - 1],
7680											  valstr,
7681											  attinmeta->attioparams[i - 1],
7682											  attinmeta->atttypmods[i - 1]);
7683		}
7684		else if (i == SelfItemPointerAttributeNumber)
7685		{
7686			/* ctid */ view japanese
7687			if (valstr != NULL)
7688			{
7689				Datum		datum;
7690
7691				datum = DirectFunctionCall1(tidin, CStringGetDatum(valstr));
7692				ctid = (ItemPointer) DatumGetPointer(datum);
7693			}
7694		}
7695		errpos.cur_attno = 0;
7696
7697		j++;
7698	}
7699
7700	/* Uninstall error context callback. */ view japanese
7701	error_context_stack = errcallback.previous;
7702
7703	/*
7704	 * Check we got the expected number of columns.  Note: j == 0 and
7705	 * PQnfields == 1 is expected, since deparse emits a NULL if no columns.
7706	 */ view japanese
7707	if (j > 0 && j != PQnfields(res))
7708		elog(ERROR, "remote query result does not match the foreign table");
7709
7710	/*
7711	 * Build the result tuple in caller's memory context.
7712	 */ view japanese
7713	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
7714
7715	tuple = heap_form_tuple(tupdesc, values, nulls);
7716
7717	/*
7718	 * If we have a CTID to return, install it in both t_self and t_ctid.
7719	 * t_self is the normal place, but if the tuple is converted to a
7720	 * composite Datum, t_self will be lost; setting t_ctid allows CTID to be
7721	 * preserved during EvalPlanQual re-evaluations (see ROW_MARK_COPY code).
7722	 */ view japanese
7723	if (ctid)
7724		tuple->t_self = tuple->t_data->t_ctid = *ctid;
7725
7726	/*
7727	 * Stomp on the xmin, xmax, and cmin fields from the tuple created by
7728	 * heap_form_tuple.  heap_form_tuple actually creates the tuple with
7729	 * DatumTupleFields, not HeapTupleFields, but the executor expects
7730	 * HeapTupleFields and will happily extract system columns on that
7731	 * assumption.  If we don't do this then, for example, the tuple length
7732	 * ends up in the xmin field, which isn't what we want.
7733	 */ view japanese
7734	HeapTupleHeaderSetXmax(tuple->t_data, InvalidTransactionId);
7735	HeapTupleHeaderSetXmin(tuple->t_data, InvalidTransactionId);
7736	HeapTupleHeaderSetCmin(tuple->t_data, InvalidTransactionId);
7737
7738	/* Clean up */ view japanese
7739	MemoryContextReset(temp_context);
7740
7741	return tuple;
7742}
7743
7744/*
7745 * Callback function which is called when error occurs during column value
7746 * conversion.  Print names of column and relation.
7747 *
7748 * Note that this function mustn't do any catalog lookups, since we are in
7749 * an already-failed transaction.  Fortunately, we can get the needed info
7750 * from the relation or the query's rangetable instead.
7751 */ view japanese
7752static void
7753conversion_error_callback(void *arg)
7754{
7755	ConversionLocation *errpos = (ConversionLocation *) arg;
7756	Relation	rel = errpos->rel;
7757	ForeignScanState *fsstate = errpos->fsstate;
7758	const char *attname = NULL;
7759	const char *relname = NULL;
7760	bool		is_wholerow = false;
7761
7762	/*
7763	 * If we're in a scan node, always use aliases from the rangetable, for
7764	 * consistency between the simple-relation and remote-join cases.  Look at
7765	 * the relation's tupdesc only if we're not in a scan node.
7766	 */ view japanese
7767	if (fsstate)
7768	{
7769		/* ForeignScan case */ view japanese
7770		ForeignScan *fsplan = castNode(ForeignScan, fsstate->ss.ps.plan);
7771		int			varno = 0;
7772		AttrNumber	colno = 0;
7773
7774		if (fsplan->scan.scanrelid > 0)
7775		{
7776			/* error occurred in a scan against a foreign table */ view japanese
7777			varno = fsplan->scan.scanrelid;
7778			colno = errpos->cur_attno;
7779		}
7780		else
7781		{
7782			/* error occurred in a scan against a foreign join */ view japanese
7783			TargetEntry *tle;
7784
7785			tle = list_nth_node(TargetEntry, fsplan->fdw_scan_tlist,
7786								errpos->cur_attno - 1);
7787
7788			/*
7789			 * Target list can have Vars and expressions.  For Vars, we can
7790			 * get some information, however for expressions we can't.  Thus
7791			 * for expressions, just show generic context message.
7792			 */ view japanese
7793			if (IsA(tle->expr, Var))
7794			{
7795				Var		   *var = (Var *) tle->expr;
7796
7797				varno = var->varno;
7798				colno = var->varattno;
7799			}
7800		}
7801
7802		if (varno > 0)
7803		{
7804			EState	   *estate = fsstate->ss.ps.state;
7805			RangeTblEntry *rte = exec_rt_fetch(varno, estate);
7806
7807			relname = rte->eref->aliasname;
7808
7809			if (colno == 0)
7810				is_wholerow = true;
7811			else if (colno > 0 && colno <= list_length(rte->eref->colnames))
7812				attname = strVal(list_nth(rte->eref->colnames, colno - 1));
7813			else if (colno == SelfItemPointerAttributeNumber)
7814				attname = "ctid";
7815		}
7816	}
7817	else if (rel)
7818	{
7819		/* Non-ForeignScan case (we should always have a rel here) */ view japanese
7820		TupleDesc	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
7821
7822		relname = RelationGetRelationName(rel);
7823		if (errpos->cur_attno > 0 && errpos->cur_attno <= tupdesc->natts)
7824		{
7825			Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc,
7826												   errpos->cur_attno - 1);
7827
7828			attname = NameStr(attr->attname);
7829		}
7830		else if (errpos->cur_attno == SelfItemPointerAttributeNumber)
7831			attname = "ctid";
7832	}
7833
7834	if (relname && is_wholerow)
7835		errcontext("whole-row reference to foreign table \"%s\"", relname);
7836	else if (relname && attname)
7837		errcontext("column \"%s\" of foreign table \"%s\"", attname, relname);
7838	else
7839		errcontext("processing expression at position %d in select list",
7840				   errpos->cur_attno);
7841}
7842
7843/*
7844 * Given an EquivalenceClass and a foreign relation, find an EC member
7845 * that can be used to sort the relation remotely according to a pathkey
7846 * using this EC.
7847 *
7848 * If there is more than one suitable candidate, return an arbitrary
7849 * one of them.  If there is none, return NULL.
7850 *
7851 * This checks that the EC member expression uses only Vars from the given
7852 * rel and is shippable.  Caller must separately verify that the pathkey's
7853 * ordering operator is shippable.
7854 */ view japanese
7855EquivalenceMember *
7856find_em_for_rel(PlannerInfo *root, EquivalenceClass *ec, RelOptInfo *rel)
7857{
7858	PgFdwRelationInfo *fpinfo = (PgFdwRelationInfo *) rel->fdw_private;
7859	EquivalenceMemberIterator it;
7860	EquivalenceMember *em;
7861
7862	setup_eclass_member_iterator(&it, ec, rel->relids);
7863	while ((em = eclass_member_iterator_next(&it)) != NULL)
7864	{
7865		/*
7866		 * Note we require !bms_is_empty, else we'd accept constant
7867		 * expressions which are not suitable for the purpose.
7868		 */ view japanese
7869		if (bms_is_subset(em->em_relids, rel->relids) &&
7870			!bms_is_empty(em->em_relids) &&
7871			bms_is_empty(bms_intersect(em->em_relids, fpinfo->hidden_subquery_rels)) &&
7872			is_foreign_expr(root, rel, em->em_expr))
7873			return em;
7874	}
7875
7876	return NULL;
7877}
7878
7879/*
7880 * Find an EquivalenceClass member that is to be computed as a sort column
7881 * in the given rel's reltarget, and is shippable.
7882 *
7883 * If there is more than one suitable candidate, return an arbitrary
7884 * one of them.  If there is none, return NULL.
7885 *
7886 * This checks that the EC member expression uses only Vars from the given
7887 * rel and is shippable.  Caller must separately verify that the pathkey's
7888 * ordering operator is shippable.
7889 */ view japanese
7890EquivalenceMember *
7891find_em_for_rel_target(PlannerInfo *root, EquivalenceClass *ec,
7892					   RelOptInfo *rel)
7893{
7894	PathTarget *target = rel->reltarget;
7895	ListCell   *lc1;
7896	int			i;
7897
7898	i = 0;
7899	foreach(lc1, target->exprs)
7900	{
7901		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc1);
7902		Index		sgref = get_pathtarget_sortgroupref(target, i);
7903		ListCell   *lc2;
7904
7905		/* Ignore non-sort expressions */ view japanese
7906		if (sgref == 0 ||
7907			get_sortgroupref_clause_noerr(sgref,
7908										  root->parse->sortClause) == NULL)
7909		{
7910			i++;
7911			continue;
7912		}
7913
7914		/* We ignore binary-compatible relabeling on both ends */ view japanese
7915		while (expr && IsA(expr, RelabelType))
7916			expr = ((RelabelType *) expr)->arg;
7917
7918		/*
7919		 * Locate an EquivalenceClass member matching this expr, if any.
7920		 * Ignore child members.
7921		 */ view japanese
7922		foreach(lc2, ec->ec_members)
7923		{
7924			EquivalenceMember *em = (EquivalenceMember *) lfirst(lc2);
7925			Expr	   *em_expr;
7926
7927			/* Don't match constants */ view japanese
7928			if (em->em_is_const)
7929				continue;
7930
7931			/* Child members should not exist in ec_members */ view japanese
7932			Assert(!em->em_is_child);
7933
7934			/* Match if same expression (after stripping relabel) */ view japanese
7935			em_expr = em->em_expr;
7936			while (em_expr && IsA(em_expr, RelabelType))
7937				em_expr = ((RelabelType *) em_expr)->arg;
7938
7939			if (!equal(em_expr, expr))
7940				continue;
7941
7942			/* Check that expression (including relabels!) is shippable */ view japanese
7943			if (is_foreign_expr(root, rel, em->em_expr))
7944				return em;
7945		}
7946
7947		i++;
7948	}
7949
7950	return NULL;
7951}
7952
7953/*
7954 * Determine batch size for a given foreign table. The option specified for
7955 * a table has precedence.
7956 */ view japanese
7957static int
7958get_batch_size_option(Relation rel)
7959{
7960	Oid			foreigntableid = RelationGetRelid(rel);
7961	ForeignTable *table;
7962	ForeignServer *server;
7963	List	   *options;
7964	ListCell   *lc;
7965
7966	/* we use 1 by default, which means "no batching" */ view japanese
7967	int			batch_size = 1;
7968
7969	/*
7970	 * Load options for table and server. We append server options after table
7971	 * options, because table options take precedence.
7972	 */ view japanese
7973	table = GetForeignTable(foreigntableid);
7974	server = GetForeignServer(table->serverid);
7975
7976	options = NIL;
7977	options = list_concat(options, table->options);
7978	options = list_concat(options, server->options);
7979
7980	/* See if either table or server specifies batch_size. */ view japanese
7981	foreach(lc, options)
7982	{
7983		DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
7984
7985		if (strcmp(def->defname, "batch_size") == 0)
7986		{
7987			(void) parse_int(defGetString(def), &batch_size, 0, NULL);
7988			break;
7989		}
7990	}
7991
7992	return batch_size;
7993}

Japanese translation

/* 外部クエリを開始するためのデフォルトのCPUコスト。 */

Original comment

/* Default CPU cost to start up a foreign query. */

/*
 * fdw_private リストに格納される FDW プライベート情報のインデックス。
 *
 * これらの項目は enum FdwScanPrivateIndex でインデックス付けされているため、
 * list_nth() を使用して項目を取得できます。例えば、SELECT 文を取得するには以下のようにします。
 *		sql = strVal(list_nth(fdw_private, FdwScanPrivateSelectSql));
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Indexes of FDW-private information stored in fdw_private lists.
 *
 * These items are indexed with the enum FdwScanPrivateIndex, so an item
 * can be fetched with list_nth().  For example, to get the SELECT statement:
 *		sql = strVal(list_nth(fdw_private, FdwScanPrivateSelectSql));
 */

contrib/postgres_fdw/postgres_fdw.c:89-100

/*
 * 同様に、この列挙型は postgres_fdw 外部テーブルを参照する ModifyTable ノードの
 * fdw_private リストに保持される内容を記述する。以下を格納する:
 *
 * 1) リモートサーバーに送信される INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE 文のテキスト
 * 2) INSERT/UPDATE の対象属性番号の整数リスト
 *    (DELETE の場合は NIL)
 * 3) INSERT における VALUES 句の末尾までの長さ
 *    (DELETE/UPDATE の場合は -1)
 * 4) リモートクエリが RETURNING 句を持つかどうかを示すブールフラグ
 * 5) RETURNING によって取得される属性番号の整数リスト (存在する場合)
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Similarly, this enum describes what's kept in the fdw_private list for
 * a ModifyTable node referencing a postgres_fdw foreign table.  We store:
 *
 * 1) INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE statement text to be sent to the remote server
 * 2) Integer list of target attribute numbers for INSERT/UPDATE
 *	  (NIL for a DELETE)
 * 3) Length till the end of VALUES clause for INSERT
 *	  (-1 for a DELETE/UPDATE)
 * 4) Boolean flag showing if the remote query has a RETURNING clause
 * 5) Integer list of attribute numbers retrieved by RETURNING, if any
 */

Japanese translation

 */

contrib/postgres_fdw/postgres_fdw.c:250

/* ワーキングメモリのコンテキスト */
